title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > Background Introduction
content:
This overall project from SM retail group and Dmall technology aims to achieve digital transformation of SAP Systems. Dmall system solution works for strategic imperatives of competitive technology, enhanced store efficiency and quick reflection on data display.

Among all the Phase1A streams that Dmall supported, this blueprint is solely prepared for the Data Dashboard working stream to state Dmall solution and cover requirements from As-Is and Wishlist Analysis.

In the second section of this document, rough information of Data Dashboard will be provided, at the same time, the important access limitation function will be included which can support the future SM standard reporting work.

In the third section of this document, based on the statistical analysis of As-is & Wishlist scenarios collected from various departments, corresponding reporting solution will be provided. This will facilitate a comprehensive review by the business department of SM retail group, culminating in the sign-off of the blueprint.

Department | Total Report Collection | As-Is | Wishlist | Representer

Marketing | 16 | Lana
Finance & Acctg | 19 | Melody
Picking | 7 | Daniel & Carl
FSC | 4 | Sean

Logistics | 1
Total | 220 | 169 | 51

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > Standard Reports Overview
content:
Standard Data Dashboard provides management analysis report and operational detail reports. Those standard reports are designed with industry best practice to support day-to-day management. It helps the management team to gain visibility of the whole business from different granularities. It enables the operation team to identify the cause of the problem and make accurate decisions.

Standard reports are classified into following domains:

Commercial (for HQ use)

Supply Chain

Inventory Management

Vendor Reports

Store Operation (for single store use)

POS Detail Reports

Big Screen & App Support

Picking Performance

eCommerce Reports (Not covered in this document)

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > New Functionalities Requirements
content: Diverging from the conventional standard reports, our examination of SM Company's retail operations has emphasized the requirement for customized services. By tailoring reporting solutions to align seamlessly with the distinctive demands of the retail sector, our objective is to streamline processes, ultimately fostering operational efficiency. This not only contributes to a more time-effective workflow but also results in tangible resource savings for SM Company.

content:
New request/function | SM Definition | Solution

title: 2. Data Dashboard Introduction > 2.1 Final Solution Format
content:
Dmall Data Dashboard Stream is operated with SM guidance and Dmall technology. It aims to serve all the management and analytical data needs with product formats not limited to performance report, operation detail reports, big screen, app reports and other customized requests.

In comparison to operational reports, Performance reporting's audience is targeted to be management level and data analysts.

The application data service focuses on master data hierarchies including store hierarchy, merchandise hierarchy and vendor hierarchy level, rather than transaction level entries which are accessible in corresponding modules.

The data sources would be merging data from multiple domains for cross-sectional analysis. Trend analysis of historical data is supported with historical data migration project accomplished.

Functionality wise, Performance reports have rich presentation of descriptive statistics with comprehensively structured layout, auto aggregation and visualizations.

title: 2. Data Dashboard Introduction > 2.2 User Accessibility Matrix
content:
All reports that Dmall provides contain the whole dimensions and suitable measures according to different scenarios analysis (exclude some customized reports which the fixed format required). Compared with SM existing many reports, Dmall offers a clearer and simple solution by generating similar purpose within one chart to display on the system.

Consider the strict control over data access and acknowledging the high sensitivity of data permission requirements. Dmall designed the user access matrix function to match the SM policy. By utilizing this feature, Dmall’s reports enable administrators to configure various roles and, through role-based data permissions, systematically control the streamlined version of reports visible to different accounts in a batch manner.

Based on our current understanding of business, our data control extends support to individual stores, categories and movement types. Additionally, Dmall can implement strict restrictions on viewing for each column as needed.

title: 3. SM Data Dashboard Solution > 3.1 Reports Design > 3.1.1 Common functionalities
content: A standard report consists of time filter, dimension/attribute filter, reset button, LFL checkbox, column editor, download button, query button and data table section. The functions above are subject to be tailored for each standard report.

title: 3. SM Data Dashboard Solution > 3.1 Reports Design > 3.1.1 Common functionalities > 3.1.1.1 Time Filter
content:
Data Dashboard provides two types of time filters.

Absolute time range

In the Time Calendar, we can select range by Day / Week / Month / Year.

Relative time range (Fixed cannot define)

For corresponding TP time selection, the current day would be set as default, the same days last year is set as default in SPLY(same period last year).

title: 3. SM Data Dashboard Solution > 3.1 Reports Design > 3.1.1 Common functionalities > 3.1.1.2 Dimension/Attribute Filter
content:
For each report, there are multiple dimensions/attributes depending on the design of report. It may include master data dimensions as merchandise hierarchy, site hierarchy, vendor hierarchy and attributes such as article status, brand, sales type etc.

All the dimensions/attributes are enabled for data filtering in Data Dashboard standard report.  The data shown in corresponding table would be set by the dimension/attribute filter.

The filter is provided in two ways.

Dropdown list with search box and multiple selection enabled.

Text search box if option quantity is huge (now support max 50 options)

title: 3. SM Data Dashboard Solution > 3.1 Reports Design > 3.1.1 Common functionalities > 3.1.1.4 LFL checkbox
content:
LFL is the short form of like-for-like. When a store has transactions in all the last full 15 months, the store's status is considered as 'LFL' from the 1st of the next month.

LFL checkbox is basically a filter option for LFL stores. While the box is unchecked, there is no filtering from this checkbox. While the box is checked, it is equivalent to filter LFL stores' data only.

title: 3. SM Data Dashboard Solution > 3.1 Reports Design > 3.1.1 Common functionalities > 3.1.1.5 Column Editor / Summary Method
content:
Column editor button is on the top right corner of the data table.  It enables users to show/hide/change orders of some fields in the data table section.

Light blue shaded headers are dimension/attributes and aggregation supportable. The hidden dimension will be aggregated.

Headers with no shading are measures/dimension/attributes without aggregation. The hidden dimension will not be aggregated.

The column freeze button is supported.

title: 3. SM Data Dashboard Solution > 3.1 Reports Design > 3.1.1 Common functionalities > 3.1.1.8 Drill Down Function
content: In certain reports, highlighted data in blue offers a special interactive feature. Upon clicking, users can choose to drill down and analyze the data from different perspectives, such as transitioning from stores to products or from products to trend analysis. The drill-down feature may involve a pop-up window or a direct transition to another report, carrying the same filtering conditions for seamless analysis.

title: 3. SM Data Dashboard Solution > 3.1 Reports Design > 3.1.1 Common functionalities > 3.1.1.9 Data Table Section
content:
Data Dashboard standard report layout is well tuned in formats. The adjustment includes but not limited to:

Number format = #，## 0.00

Percentage format = 0.00%

Number/percentage aligns to right

Text aligns to left

Colored fonts for vs. and (ppt) measures

Other functions supported:

Measure sorting click (none, ascending, descending) applies for continuous value.

The data table section has a left/right scroll bar for data viewing.

Next page buttons are available in the bottom left area.

title: 3. SM Data Dashboard Solution > 3.4 Addtional Function – Email Push & Print
content:
Dmall's advanced features include a robust system for email distribution and printing, offering administrators a streamlined approach to disseminating reports. Through this functionality, managers can effortlessly push reports to predefined email groups in a consistent format.

What sets this system apart is its intelligent backend mechanism. When reports are sent out, the system dynamically adjusts the content based on the data permissions of each recipient. This ensures that individuals only receive information that aligns with their specific access levels, maintaining data integrity, confidentiality, and relevance.

Furthermore, this multifaceted functionality extends to both email distribution and physical printouts, providing flexibility to accommodate diverse user preferences. The seamless integration of these features not only enhances the efficiency of report sharing within the organization but also upholds a high standard of data security and accessibility tailored to individual needs.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope
content:
1.1 Background Introduction

Inventory management, a critical element of store operations, is essentially the practice of ensuring store businesses have the right articles, in the right quantity, at the right time. This includes knowing when the store will receive and return articles from/to DC and/or suppliers, how much stock the store has on hand, how to decide when and what to order, and accurately track articles, all the while bearing store operation process.

Good inventory management will not only support the store, but also help headquarters successfully manage and monitor the inventory and improve store performance.

For store activities on a daily basis, staff need to perform goods receiving, return, gap check and perhaps stock adjustments if variance on articles available SOH is found.

More importantly, all store activities are recorded and can be generated in reports for supervisors or headquarters to review. Well-organized reports show the store's performance, which can help headquarters with further group decisions.

1.2 Business Process Overview

Business scenario overview

In DMALL OS inventory management module, a whole set of features can support goods receiving, returns and stock adjustment and monitoring.

Receiving | Receiving from vendor | Store receive goods from vendor | Perform itemized receiving | Page 4 | Perform itemized receiving and support to input expiry date for monitoring | Simplify Business Flow,Add New Feature | Expiry date input during the receiving process

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
In phase 1, DMALL system only manages the inventory and cost is managed by SAP system. Inventory module and corresponding inventory report (under dashboard module) will NOT show any relative cost information.

After phase 1 go live, no stock adjustment be done in SAP. All inventory adjustment should be done via DMALL system。

3.1 Inventory design

1) Design Background

DMALL system will pre-define 8 inventory locations for stores. And each store operation function/movement action will operate in a specific inventory location.

Each inventory location has its own inventory data. It will not be mixed up together so that we can clearly identify the current inventory situation and make proper decisions.

Each inventory location will be mapped with SAP system location.

2) Key design

Inventory location definition

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
Inventory location code | Inventory location name | Definition
SLOC 0001 | Selling Area | Selling area inventory indicates the sales floor articles quantity. when POS selling, inventory should be deducted from the selling area.
SLOC 0002 | Offsite Warehouse | This location indicates the storage articles quantity in the outside warehouse
SLOC 0003 | Bad Order Location | Bad order location is only used for return scenario. these inventory should not be sellable or used for any business.store should separate into an individual area for display these articles.
SLOC 0007 | STDR from DC&Store | STDR location is only used for delivery discrepancy report scenario. these stocks should not be sellable or used for any business. Stores should separate an individual area for display these articles.
SLOC 0024 | Holding location of articles | Subject for Insurance Claim, evidence for Court Hearings
SLOC 0025 | Dark Inventory | This location is used for storing articles for online order picking. And the location is on the sales floor.
SLOC 0030 | Advance Order | Storage location for reserved items for advance orders
SLOC 0031 | Backroom | Backroom inventory indicates the storage articles quantity. when articles are replenished into sales floor shelves, users should perform the stock transfer function to adjust the inventory.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
Inventory locations can be set up per store.

5 stores will have 6 inventory locations, which have SLOC 0025 Dark Inventory for online business. (it needs to confirm certain stores in the UAT phase)

Other stores will have 5 inventory locations. (except the SLOC 0025 Dark Inventory)

Terminology Description:

Noun | Description
Book Quantity | The inventory quantity on store records.
Reservation Quantity | The inventory quantity that has been reserved by orders, but has not yet been deducted from the store's accounting books，also known as locked quantity.
Available Quantity | Book Quantity take away Reservation Quantity

Quantity calculation dimension:  Site+ Inventory location + Article.

Logic of Quantity Calculation:

Beginning Quantity (The Ending Quantity of the previous period） | Book Quantity
Beginning Quantity (The Ending Quantity of the previous period） | Reservation Quantity
Beginning Quantity (The Ending Quantity of the previous period） | Available Quantity
Mobile Quantity | Book Quantity | The quantity of inbound and outbound documents
Mobile Quantity | Reservation Quantity
Mobile Quantity | Available Quantity | Book Quantity take away Reservation Quantity
Ending Quantity （Begining+Mobile） | Book Quantity
Ending Quantity （Begining+Mobile） | Reservation Quantity
Ending Quantity （Begining+Mobile） | Available Quantity | Book Quantity take away Reservation Quantity

Receiving

3.2 Good receiving from vendor (DSA)

1.AS-IS Overview

In SM current practice, RDU users will receive the DN, download and print out the PO document from SAP and perform itemized receiving. RDU users will perform the first check and note down the receiving quantity on the PO document. Counter checker will perform the second check and input the receiving quantity into FRS system. If there is differ between the first check and second check, manager will be involved to do the double check.

2.TO-BE Overview

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
Goods Receiving from Vendor is the physical inbound movement of goods from an external vendor, either without PO or with PO, which results in an increase of stock on hand(SOH). In this section, it focuses on receiving with PO, including outright goods receiving, consignor with inventory goods receiving and receiving on non trade PO's for goods, supplies, collaterals, signages and services. Consignor without inventory good receiving please refer to stock adjustment function to increase the SOH.

One DSA PO, can be multiple received in the DMALL system. For articles from a vendor to stores, the receiving store performs a detailed physical count, checks and accepts the article based on PO for goods receiving, which article could be rejected if the article is not in the receiving PO or in poor conditions.

Over-receiving is NOT allowed based on business practice and system design. DMALL System only allows receiving goods less than or equal to PO quantity.

Free goods, which are performed GR as store stock but with zero value, will be shown on the PO as an individual article line. The receiving process of free goods is the same as normal articles.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
3.TO-BE Flow

4.TO-BE Description

Step | Operation | System | Role | Input | Output | Details | Screenshot PC | Screenshot APP
1 | Download PO data from SAP | DMALL | / | PO data
2 | Receive and save the PO data in DMALL system | DMALL | /
3 | Secure truck and stocks, record time-in | / | Security dept. | / | Security logbook
4 | Delivery with DS and TS | / | Trucker | DS, TS | /

6 | Does it exist incomplete reference order | / | PO data | / | DMALL will validate it is first delivery or second delivery
7 | Complete the 2nd check (2nd scan count) | DMALL | RDU | PO number | Alert notice | If there is an incomplete reference order, that means the reference order is under 2nd check. Please proceed with step 10 In this scenario, DMALL will not allow store to receive the 2nd delivery. They need to finish the previous delivery first.
8 | Generate reference order | DMALL | RDU | PO number | Reference order | If there is not any incomplete reference order, DMALL will automatically create a new reference order for store to receive.
9 | 1st Scan: Count physical goods and input actual quantity and other receiving info. | DMALL | RDU | DS, TS | Receiving data | Count physical goods and input below information: actual receive quantity, Time In/Out, Truck Type, Plate Number, Driver Name, Helper Name, Invoice No., DR No., Truck No., Support inputting the expiry date for monitoring. Support inputting multiple expiry date data per article. (optional) Actual receive quantity default empty
10 | 2nd Scan: Count physical goods and input actual quantity | DMALL | Counter checker | DS, TS | Receiving data | Counter checker need to perform the second scan checking and input the receiving quantity

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
11 | Qty of 1st = Qty of 2nd? | DMALL | / | Qty of 1st, Qty of 2nd | Receiving data | DMALL system will validate Qty of 1st is equal to Qty of 2nd or not. If not, RDU needs to double check. If yes, reference order will be completed when counter checker submitted.
12 | Double check, verify and amend the Qty of 2nd | DMALL | RDU | / | The final confirmed receive quantity | RDU double check and amend the receiving quantity
13 | Submit receiving | DMALL | RDU | Receiving info. | Completed reference order | Save it and order will be received. Inventory will be added to the backroom site.
14 | Post and update invty to SAP(101) | SAP | / | Reference order | / | DMALL system will proceed with the final confirmed receive quantity and post it to SAP. Movement type:101
15 | Generate GR | SAP | / | Reference order | Posted GR | SAP should generate the real GR
16 | Receive the received DS from RDU | / | Trucker | Received DS | /
17 | Record the trucker time out | / | Security dept. | / | Security logbook
18 | Replenishment to shelves | DMALL | Selling | Received goods | Replenish to selling area | Perform store transfer function: transfer stock from backroom to selling area

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
1）End to End

In phase 1, PO creation happens in SAP system. DMALL will download previous day’s PO data at 5AM every day from SAP and save it in database. The reference order in DMALL is just a reference for SAP to generate the real good receipt. The reference order will be created when stocks arrive at the store.

DMALL only supports receiving goods existed in the Purchase order. Over-receiving and extra articles that do not exist in PO are NOT allowed in DMALL design. Stores need to handle the above situations offline.

For free gifts, it will be reflected in the PO. The store can receive it like normal articles. For free gifts which are not in PO, please refer to current business practice(SOP) to handle them. DMALL system only support receiving articles existing in the PO(in phase 1).

Purchase price is strictly controlled by merchandising department. Stores are NOT allowed to amend purchase price when receiving and DMALL also do NOT support it.

In phase 1, DMALL system only manages the inventory, and cost is managed by SAP system. DMALL system will NOT show any relative cost information.

For DSA order, if it is consignor with inventory PO, allow store team to receive it within 14 days after delivery date(PO ETA). If others POs, from DMALL system perspective, users can only receive before or on the expected arrivals date(ETA date). After the ETA date, stores are NOT allowed to receive. Actual receiving is only allowed by default on ETA, which will be controlled by SOP

2)  Function design

DMALL support receiving via PC side and APP side(Application can be installed on mobile phone and PDA). The screen layout will be impacted by different device resolution. DMALL will present the best layout with the agreed terminal device as the standard.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
When receiving the goods via system, inventory will be added into SLOC 0031 Backroom and store users can move the goods to each sector/each bay/each shelf in the backroom. If they want to move the stock from backroom to selling area, users can perform stock transfer function to adjust the inventory between different inventory locations.

For outright&consignor with inventory: Store will not receive more than PO order quantity. Actual receive quantity must be equal to or less than PO order quantity.

For outright items, DMALL will post to SAP with movement code 101/0001 selling area, for consignor with inventory, DMALL will post to SAP with movement code 101/vendor location. For supplies, Dmall will post to SAP with movement code 101/0001 selling area too.

For one DSA PO with multiple delivery within same day, DMALL system could support creating the second reference order. Each reference order will be posted to SAP when completion. Each reference order can be printed out the reference order note. One DSA PO can have multiple reference orders. The new good reference order can be created only after the previous reference order has been received.

PO quantity: 30 | Receive quantity | Remaining quantity
First receiving—reference order 1 (delivery qty:10) | 10 | 20 | Post to SAP with 10 quantity-- GR1
Second receiving-- reference order 2 (delivery qty:20) | 20 | 0 | Post to SAP with 20 quantity-- GR2

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
For multiple POs receiving, Dmall will support users scan or input multiple PO number and group them into a draft reference order. RDU users and counter checker should perform scan 1 and scan 2 receiving.

If delivery quantity is less than PO quantity, the article on the first series of PO must be fully received to be able to receive the same article on the second PO and so on and so forth.

If an article has regular item and free item, users should manually input the receive quantity of regular and free.

Not allow to over-receive

Not allow to receive article does not exist on the PO

When post to SAP, Dmall will generate corresponding multiple GRs.

3) Key function layout sample

Note: this is just for explaining the function and logic. It might be changed when developing.

Receiving Note: all reference order orders can be checked and supported to filter by below criteria.

Status: Pending to receive, Received

Operation type:  APP receiving, PC receiving

Document type: DSP Ref Order, STO Ref Order and IBT Ref Order

DSP is equal to DSA in DMALL system terminology. IBT(Inter branch transfer) is store to store transfer order

Receiving Note: input and complete the receiving.

Receive one line by one line.

When performing 1st scan, only the receive quantity and Expiry date can be edited.

Receive quantity should be less than or equal to plan quantity

Expiry date can be input multiple dates. Input value should be later than today

When performing 2nd scan, only the receive quantity can be edited.

Receive quantity should be less than or equal to plan quantity

Receiving Note(APP):

Reference order format:

Signature will be prefilled by system depending on account name

5.Exceptional Scenario

If receiving quantity more than plan quantity, system will block the operation. Users need to adjust the receiving quantity less than or equal to plan quantity.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
If physical goods does not exist on the receiving reference order. It is not allow to receive. Please create a purchase order for receiving.

If system prompt that there still have an incomplete reference order when submit the data, please receive the previous one and then perform the next receiving.

If cannot find the PO data. Please check the purchase order whether exists on the SAP system. If yes, please input the purchase order number in DMALL and re-pull the data. (Aligned with Sir Romil, re-pull data function is not in the scope of phase 1. if happens, users can receive it by paperwork first and contact Dmall IT team for help) If no, it means the goods are not ordered by SM.

To prevent network unstable, Dmall will download PO data and store in local database everyday morning (PDA).

1) Receiving information can be input if network does not work and information will be saved in local database. Receiving information will be automatically uploaded to cloud when connecting to the network.

2) If users need to print out the GR note immediately, they can download into PDA and copy it to PC device for printing.

3) Offline mode receiving only support 1 step receiving. Please perform scan 1 and scan 2 receiving by paperwork and keyin the final receiving quantity via PDA.

3.3 Good receiving from DC/Store

1.AS-IS Overview

In SM current practice, RDU users will receive the DN, download and print out the STO document from SAP and perform itemized receiving. RDU users only need to perform one time receiving and input the receiving quantity into FRS system. If there is any differ between STO plan quantity and actual receiving quantity, users can check in STDR report and raise the STDR process separately.

2.TO-BE Overview

Goods Receiving from DC is the physical inbound movement of goods from DC or stores to the store, which results in an increase of stock on hand.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
For conventional articles received from DC(s) or stores, goods receiving will be based on DC/store Outbound Delivery (OBD) to perform itemized receiving. Each delivery carries a DN. Multiple OBDs for one order for one store are possible, where articles will be loaded in multiple deliveries after DC pick and pack.

3.TO-BE Flow

4.TO-BE Description

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
1）End to End

In phase 1, DMALL will download previous day’s OBD data at 5AM every day from SAP and save it in database. Each reference order will be posted to SAP when completing the receiving. One STO can be delivered multiple times with multiple OBD(s). So one STO can be linked to multiple GR in SAP.

DMALL only supports receiving goods exist in Purchase order. Over-receiving and extra articles that do not exist in PO are NOT allowed in DMALL design. Stores need to handle the above situations offline.

No free gifts delivered from DC or stores. DMALL can NOT support it also.

Purchase price is strictly controlled by merchandising department. Stores are NOT allowed to amend purchase price when receiving and DMALL also do NOT support it.

In phase 1, DMALL system only manages the inventory and cost is managed by SAP system. DMALL system will NOT show any relative cost information.

For STO orders from DC or stores, no receiving timing limit, users can receive them at any time.

2)  Function design

DMALL support receiving via PC side and APP side(Application can be installed on mobile phone and PDA). The screen layout will be impacted by different device resolution. DMALL will present the best layout with the agreed terminal device as the standard.

When receiving the goods via system, inventory will be added into SLOC 0031 Backroom and store users can move the goods to each sector/each bay/each shelf in the backroom. If they want to move the stock from backroom to selling area, users can perform stock transfer function to adjust the inventory between different inventory locations.

For outright&consignor with inventory: Store will not receive more than PO order quantity.Actual receive quantity must be equal to or less than PO order quantity.

Expiry date inputting for STO order, users should use the expiry date management function to record the expiry date.

System will auto trigger STDR process based on the different receiving situation.

3.4 Store transfer discrepancy report from DC/Store

1.AS-IS Overview

If there is any differ between STO plan quantity and actual receiving quantity, users can check in STDR report and raise the STDR process separately.

2.TO-BE Overview

Goods Receiving from DC/store is the physical inbound movement of goods from DC or stores to the store, which results in an increase of stock on hand. If the receiving quantity is not equal to PO quantity, it should have corresponding process to handle it and ensure the inventory is correct between the issuance site and receiving site.

3.TO-BE Flow

4.TO-BE Description

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
1）End to End

STDR process only handles store range articles. If the article is not in the range of stores, users should manually handle it offline.

2)  Function design

DMALL support receiving via PC side and APP side(Application can be installed on mobile phone and PDA). The screen layout will be impacted by different device resolution. DMALL will present the best layout with the agreed terminal device as the standard.

When receiving the goods via system, normal goods inventory will be added into SLOC 0031 Backroom and discrepancy goods inventory will be added into SLOC 0007 STDR from DC&Store

When a STDR application is created, approver will receive an approval notification email and they need to login to the system via PDA or PC to check the details and approve it. When DC approves or rejects the application, store users will receive the approval message via PDA or PC, they can go to task center to check the details approval. Approval page of task center will show time stamp of each operation. Please refer to the key function page。

System will default no discrepancy when receiving. If there is any discrepancy, store users need to select the discrepancy type by article line level.

Discrepancy type for selection: Underserved, Overserved, Expiry, Damaged, Wrong served.

For underserved and overserved, STDR should be approved after issuance site investigation

For expiry, store users need to receive all goods, including expiry goods. And then they need to manually perform the return process.

For damaged, store users need to receive all goods, including damaged goods. System will auto trigger GI937 to charge 3rd party trucker vendor.

For Wrong served,

For the 'correct article', if it is 'underserved', then follow the underserved process.

For wrong article:

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
If store wants to absorb it, store users should manually adjust the inventory of store side with TP311(from store0007 to store0031) via stock adjustment function. And DC should manually adjust the inventory of DC side via TP301(from DC0007 to store0007).

If store does not want to absorb it, they need to return to DC offline without using the system.

For Underserved,

DC to store STDR and Store to store STDR workflow are consistent except for the different operation of issuance site.

If issuance site is DC, they will perform transfer order in SAP system after approving the STDR.

If issuance site is store, they will perform TP311 post to SAP via DMALL system (inventory from issue site0007 to issue site0031)

For Overserved,

Based on the result of STDR approval, system will trigger corresponding process to adjust DC site inventory. On the store side, no matter if the approval is approved or rejected, the overserved goods need to be received (if store wants to absorb, then they need to receive. if store does not want to absorb it, then they need to receive it and perform an extra return process).

DC to store STDR and Store to store STDR workflow are consistent except for the different operation of issuance site.

If issuance site is DC, they will perform transfer order in SAP system after approving the STDR.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
If issuance site is store, they will perform TP311 post to SAP via DMALL system (inventory from issue site0031 to issue site0007)

3) Inventory change flow (STDR from DC to store)

Underserved:

Process | PO quantity | DC0001 （refer to SAP） | DC0007 （refer to SAP） | Store 0031 （refer to SAP and DMALL） | Store 0007 （refer to SAP and DMALL） | Procedure
Create PO | 10
DC outbound | -10
Store receiving (auto trigger STDR process) | +9 | +1 | DMALL side: 9 quantities will be added into store 0031, and 1 quantity will be added into store 0007
Store receiving (auto trigger STDR process) | +9 | +1 | SAP side: Twice GR101 Post to SAP. 9 quantities for store0031 and 1 quantity for store0007
Pending approval | No change
DC approved: Store auto return | -1 | DMALL side: 1 quantity will be released from store 0007
DC approved: Store auto return | +1 | -1 | SAP side: TP301 post to SAP ( 1 quantity from store 0007 to DC 0007)
DC approved: DC auto receive | DMALL side: no change
DC approved: DC auto receive | +1 | -1 | SAP side: DC users perform transfer orders from DC 0007 to DC 0001 in SAP system
DC rejected: | +1 -1 | -1 | DMALL side: released 1 quantity from store 0007 to store 0031.deduct 1 quantity via stock adjustment from store 0031
DC rejected: | -1 | SAP side: GI937 post to SAP( 1 quantity deducted from store 0007)

Overserved:

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
Process | PO quantity | DC0001 （refer to SAP） | DC0007 （refer to SAP） | Store 0031 （refer to SAP and DMALL） | Store 0007 （refer to SAP and DMALL） | Procedure
Create PO | 10
DC outbound | -10
Store receiving (actual arrive quantity is 11, system auto trigger STDR) | -1 | +10 | +1 | DMALL side: add 1 quantity into store 0007 via stock adjustment function(adjust reason:TP301)
Store receiving (actual arrive quantity is 11, system auto trigger STDR) | -1 | +10 | +1 | SAP side: GR101 (with 10 quantities) Post to SAP. TP301(with 1 quantity) post to SAP from DC 0007 to store 0007

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
Dispatch

3.5 Transfer to vendor/warehouse

1.AS-IS Overview

Store raises a complicated RTV process and return to vendor/warehouse. based on pre-align logic to charge to vendor and dispose the unclaimed RTV order

2.TO-BE Overview

Goods transfer to Vendor/DC is the physical outbound movement of goods from store to Vendor/DC which results in a decrease in available SOH. It can be applied to below scenarios:

For outright and consignment with inventory articles should be returned to vendor (discentralized returns) or warehouse(centralized returns) in some cases, such as expired goods, damaged goods,etc,. To ensure all the return business online, we will support all the return order creation and approval online.

Some good goods need to be transferred to warehouse for some business purpose, NHO team should create the STO for store in SAP system. And store should perform the good issuance.

For backload with inventoried articles, NHO team should create the STO for store in SAP system. And store should perform the good issuance. (For backload with non-inventoried articles, need to follow the current practice to complete the good transfer).

For some special case, like SM bonus 5-gallon container return, store team can create the RPO in DMALL system and perform the good issuance.

Each article item line should choose the corresponding return reason, DMALL system can support to maintain different reason code for clarifying the business purpose.

For returnable articles, the store could return the article back to Vendor/DC, and to be approved by the approval officer with a certain threshold quantity.

Once the goods are issued from the return store, stock will be deducted from the store and the Store available SOH will be updated.

For non-returnable articles and consignors without inventory articles, users should perform stock adjustment function to adjust inventory

3.TO-BE Flow

4.TO-BE Description

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
1) End to End

In phase 1, RPO creation happens in DMALL system and SAP system. Once damaged items found, store users should scan article barcodes via PDT and generate RPO in DMALL system. Approval officer should approve the RPO in DMALL system based on current approval matrix. Approval officers do not need to do any approval in SAP anymore. After approving the RPO, DMALL system will send RPO item lines to SAP system. SAP should directly generate RPO based on data provided by DMALL and send back the RPO number to DMALL. RPO in SAP is the real return purchase order. RPO in DMALL is like a draft or reference return purchase order. There is a corresponding relationship between RPO in DMALL and RPO in SAP.

An article is returnable or not is a combination validation in DMALL system and it will integrates with SAP master data. Validation condition one: Vendor is returnable or not; Validation condition two: Vendor Item is returnable or not in purchase information. Only meet the above 2 conditions, DMALL system will regard it as returnable.

Only outright, consignor with inventory articles can be returned. Consignor without inventory cannot be returned.

Normally, return supplier is default as the supplier of source information. For some selected vendor, they request to return to DC first and vendor will pick it up in the DC. For this case, DMALL will integrate the special/selected return supplier table from SAP and map into source information table for clarifying the purchase vendor and return supplier.

Besides,DMALL system also support return to specific supplier which have purchase information. These suppliers are all integrated with SAP system vendor master data.

DMALL system only allow users to return returnable items.

Over-delivery and extra articles that do not exist in RPO are NOT allowed in DMALL design.

In phase 1, DMALL system only manages the inventory and cost is managed by SAP system. DMALL system will NOT show any relative cost information.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
2)  Function design

When users scan damaged items to return, they should select the return source location. The locations can be SLOC 0001 Selling Area, SLOC 0025 Dark Inventory and SLOC 0031 Backroom, 0002 offsite warehouse. When store return to warehouse/vendor, inventory should be deducted from the assigned location.

When scanning damaged article, if it is decentralized returns, DMALL will designate the return vendor from source information. If it is centralized returns, DMALL will list out the vendor list for user’s selection. They should pick up the correct vendor and proceed with it.

When scanning damaged article, return unit will be defaulted as base unit for convenience. Users can change to other package units, depending on the actual business. System will allow users to return different package units in same time.

When generating RPO, the same article will different package units will be considered as one return item. DMALL will group article item lines by vendor, site, category and return reason level and split them into different RPOs. Each return reason will be mapped to one Doc Type and applied when generating the RPO. The mapping relationship please refer to (SM)_(Phase 1A)_(System Integration Blueprint)_(Master Data-Config)_ver1.0.xlsx Table<Master List> Line No.13 Order Reason

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
When Dmall generates the RPO, approvers will receive an approval notification email. And they need to login in dmall system to handle it.

When DMALL generates the RPO and posts to SAP, for outright items, should post with TP311, for consignor with inventory items, should post with TP412

When DMALL get the true RPO number from SAP, users can print out the RPO label from DMALL system. The RPO label will be the same as current format.

DMALL system will support to return to previous level with comments/documents/images during the RPO approval process.

When vendor/warehouse trucker claim the RPO, store users need to scan case barcode or item barcode to add up the quantity for release.Dmall system will not allow users to input the return quantity for release directly.

DMALL system will follow the below rule to auto-disposal the goods and charge to the vendor (DMALL will post to SAP with 933).  store users need to offline communicate with warehouse and deliver the goods to them for disposal.

Fresh | Approval date + 7 days
Food | Approval date + 15 days
Drygoods | Approval date + 30 days

Dmall will execute the auto disposal programme to handle the Un-claimed RPO at midnight everyday.

Example:

RPO for fresh approval time: Jan 1st 2023,14:23:00

Dmall will auto dispose it at Jan 9th 2023,00:00:00 and disposal date is Jan 9th 2023

If damaged item is a consignment item or non-returnable, store users should perform stock adjustment function to adjust the inventory.

Return reason can be configured by merchant. Aligned as below:

Reason reason code | Return reason description
001 | Manufacturing Defect above 1 case
002 | Customer Return
003 | Others
004 | Mdsg Approved (Expired/Near Expiry)
005 | ZD_Vendor Pullout
006 | Change in Packaging
007 | Mdsg Approved (DTI/BFAD etc)
008 | Article Conversion
009 | Returnable Packaging
010 | Special Scenarios Aprvd by Mdsg Below 1cs
011 | Manufacturing Defect below 1 case
012 | Empty Gallon

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
3.6 Store transfer to store

1.AS-IS Overview

NHO team create STO in SAP system. Issuance site performs the dispatch in FRS system and handover the goods to trucker. Trucker will deliver to warehouse, then delivery to Receiving site. Receiving site performs the receiving in FRS system.

2.TO-BE Overview

Store transfer to store happens within same company. It can be called inter branch transfer (IBT). The STO creation happens in SAP in phase 1. Dmall will follow the AS IS process, for the issuance site and receiving site, they only perform the good issuance and good receiving.

Cause the trucker can not deliver the goods from store to store. They can only deliver the goods to the warehouse first. Then the warehouse request the trucker to delivery to the receiving site. To keep the order data flow the same as the physical goods transfer flow. IBT will be handled as 2 individual procedures in DMALL system.

3.TO-BE Flow

Dmall will support below two options

Option 1: Store transfer to store directly

Option 2: Store transfer to store via DC (The prerequisite is the transfer articles must be listed in DC)

4.TO-BE Description

3.8 Stock take(Inventory count)

1.AS-IS Overview

Two kinds of stock take: Physical count and cycle count

Operation DA users prepare the article list and get the approval from manager. Accounting department will create the Pcount order in current system. Count check will finish the counting and note down the count quantity in the finish sticker. Store officer will input the count quantity in current system. Accounting department will do the spot check by bay.

2.TO-BE Overview

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
Stock take is a physical inspection of all inventory's articles to verify their quantity and condition which encompasses all physical products that can be sold from the store and are under the store's ownership.

DMALL system can support physical count for all articles, cycle count for selected articles.
Accounting team create the inventory plan, each staff member should be responsible for a specific sector/bay to perform the counting execution and DMALL system will summarize the counting details.

3.TO-BE Flow

4.TO-BE Description

3 | Set inventory count plan | DMALL | Accounting | Approved Inventory count article list | Inventory count order | Accounting sets inventory count plan as follows: Inventory site: designated one inventory site, support to click all to select all inventory site. Stocktake mode: Store stocktake, 3rd party count Operation mode: Inventoy disply (show SOH when counting) Blinding count (hide SOH when counting) Over-range counting: Yes (can count articles out of the plan article lists) No (can not count articles out of the count article lists) Un-counted processing: Perform 0 inventory check (mandatory request users to count missing counted articles, otherwise not allowed to submit for approval) Auto count as 0 inventory (alert users missing counted articles will be regarded as 0 when submitting for approval) Choose inventory count date (multiple) Choose stores (multiple) Choose count article lists: All , by article , by category. support upload article list

4 | Count articles | DMALL | Selling | Inventory count order | Counting tasks and counted quantity | Before counting the articles, we should create the counting tasks first. 2 methods to create counting tasks: Accounting department or store manger to create and assign the counting task in the PC side. Counting staff members create the counting task by themselves on the PDA Staff login DMALL application/DMALL system(PC) to create count sheet by themshelves. Input Bay number first, scan article to count and input count quantity then submit. Each counted article should attach a finish sticker on the shelf to simply identify article is counted or not. Suggestion: each staff should be responsible for an assigned sector/bay to perform the counting execution.
5 | Monitoring counted situation | DMALL | Store manager | Counting tasks and counted quantity | / | Store supervisor can login to DMALL system(PC) to check count details. They can also download the count details in excel format.
6 | Spot check (re-count) | DMALL | Accounting | Inventory count details | Re-count task | Accounting assistant can create a re-count task to do spot check and adjust the count quantity
7 | Check completeness of count article lists | DMALL | Accounting | Inventory count details | / | Accounting supervisor can login to DMALL system(PC) to check final count details. They can also download the count details in excel format.
8 | Check the variance | DMALL | Selling | Inventory count details | Pending approval inventory count order | Check articles with high variance based on the count detail page. If no exception, then submit for approval
9 | Amend count quantity (Edit count quantity) | DMALL | Accounting | Inventory count details | Edited Inventory count details | Inform accounting to amend the count quantity if the counting result is not correct

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
10 | Submit for approval | DMALL | Selling | Inventory count details | Pending approval inventory count | After the financial department spot check and internal check, selling staff should submit the inventory count order for approval. Users can provide some evidence for approval (pictures, documents.,)
11 | Approve the variance | DMALL | Store manager | Pending approval inventory count order | Approval result | Store supervisor approves the count order. If rejected, count order will be turned back for checking.
12 | Approve the variance | DMALL | Approving officer | Pending approval inventory count order | Approval result | Approving officer approve the count order. If rejected, count order will be turned back for checking.
13 | Post inventory difference to SAP(955/956) | SAP | / | GI/GR | Posted GI/GR | After approval, DMALL will post count variance per stock take order to SAP with movement type-955/956

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
1）End to End

Stocktake function relies on merchandise master data and inventory data in DMALL. Only after approving the stocktake document, the counting variance will be posted to SAP.

All inventoried articles (‘Whether to manage inventory’ tag in merchandising master data is Yes) can be counted except below scenarios:

In merchandising master data, article is NOT allowed to perform counting

Article exists on the inventory count blacklist

Article without source information in master data

2)  Function design

Support counting during business hour:

Usually, stock take will be performed at store closure. But for 24 Hour Store on some special holidays, stock take can be performed during business hours if needed.

Either before or after physical stock take, stock movements may occur during business hours. These orders need to be closed by the store before the stock take. DMALL system will capture the stock movements which will impact the counting result.

When start the counting, DMALL will capture the stock SOH as reference(control group). DMALL system will also capture all stock movement happening before the counting, which will impact the counting result. DMALL system will not capture the stock movement after the counting because these stock movements will not impact the counting result.

System will list out the stock movement happening before the counting, and users need to accept or reject the impact. If accepted, count quantity will be adjusted. Otherwise, count quantity will not be changed.

Based on different purpose of counting, DMALL system has 3 types of count methods.

Primary count(can be called as initial count): count by count staff member. One article is displayed in different location and counted by different staff member. The counting result will be accumulated.

Re count: usually for supervisor or accounting department to do the re-count or spot check. The re-count quantity will override the primary count quantity.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
Edit count quantity: usually for supervisor or senior manager to directly amend the count quantity after variance investigation. The count quantity will override the count quantity of above 2 types of count methods. (highest priority)

articles | Before count quantity | Count quantity | After count quantity
Primary count 1 | / | 20
Primary count 2 | 20 | 10 | 30
Recount 1 | 30 | 35
Edit count quantity | 35 | 38 | Highest priority
Recount 2 | 38 | 40 | 38

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
Support 3rd party inventory count mode. 3rd party inventory count in DMALL system is just a count mode record. 3rd party users need to offline count the physical goods and upload the count quantity in excel format by article level to update the count result into DMALL system. And DMALL can continue counting approval and posting.

Support maintain inventory count article blacklists. When creating the inventory count order, system will filter out articles from the blacklists and these articles can not be counted.

Support maintaining the Location code dictionary by inventory location to manage the shelves in the store location. When performing counting, users can follow the location code to count. DMALL system will capture the location information and corresponding count quantity of articles.

When scanning an INF article, users can still input the counted quantity. Dmall will record and generate an INF article count report for users checking. They can download the report too. These articles will not be posted to SAP.

When users submit the inventory count order for approval, they can provide/upload some pictures or documents as evidence for explaining the high variance.

Approval matrix: The approval matrix shall be implemented based on the net amount(based on EOD MAV of stock take date) of adjustment per cycle count date.

SVI

Amount of adjustment (per inventory count) | Store manager | Cluster Head | AVP Operations | Mylene Mendoza | President
Below P5,000 | ✓ | ×
P5,000 to P40,000 | ✓ | ×
P40,001 to P200,000 | ✓ | ×
Above P200,000 | ✓

SMCO

Amount of adjustment (per inventory count) | Store manager | Cluster Head | AVP Operations | Athena Cajucom | President
Below P5,000 | ✓ | ×
P5,000 to P40,000 | ✓ | ×
P40,001 to P200,000 | ✓ | ×
Above P200,000 | ✓

SSM

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
Amount of adjustment (per inventory count) | Store manager | Cluster Head | AVP Operations | Vacancy (Replaced by President) | President
Below P5,000 | ✓ | ×
P5,000 to P40,000 | ✓ | ×
P40,001 to P200,000 | ✓ | ×
Above P200,000 | ✓

When Dmall posts to SAP, SAP will feedback the posting result to Dmall. Dmall will generate a corresponding Unposted Article Reports.

3) Key function layout

Note: this is just for explaining the function and logic. It might be changed when developing.

Add stocktake plan:create the stocktake plan.

Stocktake date can be today or the future day. But it cannot be the past day.

Stocktake order: query and manage all stock take order

Status:

Data preparing: when creating the stock take order, system will run a programe to obtain the aritcle and filter out some invalid article. After preparing, status will change to ‘stock take preparation’

Stock take preparation: stock take order is not active. Once users lock the inventory to capture the stock information, status will change to ‘Locking stock’

Locking stock: system is capturing the stock information as a copy for counting. Once completion, status will change to ‘Ongoing’

Ongoing: stock take order is active, users can operate related stock take operation. Once order is submitted, status will change to ‘pending approval’

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
Pending approval: stock take order is pending approval office approval. Once order is finally approved, status will change to  ‘posting’

Posting: system is handling the posting. This procedure will not take too much time. Once order is posted, status will change to ‘completed’

Completed: final status. It means the stock take order is completed and posted.

Canceled: if there is no valid article when system preparing the data, stock take order will be auto canceled. Or users can cancel the order if they want. Canceled Stock take order cannot be renewed.

Stock take order details: Manage and check count details

Article summary table is by article + inventory site + location code level.

DMALL will create 2 more tables under the 'Article summary' to sort out articles by article level or article-location code level.

Support query by below criteria:

Article

Category: follow the merchandise hierarchy

Status: counted, un-count, miss count

Types: stock loss, stock gain, no difference.

MAV: 0, negative and positive. ( EOD MAV of stock take date)

Add count task: store manager or supervisor can create count task and assign to specific count checker

Input Task name to describe the counting is for which location code

Select the unique stock count checker

Store manager can assign specific articles to the checker. (optional)

When checker receive the task, they can perform the counting

Create count sheet: count checker can create counting tasks by themselves

Input Task name to describe the counting is for which location code

Select the unique stock count checker (themselves)

Scan location code first, and then scan article one by one and input counted quantity

Scan the next location code and proceed with the counting as previous step.

Period change record: sales transaction which might impact on the counting result will be listed out in this table. Users should check and approve or reject it.

5.Exceptional Scenario

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
If the network is unstable or no network connection, users can switch to offline mode and perform the counting. Counting results will be updated to backend when network connecting. (only available for annual count/physical count)

If users find out the stock take plan is wrong during the stock take. Managers can download the counted result and create a correct stock take plan and upload the counted result. This solution is only for stocktake during the cloure time. It is not applicable for stock take during business time.

6.Related Reports

Real-time Inventory

Inventory Flow

Stock summary

Movement summary

When they scan the next start code, system will auto complete the previous bay counting | Simple the process | Count check | All companies in scope.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
3.9 Expiry date management

1.AS-IS Overview

Store users will note down the expiry date on the yellow sticker and attached outside the box. They will manually check the expiry date and perform the corresponding procedure.

2.TO-BE Overview

To avoid treading on brand reputation or causing customer complaints, store staff should check the shelf life of the goods on a daily basis and deal with the expired goods on time. This function is mainly for groceries or goods with long shelf-life. It is not suitable for monitoring goods with short shelf life.

3.TO-BE Flow

4.TO-BE Description

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
Step | Operation | System | Role | Input | Output | Details | Screenshot PC | Screenshot APP
1 | Setup the expiry monitoring rules for store | DMALL | NHO | / | Expiry monitoring rules | Setup monitoring rules as follows: Handle method: Sold out Disposal (auto trigger adjustment) Notice config: Alert X days in advance Alert time (xx:xx) Monitoring item scope: All By category By item
2 | Input the expiry date when receiving | DMALL | Selling | Expiry date | / | Input expiry date when receiving from vendor
3 | Find out Item not recorded expiry date | / | Selling | New expiry date batch | / | During the daily checking, they can input the expiry date data in any time. Or use this tool to input the expiry date when receiving from warehouse.
4 | Input expiry date by item | DMALL | Selling | Expiry date | / | Query by item code and input the new expiry date
5 | System will run a programe to capture goods that are about to expire | DMALL | / | pending checking item list | System will run a programe to capture the goods which hit the expiry monitoring rules every day
6 | Alert users pending checking item list | DMALL | / | pending checking item list | Notices | Check the pending list
7 | Find the about to expiry date goods | / | Selling | pending checking item list | / | Find out the specific goods
8 | Select handle method | DMALL | Selling | / | Handle method | Disposal, return, sold out or handle it next time
9 | Input quantity | DMALL | Selling | pending checking item list | Disposed quantity | If the goods need to be disposed, users need to input the disposal quantity. if they can't find any stock, they need to select sold out. System will not monitor this expiry date anymore until users input the same expiry date again.
10 | Auto trigger stock adjustment | DMALL | / | Disposal | Stock adjustment order | If the goods need to be disposed, system will auto trigger stock adjustment order and auto approve it.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
11 | Post to SAP (mvm type: 551) | SAP | / | GI | Posted GI | After approval, DMALL will pass the data to SAP with movement type 551
12 | Input quantity | DMALL | Selling | pending checking item list | Return quantity | If the goods need to be returned, users should input the return quantity for recording.
13 | Manually perform return process | DMALL | Selling | Return quantity | RTV process | Users should manually perform RTV process via DMALL system
14 | Pending execution next alert time | DMALL | Selling | pending checking item list | Notices | If users can not decide the handle method right now, they can choose to handle next alert time

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
1) End to End

Expiry date management function needs expiry date data to do monitoring. It relies on receiving function to collect the expiry date data, otherwise users need to input expiry date data manually in daily basis.

2)  Function design

Expiry date monitoring rules is by merchant level and it should be managed by NHO team.

Alert messages will be sent to designated roles which can be configured in system.

System support setup multiple monitoring rules. Each monitoring rule can apply to multiple or all articles.

When system runs the procedure to calculate the pending check item list, DMALL system will follow the below priority settings to generate the item list.

Rule | Item scope | Notice days | Process method | Priority | Result
A | Only for Item 123456 | 30 | Sold out, diposal | 1 | Hit
B | For all items | 15 | Disposal | 2 | No Hit

2 rules apply to item 123456. The priority of ruleA is higher than ruleB. DMALL will take rule A to generate the item list. Item 123456 will be warned before 30 days and they can decide to ‘disposal’ or ‘sold out’.

When setup the monitoring rules, system support to maintain the second alert time. Second alert time will start from the time when users confirm to check later. (confirmation time of check late + X days)

3) Key function layout sample

Note: this is just for explaining the function and logic. It might be changed when developing.

Edit Freshness rules: create freshness rules

The re-alert function is optional. Re-alert time starts from the first notice time. And users have one chance to delay the handling. The re-alert time value must be smaller than the first notice days value. (Aligned with Sir Romil: remove from the phase 1 scope)

Freshness Query: can review the checking status of each expiry date data by article level

Status: Check later (not hit the monitoring rules), To check (Hitted the monitoring rules ), Checked (completed checking).

Filter by : site, article, status, expiry on, enter on, check on

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
Support to query no more than 90 days data.

5.Exceptional Scenario

Our software needs network to operate and perform the data transmission.If the network is unstable or no network connection, PDA will not work. Please use it when the network available.

DMALL system will monitor the article and alert the users an article list that near expiry. The offline handling can be kept also. | New function | Selling RDU | All companies in scope.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
3.10 Gap check

1.AS-IS Overview

In SM current practice, the stock of selling area and backroom will mix up together. Dicer will move the goods from backroom to selling area for replenishment without any record or system involved.

2.TO-BE Overview

Gap Check is a regular daily routine of the store. Store Staff will have to identify article inventory with gaps and perform stock replenishment manually.

Store Staff detect the gap checks on shelves for defined article range according to the store planogram. They should proceed with manual replenishment to fill up the empty shelf slot and update the Store SOH of the article if any discrepancies have been found. If no stock can be found in the store, Store Staff should put on empty shelves and set the Store SOH(stock on hand) to zero to ensure the system SOH record is accurate.

For merchandiser replenishment, Dmall will provide a simple way for them. They can use the store inventory movement process function to finish the goods replenishment. (Business flow chart please refer to below, and function design please refer to 3.11 Store Inventory movement process)

3.TO-BE Flow

Flow chart with role

Standard Gap check flow:                                                                     Selling area replenishment flow for merchandiser

4.TO-BE Description (Standard Gap check)

content:

1 | Pre-set the gap check rules | DMALL | NHO | / | Gap check rules | Headquarter users should set the gap check rules: Assign specific roles to grab the checking task Checking task closure time configuration: Close it after X hr(s), start from creation time Close it at xx:xx:xx article with SOH=0 filter out configuration: If this configuration turns on, system will auto filter articles with SOH=0 and not generate the checking task.
2 | Find a gap on the shelf | / | Selling | / | Empty shelf | Selling area staff find an article is out of stock on the shelf
3 | Scan the shelf label | DMALL | Selling | Shelf label | Article information | Scan the shelf label or input article code
4 | Submit and create tasks | DMALL | Selling | Scanned articles | Replenishment task | The system will show the bin location number on the screen for reference. Based on the SOH, users can recommend the replenishment quantity and submit for creating checking task.
5 | Grab the checking task | DMALL | Selling | Replenishment task | Pending execution replenishment task | Someone who has the authority can grab/receive the checking task
6 | Check physical goods in the backroom | / | Selling | Pending execution replenishment task | / | Someone who grabbed the checking task should go to the backroom to check the physical goods
7 | Mark as out of stock | DMALL | Selling | Pending checking article | OOS | If an article is out of stock in the backroom(can not find any stock), users should mark it as out of stock
8 | Manually perform stock adjustment/cycle count to correct inventory | DMALL | Selling | OOS article | Stock adjustment order | If they find out the inventory is not correct, they should manually perform stock adjustment or cycle count to correct the inventory

10 | Auto trigger storage location transfer | DMALL | / | Replenishment | Stock transfer order | DMALL system will auto trigger storage location transfer function to adjust the inventory

3.11 Store Inventory movement Process

1.AS-IS Overview

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
In SM FRS system, there is a TP posting function to adjust inventory from one site-one location to another site or the same site – another location. Based on different scenarios, there are different movement types to handle it. Article stock dose not divided into selling area and backroom.

2. TO-BE Overview

This function is for storage location transfer which happens in one store. It can be performed by store users at any time.The scenarios for stock inventory transfer may include: replenishment for selling area from backroom, moving goods from offsite warehouse to selling are and other regular transfers in store. Not include the STDR scenario.

3. TO-BE Flow

Flow chart with role

4. TO-BE Description

Step | Operation | System | role | input | output | Details | Screenshot PC | Screenshot APP
1 | Create a stock transfer order | DMALL | Store staff | site transfer-in location、department transfer-out location、department article Transfer quantity | transfer order draft created | Select store first, and then select transfer-out location and transfer-in location Select transfers items and key in transfer quantity
2 | Submit the order | DMALL | Store staff | Click save button | transfer order created
3 | Auto Approval | DMALL | System | Task center receives transfer order request from user and pending approval by approver | transfer order to be approved | The system will perform an auto approval procedure.
4 | Update inventory in DMALL | DMALL | System | Transfer order is approved | Inventory adjustment | Transfer-out location inventory will be deducted Transfer-in location inventory will be increased
5 | Post to SAP | DMALL | System | Inventory adjustment | Posted data | If involves 0030 advanced orders location, Dmall will post to SAP with movement type 311

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
1)End to End

dependent on upstream data：site master data（include location management）、merchandise master data、inventory  data

2)Function design

The inventory transfer does not support SLOC 0003 Bad Order location and SLOC 0007 STDR from DC&Store location.Transfer-in and transfer-out location could be SLOC 0001 Selling Area, SLOC 0025 Dark Inventory, SLOC 0031 Backroom, SLOC 0002 Offsite Warehouse, SLOC 0030 Advanced Orders.

Transfer-out location default as backroom, and transfer-in location default as sell ing area. Users can click ‘Reverse’ button to quickly flip transfer-out location and transfer-in location.

If users are merchandiser, after submitting the document, system will re-direct to create new order page.

Merchandisers are not allowed to see article SOH and article cost.

If approval matrix is needed, the system can support setup transfer quantity as approval criteria.

When input the transfer quantity, users can choose to input by barcode and then key in the quantity by barcode unit. Transfer quantity will be shown by piece.

Supporting the management of inter-departmental information in handling adjusting inventory processes.

If the inventory location configured for the 'transfer out' operation does not allow negative stock, the system will prevent the execution of the transfer when the quantity to be transferred exceeds the current stock quantity at that location.

in such a scenario, to proceed with the transfer, you have two options:

Adjust the transfer quantity: User can modify the quantity to be transferred to ensure it does not exceed the available stock at the current location.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
Adjust the configuration of the transfer-out inventory location: If it is essential to transfer the originally specified quantity, you may consider allowing negative stock in the configuration of the transfer-out inventory location. This adjustment would permit the system to execute the transfer even if the outgoing quantity exceeds the current stock, allowing for negative stock levels in the specified location.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
3.12 SM Eat

1. AS-IS Overview

When As is performing a SM Eat in the SAP system, it is required to create two Goods Issue (GI) documents separately for the goods being transferred out and the goods being transferred in. Use the movement type to indicate that the current document corresponds to inventory adjustment related to SM Eat

2. TO-BE Overview

This function can be used for handling raw material articles and processed articles inventory transfer. It requests to maintain the Bill of materials(BOM) first.store staff create an SM Eat document to record the SM Eat process and system will transfer the inventory based on it.Both raw material articles and processed articles inventory can be managed in the system.The scenarios for production may include:bundle ,saleset,valuepack and other regular production in store,e.g turon、fruit/vegetable cuts.

The key points of the process design include:

The central manufacturing team maintains the production ratio of ingredients and finished articles.

The store checks if the raw materials inventory quantity is available as per SM Eat requirement .

BOM relationship management function is in article master data module, for detailed BOM information, please refer to the master data blueprint.

3. TO-BE Flow

Flow chart with role

If approved, order will be approved and inventory will be updated.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution
content:
3.13 Dismantle

1. AS-IS Overview

When As is performing a dismantle in the SAP system, it is required to create two Goods Issue (GI) documents separately for the goods being transferred out and the goods being transferred in. Use the movement type to indicate that the current document corresponds to inventory adjustment related to dismantle

2. TO-BE Overview

This function can be used for handling raw material articles and processed articles inventory transfer. It requests to maintain the Bill of materials(BOM) first. Store staff create an dismantle document to record the dismantle process and system will transfer the inventory based on it.Both raw material articles and processed articles inventory can be managed in the system.

The scenarios for dismantle may include: Articles bundled at the DC, if unsold at the store, are dismantled and sold as individual components. （ This functionality is only applicable to disbundle with a fixed BOM relationship. For disbundle without a fixed BOM relationship, the disbundle needs to be performed using the inventory adjustment feature and completed with movement type 949/950 for the TP process.

The key points of the process design include:

The central manufacturing team maintains the dismantle ratio of depack and pack articles.

The store checks if the raw materials inventory quantity is available as per dismantle requirement .

BOM relationship management function is in article master data module, for detailed BOM information, please refer to the master data blueprint.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution > 3.16 Stock adjustment
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

When SM as is performing inventory adjustments in the SAP system, it is necessary to create a Goods Issue (GI) document. For each document, input the corresponding movement type number to identify different reasons for inventory adjustments.

2. TO-BE Overview

In the process of running stores, staff need to constantly monitor the accuracy of store inventory. When the physical inventory of  articles doesn't match the system's records, staff need to initiate the inventory adjustment process.

The scenarios for inventory adjustments may include: No PO receiving,goods from heaven,throw away,surplus,scrapping ,product expiration, product quality issues, internal and external theft,  product compensation, store-initiated inventory adjustments, inventory calibration etc.

Since inventory adjustments can affect inventory costs and performance calculations, store operators need tools to assist with their daily inventory adjustments.At the same time, the management of the business needs to control the limits of inventory adjustments. They approve inventory adjustment forms submitted by internal staff, review the data records of inventory adjustments, and conduct issue tracing.

In total, the current design for the stock adjustment has two functions:

1) stock adjustment reason mapping with movement type;

2) stock adjustment order management,which includes creating,deleting,searching and editing.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution > 3.16 Stock adjustment
content:
Step | Operation | System | Role | Input | Output | Details | Screenshot PC | Screenshot APP
1 | Create a stock adjustment order | DMALL | user | site、adjustment type、adjustment reason、remark（optional） article、quantity | Adjustment order draft created | Select store first, then select adjustment type, inventory location and adjustment reason (mandatory) Select articles and key in adjustment quantity
2 | Submit the order | DMALL | user | Click save button | Adjustment order created | Click save button After Input all required fields
3 | Approval | DMALL | approver | Task center receives adjustment order request from user and pending approval by approver | Adjustment order to be approved | Approvers who have the authority should check and approve it If rejected, order will be rejected and inventory will not be changed. If approved, order will be approved and inventory will be updated.
4 | Update inventory in DMALL | DMALL | Inventory system | Adjustment order is approved | Adjustment order is adjusted, and available SOH changed | The system updates adjustment order & increase/decrease available SOH
5 | Post to SAP | SAP | site、article、TP type、location、quantity | Adjustment data is transferred back to SAP | After approval, DMALL will post it to SAP with assigned movement type

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution > 3.16 Stock adjustment
content:
1)End to End

dependent on upstream data：site master data、merchandise master data、inventory initial data

2）Function design

Each location in DMALL must be mapped with  one location in sap

store staff can select adjustment location by each order

Adjustment location has no default. It could be SLOC 0001 Selling Area, SLOC 0025 Dark Inventory, SLOC 0031  Backroom, SLOC 0002 Offsite Warehouse

Adjustment reason will cover a specific scenario from one movement type

If specific adjustment reason needs to be restricted to certain roles, additional authorization configuration is required.

If specific functionality needs to be limited to a specific range of articles, users can follow SOP manually,the system will not control it.

Article status will control one article can be adjusted or not by adjustment reason which is mapping with movement type 949\950

Approval matrix： The approval matrix shall be implemented based on the net amount(based on cost/MAV of creation date) of adjustment per cycle count date.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution > 3.17 TPS
content:
System background introduction

To provide key information for customers to decide, article information, promotion information and price information will be displayed by price tag, according to different scenarios, it can be put on the shelf or directly tag on the articles.

TPS system provides Shelf tag to manage price tag information, simplify the price tag print and paster operation.

1. AS-IS Overview

Currently, the SM TPS system supports printing shelf label price tag and other tags like location rack, pallet ID, employment ID. Each price tag template is programed in the system in advance.

There are regular PCA, promotion PCA and Majorcon price label types, different label templates will be auto mapped by outright and majorcon articles.

Billing from the vendor by quantity when printing the price labels for consignment vendors.

2. TO-BE Overview
