title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution > 3.17 TPS
content:
3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

4.1 Basic configuration

Set price tag types which will be used for the merchant

Set maximum number of prints: it's to avoid paper waste if manually input too large numbers per single price tag

Set by store level or account level

4.2 Paper configuration

Define paper name and choose paper type: shelf price label or value pack

It can be available to all stores or selected stores, so the corresponding data can be found when query by stores

Set paper size(mm), width and height

Set paper Margin(mm), Left and top margin

Set label size(mm), width and height

Set the columns and rows for the label. For example, one row-two price tags, one paper -8 price tags.

Enabling Status to control whether apply the paper or not

Quick copy of current paper format to edit and save to be a new paper.

4.3 Template Mgt

Define template name

Choose label type: shelf label or clearance price tag

Choose template type: Promotion or normal

Choose print paper: which is created in advance

Enabling Status to control whether apply the template or not

Quick copy of current paper format to edit and save to be a new template

Visible canvas to let suer design the template, components like text, image, QR code, rectangle, barcode, line are provided and can be easily dragged to combine

Preview to continue editing or saved

4.4 Print request

When there's missing, damaged or wrong information price tag, store staff can scan the articles via DMALL APP and batch submit print out applications, the records can be found in the system.

Print request menu in PC

4.5 Full store label

When a new store opens, all store articles price tags information can be checked under the tab, user can choose certain or all line articles to be printed out.

Query condition filters like store code, article code, category code and price type are provided.

Download the excel template file from the panel by clicking the Import button.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 3. Function Design Solution > 3.17 TPS
content:
Fill in the excel template file with articles and print count

Upload the file to system via the panel open by clicking the Import button

Choose to print all articles or print all the articles just uploaded.

Export (This feature is available in both Full Store Label/Changed Label/To be changed Label)

Export articles in excel form. (not for printing, just a list of articles)

Print log (This feature is available in both Full Store Label/Changed Label/To be changed Label)

Previous print operations history. A short-cut for users to re-print.

4.6 Changed label

If the article or promotion information is changed, updates will be showed under this tab, after comparison, changed data will be recorded and generate pending printing data for the store staff.

If the user only has one store authority, the system will auto display all the data of the store, if user has multiple stores’ authority, system will take one of the stores to display data, user can user condition filter to check other stores.

Multiple condition filters are provided for user to easily check

title: 3. Function Design Solution > 3.1 Site Master data system design and structure > 3.1.3 Inventory location by site
content:
1.AS-IS Overview

Inventory sites are managed in SAP

2.To-Be Overview

Inventory sites will be managed in DMALL system, total 7 inventory sites

3.To-Be Flow

In the DMALL system, the store will be divided into 7 inventory locations. And each store operation function/ movement action will operate in a specific inventory location. Each inventory location has its own inventory data. It will not be mixed up together so that we can clearly identify the current inventory situation and make smart decisions.

SLOC 0001 Selling Area: selling area inventory indicates the sales floor goods quantity. When POS selling, inventory should be deducted from the selling area.

SLOC 0025 Dark Inventory: This location is used for storing goods for online order picking. And the location is on the sales floor.

SLOC 0031 Backroom: Backroom inventory indicates the quantity of storage goods when goods are replenished into sales floor shelves, users should perform the stock transfer function to adjust the inventory

SLOC 0002 Offsite Warehouse: This location indicates the storage goods quantity in the outside warehouse

SLOC 0007 STDR from DC&Store: claim location is only used for delivery discrepancy report scenario. these stock should not be sellable or used for any business. Stores should separate an individual area for displaying these goods.

SLOC 0003 Bad Order Location: bad order location is only used for return scenario.

SLOC 0024 Holding location. These inventories should not be sellable or used for any business.store should separate an individual area for displaying these goods.

SLOC0001 selling area, SLOC 0025 Dark Inventory, SLOC 0031 backroom will be applied to picking system.

Inventory location can be set up by store level

Inventory site to be maintained in advance.

4. TO-BE Description

Page screenshot | Function Description
Create inventory site and fill in necessary information
Choose existing inventory sites to be applied when maintaining the new site

title: 3. Function Design Solution > 3.2 Article master data design > 3.2.1 Article Master Data Integration details
content:
1.AS-IS Overview

Currently, all the article master data is maintained in SAP，user can only add, delete, edit article information in SAP and interface to other systems.

2.To-Be Overview

1.Some fields existed in SAP and necessary to be interfaced in phase 1A still will be maintained in SAP and interfaced to DMALL system, data will be transformed and saved in DMALL database, user can only review the data, can’t edit or delete.

2.Some fields which existed in DMALL but not available in SAP, user can manage in DMALL system, data can be reviewed and edited. The data will be applied to inventory and POS.

3.Some modules like emergency price change, Bom articles process will be managed in DMALL system, user can review and edit and the data, the data will be applied to inventory and POS.

3.To-Be Flow

4. TO-BE Description

Integration:

Integration after phase 1A switch involves systems such as SAP, Retail Affiliates MMS, DAC, ARC, BIR, e-Treasury, Credit Info System, and Mall sales etc. In the following sub chapters, these integrations are categorized into master data, Inventory and EOD.

Master-data includes Merchandise Hierarchy, Brand, Article Base Info, Article Package, Article Package Barcode, BOM Article, Site-Article, Site-Article-Price, Vendor, Purchase Information and Source Information. Most of these data should be loaded from SM SAP to DMALL Master data System, with some exceptions:

Major consigner master data should be loaded from Retail Affiliates MMS.

Saleset will be maintained in SAP and interface to DMALL system.

The other Bom articles like dismantle article, SM Eats article and TP article can be maintained in DMALL OS system.

SAP

1.Merchandise Hierarchy

Background: Merchandise hierarchy is applied to define the article belongs to which category level; user can check business data according to different category.

All the fields can only be reviewed, can’t be edited.

Integration Approach: JCo API (FM: ZRFC_RETRIEVE_MERCAT)

title: 3. Function Design Solution > 3.2 Article master data design > 3.2.1 Article Master Data Integration details
content:
3.1 For Top items.  User will maintain in DMALL and be saved in Article master data＞basic information＞Extended information

Article tags can be overlapped, such as one article can be top 888 and top 4k at the same time

Top 888
Top 4k
SME ARTICLES (Small and Medium Enterprise)

3.2 For special items, system will tag it by article name

If one article is Value pack, it will not be other tag anymore and will not allow to be involved in promotion.

The other tags can be overlapped, such as one article can be SC/PWD and medicine item at the same time

VP: Value Pack
+: SC/PWD item
@: medicine item
#: RTE Item
^: single parent tag

3.3 SPU articles

The information will be included in integration. Here's to describe more details related to the SPU articles scenario.

Item may have multi specification, like different color and size will be managed by clothes. Currently in SAP there's generic and variant structure to identify the other item and child item.

There's new article category in DMALL will be generic/variant

Different attributes can be different article code, if there's multi- package, then system will also have different packages

One package can have multiple barcodes, but one barcode can only belong to one package

There's no store article range for generic items

Variant item+package is the smallest sales unit, selling price can be different per variant+package, inventory deduction will be pc

Example

content:
5.1 Barcode analyze rule

This will follow current as is rule, no change to current practice

There are two barcodes analyze rule, one is for standard item with price, the other is weighted items with weight, price and weight will not be included in one barcode at the same time.

Barcode service to Inventory& POS

POS | When POS scan the code, need to analyze the article code, package information and scale information（total weight and total price）
Inventory | Scan article code to perform SM Eat, Dismantle, inventory adjustment and inventory movement
Picking | Scan the scale code to analyze the article code for fresh count and weight articles Scan the barcode and analyze the article code

b.Barcode analyze rules for weighted items.

1.1st 2 digits-28-weight based;27-price based

2.Next 5 digits is item code

3. Weight-5 digits

4.Price (Total price)- 5 digits

5.Check digit-1 digit

Sample:

c.Special scenarios

Saleset will be integrated from SAP to Dmall system, and saved as in store bundle and dismantle relationship at the same time.

Other Bom articles like SM Eats and conversion can be managed in the system and the data can be reviewed and edited, Bom relationship will be applied to inventory for operation.

title: 3. Function Design Solution > 3.2 Article master data design > 3.2.1 Article Master Data Integration details
content:
If article status is Z1 new article, then it means item in all stores should not be allowed to sell.

7.1 Article status and business control

Below table defines the available status and functionalities and the setting can be checked as article lifecycle in achieves setting module in DMALL system.

For majorcons items, the article status will be blank during the integration.

Article status control in DMALL system

No. | Article status | Description
1 | Allow transfer | To create transfer order between store to Store
2 | PI Doc | Allow store to perform stock take PI=Physical inventory.
3 | Returns | Can create return order
4 | PO Auto | System can generate suggested order quantity and release PO automatically, it includes of store to DC STO, store PO to vendor or DC PO to vendor
5 | PO Man | Manual input order quantity by site by article and create order, it includes of store to DC STO, store PO to vendor or DC PO to vendor
6 | Allow sales returns | Even an article is sold out, it can be returned to the merchant
7 | Sales | Article can be sold in store
8 | Is terminate lifecycle | The final status of an article, no business control
9 | Is start lifecycle | The initial status of an article, no business control
10 | Allow adjustment | Adjust the article’s inventory
11 | Blank | No Restriction, article will be regularly ordered via automated replenishment, this one will keep same
12 | Manual out/auto out | Delivery Note (DN) can be created for the automatic STO for the tagged article/store Manual Out - Delivery Note (DN) can be created for manual STO for the tagged article/store

After DMALL receives the article status, it will be saved and tagged Yes or No for each business control and the result will be applied to POS and inventory system when perform certain operation.

title: 3. Function Design Solution > 3.2 Article master data design > 3.2.2 More Description with article master data scenario > 3.2.2.1 PLU by request change flow
content:
1.AS-IS Overview

There are two price change flow. Regular price change and PLU by request, all the price change will be complete in SAP system.

2.To-Be Overview

Regular price change: keep current practice and integrate to DMALL system before cutting off time: This will be included in integration.

Real time interface: For PLU by request, user need to change store article price in SAP, then log in DMALL system to upload store article list, then the latest price will be reflected for POS.--This will be maintained in DMALL system, it requires user to manually operate.

3.To-Be Flow

Description:

No. | System | Node | Description | Remark
1 | SAP | Upload | Upload all store article price change information in SAP system
2 | DMALL price file management | Logging | There will be a new menu which provided for the users to upload store article list which require price change
3 | DMALL price file management | Synchronize | After uploading the store article list and confirmation, the task will be executed to catch the new price from SAP by store article level.
4 | DMALL price file management | Data query | User can check the latest selling price in the module: Article retail price

4. TO-BE Description

Page screenshot | Function Description
User can upload by store by article to confirm to catch the latest price change Check the latest selling price in the module: Article retail price

5. Exceptional Scenario

Not applicable

Not applicable

Key Changes

title: 3. Function Design Solution > 3.3 Majorcon
content:
This chapter is designated for the Majorcon master data integration.

Note that the Majorcon data should be Read-Only in DMALL system.

1.AS-IS Overview

Majorcon(like MINISO) articles are managed by MMS, without inventory, only use by POS system for sales, but in SAP maintained by a unified item code for each vendor.

2.To-Be Overview

Strictly follow the as-is design, not allowed to be managed in DMALL master data system, because of the large amount of articles coming from majorcon that may pollute the regular articles pool.

There are two proposals in the document and only solution 2 is adopted to be released in Phase 1A.

3. To-Be Flow

Solution 2:

An item tag logic will be created in article master data to logically separate majorcon and regular articles.

A sales code transfer logic will be created between POS order and Omnichannel order.

Only 1 site price record for site articles if the price is the same for all stores.

Majorcon article codes: It will be MJ+majorcon article code, user can separate the majorcon articles by the prefix MJ

Duplicate barcode between majorcon and outright articles: The data will be filtered and regarded as abnormal data; it won’t be saved in article master data. Exception messages will be included in the integration exception report.

Delete majorcon articles: To avoid too much outdated majorcon data staying in the system, SM will provide majorcon DELETE files periodically to remove the outdated data.

User can still find the article in the system with the status: Deleted. Delete majorcon articles will not be available to do business.

Sales：All the sales will be counted in the major article code.

4.To Be description

There are 6 Majorcons maintained in Affiliate system and will be integrated to DMALL system

Majorcon name | Majorcon code | EAN category
Supplies Station | 25 | Z2
Toy World | 33 | Z2

title: 3. Function Design Solution > 3.4 Vendor master data design > 3.4.2 Vendor return maintenance
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

There’s only vendor article level return control, no vendor level return control in SAP

2. TO-BE Overview

User can maintain vendor level return in DMALL system and whether one article can be return or not will apply vendor level and vendor article level verification

Only both vendor level and vendor+item level are returable,then the item can be returned.

Example:

Vendor | Article | Returnable | Can create return order
Alpha | Returnable
1 | Returnable | Yes
2 | NonReturnable | No
Beta | NonReturnable
3 | Returnable | No
4 | NonReturnable | NO

3. TO-BE Flow

There’s no field to indicate whether the vendor can be returned or not currently, for phase 1A, user can maintain vendor level return flag in vendor master module in DMALL system.

4. TO-BE Description

Page Screenshot | Function Description
Whether an item can be returned or not will be decided by vendor returnable flag maintained in DMALL and vendor+item returnable flag maintained in SAP which will be interfaced to DMALL system

5. Exceptional Scenario

Not applicable

6. Related Reports

Not applicable

7. Key Changes

Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
There’s only vendor article level return control, no vendor level return control in SAP | User can maintain vendor level return in DMALL system and whether one article can be return or not will apply vendor level and vendor article level verification | New | Master data team /Inventory team | All companies in scope.

Remark: Aligned fields contained in will be maintained  in Dmall system: Integration Documents to SM mapping.xlsx

title: Blueprint Document Scope > Background Introduction
content:
In SM store digitalization project Phase 1, DMALL is going to help SM to replace and implement POS, store operation and inventory related systems in order to enhance both business and system operation flow to meet current business requirements and to face future changes. As defined in the project scope, this document will focus on POS and POS related systems functions which describe system solutions for different scenarios or user cases.

The aim for the document is to make sure that the system design can fully support SM business requirements and will be the fundamental and instructive document for the coming PRD and development stage.

In part 2 of this document, a list will be provided to list all the user cases with the understanding of the scenarios and to-be basic function description as the result document for TO-BE stage and have already reached an agreement between DMALL, JWD and SM. In part 3, all the user cases in part 2 will be described in detail in the form of system solutions with system flow chart and function description.

Main Scenario | no change | optimization | new function | Total | Not in scope | Description
1. Installation & Start | 1 | 2 | 3
2.User management | 2 | 1 | 5
3.Sales | 5 | 12 | 2 | 19
4.Marketing | 3 | 4 | 7
5. Pay | 3 | 1 | 7
6. Return | 1 | 3
7. Cash management | 4 | 1 | 5
8. Other case | 5 | 4 | 2 | 11 | 2 | PVT/TPS
Total | 24 | 29 | 7 | 60

title: Blueprint Document Scope > System Overview
content:
Overall architecture diagram (For detailed information, please refer to the integration blueprint)

POS functional architecture diagram

DMALL system/module definition

System | Module | Definitions
POS | Device Mgt | POS installation & registration Remote upgrade
Account Mgt | Cashier login/out Authorization based on functions with its amount range Lock & unlock POS
Sales | Regular sales functions include different article type support, add-to-cart, article/order price override, void, suspend/restore order, etc. Other types of sales such as govn sales, pre-order will be supported
Marketing | Marketing promotion and discount calculation SMAC membership support E-voucher support
Pay | Different tender support such as cash, EFT, online tender, offline tender
Return | Return with/without receipt Refund
Cash mgt | End of Shift (Pre-commit), End of Day Float, pickup management
Receipts | Receipts for different types of transactions Reprint
Offline service | Including business logic and offline database to ensure basic transaction without in-store/cloud server
Other | Customer screen, setting, item/order query, etc.
Integration/peripherals | EFT-Card terminal provided by BDO which integrated with POS for payment (credit/debit card, G-cash, SMAC points, etc.) Other hardware such as scanner, printer, drawer
In-store POS server | Offline data mgt | Fetch business offline data from cloud server and distribute it to POS terminal
Sales data transfer | Fetch and store different types of transaction data and send it to cloud server whenever the network is connected
Registration & Upgrade | Registration and remote upgrade for in-store POS server program
Cloud server | Device center | POS device registration, verification and peripheral setting POS version management POS device status monitor
Configuration center | Different configurations related to business logic or interface display in merchant & store level

title: Blueprint Document Scope > System Overview
content:
User Mgt | User & account management such as registration, verification, etc. Authorization management for function and data perspective
Site Mgt | POS belonging store information maintenance and verification
Article system | Article master data management Category, brand, bom, etc. management Barcode analysis service
Promotion system | Various promotion types of activity creation and management Promotion calculation and discount allocation logic
Payment system | Tender management and configuration Payment amount allocation and record Online payment integration
POS order system | POS sales transaction record with list and details
POS return system | Return verification and refund amount calculation POS return transaction record list and details
Advertisement system | Advertisement activity management (effective time, store, priority) Synchronize advertisement activity with images that display on POS customer screen
Cash management system | Float, pickup, end of shift record Short and over report
Offline data platform | Offline service registration & upgrade Offline data management including transfer data from different system to offline data platform then to POS with data version management
Other | Other systems that do not have direct contact with POS

title: Function Design Solution > Installation & Start > POS Installation/Upgrade
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today legacy POS can be installed with an installation package and upgraded remotely with the store patch program.

2. TO-BE Overview

With the DMALL POS, the new POS machine with at least Windows 10 version needs to install the DMALL POS program manually with an installation package for the first time.

For further iteration, upgrades can be downloaded and installed remotely & automatically on each POS terminal individually via backend version release management.

3. TO-BE Overview

4. TO-BE Description

Page Screenshot | Function Description
Automatic Install New Version | Installation DMALL will provide an installation package to SM team, and we can manually install the DMALL POS on the Window 10 (and above) system by following the step-by-step guide. No manual input required. Start POS Application POS will be automatically started along with the Window System start. Upgrade Before a new version is released, DMALL team will align with SM IT team to fully test the version in the lab with testing environment. When a new version of the POS system is available and pass the SM team SIT/UAT process, the update process is as follows: DMALL implementation team notifies the SM team about the new version and its release note, and discuss the upgrade plan, including: Upgrade Timing Release method: general release or grayscale release DMALL will remotely push the new version to the POS system. The POS terminal detects the new release on the cloud and automatically initiates the download of the new version in the background without impacting sales. Update installation: Once download is finished, the POS system will be updated automatically during the next reboot.

5. Exceptional Scenario

title: Function Design Solution > Installation & Start > POS Installation/Upgrade
content:
Typically, the POS program will initiate automatically upon the startup of the Windows system. However, if the POS is exited or fails to start automatically, store team members can locate the DMALL POS application icon on the desktop and initiate the program by double-clicking on it.

If a new version of the POS has been released online, the POS terminal will automatically download it in the background, ensuring uninterrupted sales operations. If the download is unsuccessful due to a network issue, the POS will automatically resume the download once the network connection is restored.

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
POS upgrade needs a specific upgrade patch program deployed in the store. | Each POS terminal can upgrade on its own as long as there is network connection. | Change: AS-IS needs an upgrade patch program deployed in store but the To-BE solution no need to have this. | Store IT | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Installation & Start > POS Device Registration
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Presently, new POS registration and POS management is handled by the POS vendor. IT team needs to reach out vendor to finish the registration or enable/disable POS device.

2. TO-BE Overview

Moving to DMALL, every new POS needs to be registered from backend or from the POS client directly by assigning store ID, POS ID, external device info with a specific mac address. In this way, we can easily view the device situation and manage the POS devices from the backend system.

Please note that TIN, Store info is not per device, but per store. When we select the store ID, the store info will be attached automatically.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

Prerequisite:

DMALL implementation team will assist in creating all stores in the DMALL system during the UAT phase. For new stores in the future, it is suggested to reaching out DMALL to finish all necessary configuration, or we can handover the step-by-step tutorials on how to create the new store during the training phase.

title: Function Design Solution > Installation & Start > POS Device Registration
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Device List Entry Device List & Create Button Create POS Device Fill POS Device Info Fill in Peripheral Information Enable/Disable POS | POS Backend Registration (recommend) It is most common to use the backend system to finish the POS device registration process. Access the Partner backend system and navigate to "Device Management / Device List". Choose the store where the device is registered and click on the "Add" button located in the upper right corner. Follow the steps below to successfully add the POS device: Step 1: Select the store and equipment category Select a Store; Select the equipment category < Cashier Equipment/Manual Cash Register >, select the category name < Win-POS >, click < Next >. Step 2: Provide the necessary device details Device code: Enter the MAC address of the POS machine, which serves as the unique identifier for device registration. Device Name: Assign a customized name to the device. Terminal ID: Unique terminal ID in the store ranging from 001 to 999 Location: Optionally specify the physical location of the equipment. Touch screen functionality: Indicate whether the device's main screen is touch-enabled or not. Select < touch screen > for all SM devices Step 3: Fill in the peripheral information. Peripheral information (like printer, scanner, etc.) can be registered in the Partner backend without the need for manual configuration. Additionally, the POS Client is equipped with the capability to configure peripherals. Upon completing the information, click on the <Save> button to register the device. Enable the device: Once successfully registered, return to the list of POS devices. Select the corresponding store and click on <Query>. Locate the newly registered device and click on <Enable>. The device will then be ready for use.

title: Function Design Solution > Installation & Start > POS Device Registration
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Start POS Program without Registration Register from the POS Client | POS Client Registration New POS usually is registered from the backend system. But POS also provides the ability to register from the POS client. When we start up the DMALL POS application, if the POS is not registered from the backend, we can also finish the registration from the POS client by following steps. Launch the POS Client. If the device has been registered before, then it will jump to the login page directly. If the device is not registered, the system will prompt with the message "The device has not been configured. Would you like to initialize the device?" Click on [Confirm] to proceed to the device initialization page. Click on [Cancel] to close the pop-up window. If you still need to initialize the device, you can click on [Initialize] to register the device. Device Initialization: Once confirm initialize POS from the POS client, there are a few fields needs to fill. Merchant No: Enter the unique code assigned to the merchant that requires registration, usually provided by DMALL team. All SM POS will share the same merchant No.; Store Code: After entering the merchant ID, click on "Store" to select the appropriate store where this device needs to be registered. Terminal No: Enter the corresponding unique POS ID assigned to this device within the store. Once all the information is entered, click on [Confirm] to complete the device registration process.

5. Exceptional Scenario

If you encounter the POS prompt "Device not found, please enable the device first," when you startup POS you will need to activate POS on the Partner System.

Access the "Device Management / Device List" section, locate the device you registered for, and click on <Activate>. This will enable the device and allow for its usage.

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

title: Function Design Solution > Installation & Start > POS Device Registration
content:
Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
POS can only be registered or managed by reaching out to the vendor. | POS can be registered from both the backend portal and POS client directly. | New: POS can not only be registered from the backend, but also be registered directly on the POS client. | Store IT | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Installation & Start > Roving POS
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today, all roving POS are kept in the HQ and the IT team needs to manually move the POS, POS Server to the store, waiting for BIR approval and then ask the vendor to finish the configuration.

2. TO-BE Overview

Moving to DMALL, with the device registration feature, any POS device can be effortlessly assigned into a certain store by changing the Store ID from the registration page mentioned above and eliminating the requirement for an additional POS server.

IT team just need to move the POS device to the store (no need POS server), waiting for the BIR approval and then change the store ID of each roving POS from the Parter System & reboot POS. No need to reload the master data & promo data as POS should read the article & promo info from the cloud.

Since the roving POS are not frequently used and when needed it might remain lower version, it is recommended to turn on the POS beforehand with a few hours with network connection, so that POS can start downloading the latest version and install the upgrade in the next reboot.

Upon successful store ID change, sales transactions conducted on this POS will be automatically synchronized with the store's POS server, eliminating the need for manual merging of data. (Pending BIR Confirm)

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

SM needs to apply moving the POS from HQ to certain store with BIR and waiting for approval.

In the meantime, SM can move the POS from HQ to the store physically. No POS server is needed.

Once it gets approval, SM just needs to find the roving POS from the device list in the Partner System (see 3.1.2 above), edit the POS info and change the store ID (and Terminal ID if the current terminal ID is not unique in the target store) from the HQ to the target store. Click Save.

Store members can then reboot the roving POS, and the store ID on the POS top status bar should show target store rather than HQ.

title: Function Design Solution > Installation & Start > Roving POS
content:
All sales of roving POS will be counted on the target store rather than HQ. (Follow BIR Requirement)

For multiple Roving POS, each POS needs to change the store ID individually.

Since Roving POS can only be used for 15 days max, after 15 days the roving POS store will be automatically reset to HQ.

BIR Relate – Below are the expected design but might adjusted as per BIR advice.

The store address and TIN number on POS receipt of roving POS will also shows the target store rather than HQ (Follow BIR Requirement)

Roving POS Terminal Z will also be count in the target store (Follow BIR Requirement)

Roving POS grant total sale won’t be impact, it should be the total of all sales among various stores.

Since the BIR Combine report is per POS but not per store, the report should remain the same unless it meets additional requirements. (Follow BIR Requirement)

5. Exceptional Scenario

If reboot the Roving POS and found the store ID remains the HQ, please check the network connection of the roving POS as the store change require network connection. Once the network is restored, rebooting roving POS can successfully fetch the updated store ID.

title: Function Design Solution > Installation & Start > Roving POS
content:
6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization

Today 10 POS devices and 1 POS server is a roving group | Since no POS server is needed, and each POS can be config easily, team only needs to manage the POS individually | Change from managing roving POS as a fixed group to managing roving POS individually with enough flexibility. | Store IT | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > User Management
content:
Prerequisite

DMALL implementation team will assist in creating all team member accounts and corresponding roles, permissions, etc. in the DMALL system during the UAT phase.

Additionally, the step-by-step tutorials about the user creation/deletion, roles creation, permission config on the DMALL backend Partner System will be included in the training phase.

title: Function Design Solution > User Management > Prerequisite - Cashier Auto-deactive
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

The legacy system today two significant concerns: the inability to delete cashier accounts from the system after their leaving, as well as the lack of effective tracking to determine whether the accounts of departed cashiers have been deactivated or invalidated.

2. TO-BE Overview

SM team has expressed a requirement to pre-configure the expiry date of user accounts based on different roles during user creation. For example, a cashier role may have a validity period of five months, after which the user will be automatically deactivated, and manual activation will be required to continue using the account.

Users in a deactivated state will not be able to log in to any platforms (including Partner System, POS, OS App, etc.), but their data will still be retained in the system.

3. TO-BE Flow

Not Applicable

4. TO-BE Description

Clearly define the roles required by the merchants and add an expiry date to the role configuration. Users can choose not to set an expiry date or set it on a monthly basis.

All newly created users under this role will be affected by this expiry date. After the expiry date, the user's status will automatically change to deactivated and manual activation will be required in the Partner System to continue logging in to the Partner System or POS.

This configuration is only used to deactivate users and will not delete the user. The action of deleting a user still needs to be performed manually in the Partner System.

5. Exceptional Scenario

When a user is already in a deactivated state and is manually activated, POS needs to be in the network connected state to synchronize the activation operation.

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

title: Function Design Solution > User Management > Prerequisite - Cashier Auto-deactive
content:
Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
Today cashier account cannot be deleted | Cashier account can be deleted from the system without impacting the existing data | New function to be able to delete the cashier account in the system. | Store IT | All companies in scope.
Today cashier account cannot be automatically disabled after a period. | Cashier account can be automatically disabled after a period as per config. | New function to automatically disable an account after a pre-defined period. | Store IT | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > User Management > Cashier Login/logout
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today the cashier is able to login via either manually inputting the account and password or scan a barcode on the employee card to quickly login. Also, the cashier is not allowed to log out until all suspended orders have been addressed.

2. TO-BE Overview

DMALL POS provides two login methods: account and password login or scanning the QR code of the DMALL OS App for login. Currently, SM will exclusively utilize the account and password login method, as the store team members are prohibited from using mobile phones. But the QR login function is there in case we need it in the future.

For logout, DMALL POS follows the as-is logic, the cashiers could log out when there are no ongoing sales transactions and no suspended orders.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

Page Screenshot | Function Description
POS Login Page | Login POS Upon launching the POS system, input the appropriate cashier account and POS login password into the designated fields on the login interface. Subsequently, click on the <login> button to proceed. While login, if there are suspended orders in the POS right now, then cashier is not allowed to login until he/she gets the supervisor authorization on POS. Log Out POS Once the cashier has successfully logged into the POS, he/she can access the system menu by clicking on the DMALL POS logo located in the upper left corner. Within the system menu, the cashier can initiate the logout process by selecting the "Logout" option. While logout, if there are unfinished suspended orders, cashier is not allowed to logout until he/she clear out all these suspended orders. Exit POS Program Once the cashier has logout and goes back to the login page, if he/she wants to exit the POS program, he/she proceeds to click on the <Exit> button located in the upper right corner to exit the POS program.

5. Exceptional Scenario

Cashiers should be able to login & logout if the POS has no network work connection as there is an offline database.

title: Function Design Solution > User Management > Cashier Login/logout
content:
6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

Key Changes

Today the cashier can login via scanning the manually printed barcode on the employee card. | No longer has the barcode on the employee card due to the security concern. Instead, other than using account & password, a QR Code login feature is provided in case SM needs it in the future. | Change the login approach of scanning the manually printed employee barcode with two login approaches: 1). Login with inputting account and password. 2). Login with scanning the dynamic QR code generated on the DMALL OS APP. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > User Management > Change password
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today the POS will automatically force reset the cashier password every 30 days.

2. TO-BE Overview

Moving to DMALL POS, the existing force reset password function remains and the force reset period is configurable from the backend. In addition, cashier password can also be manually updated on either the POS client or the Partner System.

Furthermore, for SM markets, to ensure the complexity of the password, the cashier password rule becomes 6-8 alphanumeric characters now instead of the pure numeric or pure alphabetic passwords.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

Page Screenshot | Function Description
Partner System Account Mgt Reset Password Entry Partner System Account Mgt Reset Password Form | Reset Password from Partner System Access the Partner System and navigate to the "Account Mgt" section. Locate the employee in the list and click on the <Reset Password> option. On the popup window, choose "POS" as the account type, proceed to enter the new password, and finalize the password reset by clicking on the <Confirm> button. Notes that resetting password from the partner system does not require the original password.
POS Client Change Password | POS Client Change Password Access the login page of the POS Client and select the option <Change Password>. Enter the account details, including the original password and the desired new password. Proceed by clicking on <Confirm> to successfully complete the password modification. Subsequently, utilize the newly updated password for logging into the POS.

5. Exceptional Scenario

Cashier is not able to update password on POS client when POS has no network connection. If he/she has to reset the password, it is suggested to go with the Partner System.

If cashier forgets his/her password, the supervisor can reset cashier login password with the Partner password reset approach.

title: Function Design Solution > User Management > Change password
content:
If the supervisor or store manager is on temporary leave, to meet the as-is handling, technically they can assign a person-on-duty for authorization via assigning the supervisor/store manager role to this user manually from the user management page of Partner System. But this operation is potentially risky and not recommended from the security perspective.

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
Today the cashier cannot update the password on his/her own. | DMALL POS allows cashiers to manually update the password on the POS Clients. | New feature to allow cashiers to update the password by themselves with the original password. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > User Management > Authorization
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Some functionalities in the POS system are only accessible with the function code that is managed by the supervisor. Cashier cannot see these functions but ask cashier to help.

2. TO-BE Overview

With DMALL POS, all functionalities within POS are visible to all cashiers and can be authorized based on user permissions. In the event that users require authorization to access certain functionalities, we offer two methods for granting authorization:

Input the user account and password of the authorizing personnel.

Scan the QR code of the authorizing personnel from the DMALL OS App (Please note that SM currently does not support this feature).

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

Page Screenshot | Function Description
POS Client Change Password | POS Authorization: When certain user attempts to perform a function on the POS that exceeds the permissions of the current login role, authorization must be done from a role that with the necessary permissions. This can be achieved through the following methods: Password-based authorization: Authorize access by inputting the account & password. Scan code authorization: Obtain authorization by scanning the personal QR code found on the DMALL OS App.
Add cashier manager role | Granting the Manager Role: Login to the Partner System and then navigate to "/Settings/Role Management" and select the option to "Add Role." Proceed to create the role of Cashier Store Manager and authorize the necessary functions accordingly.

5. Exceptional Scenario

Authorization can proceed even if the POS has no network connection.

If the authorization page keeps appearing even after supervisor input his/her account & password, it means this supervisor does not have the required permission. In this case, please check if the supervisor role has the necessary permission on this function or reach DMALL local implementation team for help.

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

title: Function Design Solution > User Management > Authorization
content:
Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
Today the advanced functions are managed with the function code that only supervisor knows. | DMALL POS has all functions that appear on the screen. Only verify the permission when user tries to access this function. | Change from supervisor hold the function code to a permission-based control. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > User Management > Lock/unlock
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today there is no lock up function but a “Temporary Leave” feature. Cashier who temporarily leaved needs to resume with his/her own password.

2. TO-BE Overview

DMALL POS offers various lock and unlock functionalities to effectively handle different scenarios. These include the following features or mechanisms:

Automatic machine locks after a specified duration of inactivity.

Manual lock for temporary absences.

Unlocking can be done using the original cashier’s account or supervisor’s account.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

Page Screenshot | Function Description
Manual locking machine Automatic Logout Countdown | Manual Machine Locking: Access the function entrance through the POS left sub menu. Alternatively, users can click the lock button in the middle function area or the right-hand side function area (check details on the 3.3 sales section) Click on "Confirm" on the popup confirm window to proceed to the machine lock page. Automatic Machine Locking: After the specified automatic lock duration, the POS will display the automatic machine lock page and 10 second countdown. If no action is taken within 10 seconds, the POS will automatically be locked. Both the auto login period and the countdown time are configurable from the Partner System.
Login Back After Lock Up | Unlock The account used for login prior to the machine lock remains the same and cannot be altered. To unlock the machine, enter the password and submit. This will redirect you to the page that was active before the machine was locked. The supervisor has permission to unlock any machine (to the original account)

5. Exceptional Scenario

Both lockup & unlock function can proceed even if POS has no network connection.

While POS is lockup, if the POS get force exited, once POS reboot, it should resume the lockup status.

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

title: Function Design Solution > User Management > Lock/unlock
content:
Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
Today cashier is able to use the “Temporary Leave” function to lock the POS manually. | DMALL POS provide both manually lockup function and automatically lockup function. | New function to lockup POS automatically after x mins no actions. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Sales page intro
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Current POS uses an old-fashioned interface for the sales & payment page with only the shopping cart shown on the screen. All the functionalities come from the physical keyboard.

2. TO-BE Overview

The Sales Page is a crucial component of the DMALL POS system. It provides a comprehensive overview of the current order, including the added articles, quantity, the total amount and a lot of functionalities. This page is designed to offer a user-friendly and efficient experience, allowing users to manage their shopping carts and complete transactions related functions seamlessly.

Additionally, it provides function areas that allow cashiers to perform multiple tasks on the screen.

3. TO-BE Flow

Not Applicable

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Sales page intro
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Sales Page Sidebar Menu Middle Functions Bar Shortcut Function Area | Below is the introduction of each component on the sales page following a top-to-bottom, left-to-right sequence. The sales page encompasses a designated shopping cart section, a centrally positioned functional bar, and a shortcut functions section on the right-hand side. Top Status Bar: The top status bar is located at the top of the page and displays essential information such as the store name, store code, POS code/cashier code, cashier's name, POS version and transaction number. On the right side, there are icons indicating the status of the printer, offline service, and network. If any of these icons turn gray with a red cross, it indicates an abnormal status. Sidebar Menu: By clicking on the "DMALL POS" icon on the top left corner, the left sidebar menu can be accessed, revealing a menu drawer. This menu drawer allows users to perform system settings and management operations like logout, EOD, POS Setting and switch to return mode, etc. Cart Total Display Area: Located in the middle of the top header bar. The cart total display area showcases the cumulative information of the added items in the cart. It includes the total quantity, total discount and the overall amount. Search Bar: The search bar is positioned on the top right corner of the shopping cart area. Users can enter keywords or barcodes to search for products available in the current store. Shopping Cart Section: The shopping cart section is situated on the left side of the page. It displays the products that have been added to the current order. Users can view the detailed information of each product, modify the quantity, or remove items from the cart. Middle Functions Bar: The middle function bar is located in the center of the page and is designed to showcase choose functionalities that are most often used, such as promotional activities or coupon usage. The exact functions displayed in this

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Sales page intro
content: area can be customized based on the client's requirements. Shortcut Function Area: The shortcut area is positioned on the right side of the page and displays the configured quick access products and function buttons. By switching the tab, users can quickly add items or perform operations here, such as adding products or applying discounts, by clicking on the shortcut products or buttons. The Sales Page aims to enhance operational efficiency and user experience by providing clear information display and intuitive interaction. With its user-friendly interface, users can easily manage their shopping carts and complete transactions efficiently.

content:

Sales page is the main page of the POS and it’s accessible even if POS has no network connection.

If POS realize that SI# cannot remain continued (like the local DB broken) the POS system will temporarily lock up the POS via prompting a warning message, and wait IT team to fix it before put POS back to sales.

content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today the POS can add the articles via scanning the item barcode or manually input the barcode.

2. TO-BE Overview

DMALL POS supports various approaches adding items to shopping carts. Specifically, here mentions the most frequently used approaches:

Scan To Add

Shortcut Product Flat Keys

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Adding article approaches
content:

Users can scan product barcodes or weight item barcodes using the "Handheld Scanner" or the vertical scanner that is embedded on the counter. title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Adding article approaches
content: First & Second Level Category (no image configured) First Level Category expanded mode PLU Items with image configured Product Details Page | Shortcut Products Flat Keys The right side of the sales page displays the configured shortcut products & shortcut functions. Clicking on the "PLU items" tab (if the default display is configured for shortcut products, no need to click) will display the first-level categories of shortcut products. The first-level categories are displayed in a maximum of two rows. If there are more categories, an "Expand Icon" will be displayed for the overflow (see screenshot "First Level Category expanded mode" for the "Expand" function) Selecting any first-level category will switch to the display of products under that category. Clicking on a product card will add the corresponding product to the main shopping cart.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Adding article approaches
content:
5. Exceptional Scenario

The POS system can still function and add items to the cart using a local offline database even when there is no internet connection. However, under normal circumstances, the POS system will have access to the cloud-based database by default to fetch the latest data.

If the POS system encounters an abnormal exit, power outage, or restart after items have been added to the cart, upon restarting and logging in again, it will restore the ongoing transaction and retrieve all the previously added items in the shopping cart.

MajorCon articles will appear in the shopping cart with the real article name. But moving to the order system, return or reports, it should use the MajorCon dummy code.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Article search
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Currently POS has no function to search article details include promo info.

2. TO-BE Overview

The sales page supports cashiers performing article searches. Users have the capability to search for an item on the POS by inputting either the item's barcode or a descriptive query in cases where the barcode is not readily scannable or legible.

The item search function also assists cashiers in effortlessly verifying product details and retrieving relevant promotional information in response to customer inquiries regarding product information.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Article search
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Search Button Soft Keyboard Fuzzy search needs at least two chars Search Result Page | By selecting the search button located in the upper right corner of the sales page, users can evoke the soft keyboard. They can then proceed with the following input methods: Inputting the full barcode In the case of regular items (non-weighted), if the cashier enters the complete and accurate barcode, the POS system will directly initiate a search for that specific item. After inputting the barcode, clicking the confirm button will initiate the search process. If a matching result is found, the product detail page will be displayed. If no matching result is found, the search result page will appear with a message indicating no results were found. Inputting the article name When the POS system is online, a fuzzy search function is available as long as the user inputs more than two characters. For example, if the user inputs "JA" or "BREAKFAST," the result "JAMIE BREAKFAST CEREAL" will be displayed. However, if the POS system is offline, the fuzzy search function is not available. In such cases, the user must input the full name or full barcode to search for the article. After inputting the search keywords, clicking the confirm button will initiate the search process. If matching results are found, the search result page will be displayed. The cashier can directly add the item to the cart by clicking on the product card or inspecting the product details. If no matching results are found, the search result page will appear with a message indicating no results were found.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Article search
content:
5. Exceptional Scenario

The article search function can be used by accessing the local offline database even when there is no internet connection. However, under normal circumstances, the POS system will have access to the cloud-based database by default to fetch the latest data.

MajorCon items can be searched here with this function

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Article price override
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today POS has price override function to change price up or down. Only when the override amount exceeds a certain threshold is authorization required.

2. TO-BE Overview

In scenarios where promotions are not applied, incorrect promotion, incorrect retail price or temporary promotions are needed, cashiers can modify the price of a single item line in the sale page to complete the transaction.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Article price override
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Item Price Override Entries Item Price Override Menu - Edit Price Item Price Override Menu - Edit Discount Item Price Override Reason Selection | Price Override Entries (Product must be added to the shopping cart first) Click on the "Unit Price" area of the Item line in the shopping cart Click on the "Item Price Override" button on the shortcut function area or the middle function bar (configurable) Price Override Menu Price override might need supervisor intervention depending on the current user role. If the current user is a cashier, then he will see an authorization page shows up and require the supervisor to input the account and password to authorize. If the current user has the permission to do price override already, like supervisor, then there is no need to authorize. There are two approaches for the price override: Edit Price (by default) - change the unit price directly Enter the modified unit price in the input box and then submit Below the input box is the section that shows allowed price range (based on the single item price modification range configured in the POS system) Edit Discount - change the unit price by inputting the percentage off Enter the discount percentage in the input box and submit (percentage of amount deducted) Below the input box is the section that shows allowed discount range (based on the single item price modification range configured in the POS system) After selecting the new price, there will be an extra step for the cashier to select a reason for the price modification and then submit it. The reasons option is configurable, needs to be provided before the SIT phase. Then the new price will take effect on that specific affected item line. Note: Only items eligible for price modification are allowed to cater to the item price override.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Article price override
content:
5. Exceptional Scenario

Some articles might not be able to apply price override if it has a “Forbid Price Override” attribute on the article master data.

Price Override can function normally even if POS has no network connection.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Order price override
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today POS only has item price override but no order level price override.

2. TO-BE Overview

Whenever the store needs it, the entire transaction can be adjusted using the order price override function.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Order price override
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Order Price Override Entries Order Price Override Menu - Edit Price Order Price Override Menu - Edit Discount Order Price Override Reason Selection | Order Price Override Entry (Products must be added to the shopping cart first) Middle Function Area (configurable) Shortcut Function Area (configurable) Price Override Menu Price override might need supervisor intervention depending on the current user role. If the current user is a cashier, then he will see an authorization page shows up and require the supervisor to input the account and password to authorize. If the current user has the permission to do price override already, like supervisor, then there is no need to authorize. There are two approaches for the price override: Edit Price (by default) - change the total order amount directly Enter the modified total order amount in the input box and submit. Edit Discount - change the total order amount by inputting the percentage off Enter the percentage discount in the input box and submit (percentage of amount deduction). The modified order amount is based on the total amount after applying all promotion and coupons. After selecting the new price, there will be an extra step for the cashier to select a reason for the price modification and then submit it. The reasons option is configurable, needs to be provided before the SIT phase. After applying the order price override, the cashier will land on the payment page directly with the updated price. Note: Only items eligible for price modification are allowed to cater to the item price override. Once applied order price override, the new amount will be carried out with a discount, and all the discount amounts will be allocated to all eligible items per rate.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Subtotal (Price Calculation)
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today POS uses subtotal as the final step to calculate the order price and take payment accordingly. Once subtotal the shopping cart cannot be further edited.

2. TO-BE Overview

In the DMALL POS, the subtotal function calculates the pricing of articles in the shopping cart based on promotional activities and other types of discounts (like government sales). It can also provide the details of various discount amounts and the total amount except for the discount.

Most importantly, the subtotal is not mandatory in the transaction. It is okay to not manually trigger the subtotal as the final order submission will automatically trigger subtotal.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

Page Screenshot | Function Description
Subtotal Function Entry The Total Amount & Promotion Area Get Updated | Users can add articles to the shopping cart, and the total amount displayed is the sum of prices after applying unit promotions, excluding other discounts. To update the shopping cart and view adjusted prices, click on the "Subtotal" button in the middle function area. Upon clicking, the shopping cart will automatically refresh, reflecting the updated total amount and individual item prices that applied all eligible promotions and discounts, including government sales discounts. After applying the subtotal, cashiers can also view the updated order-level promotions that the current order qualifies for in the bottom left corner of the shopping cart. Note: Manual subtotal is not always needed. Once you click the submit button to enter the payment page, POS will automatically run a subtotal calculation to get the final price.

5. Exceptional Scenario

Subtotal function can function normally even if there Is no internet connection.

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Subtotal (Price Calculation)
content:
Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
Today POS used subtotal as the final order submission. | DMALL POS uses subtotal as the optional action to check the current shopping cart price. The order can still be editable after subtotal. | Changing the meaning of subtotal from the final order submission to an optional operation that can be performed anytime on the sales page to check the current order price. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Tax calculation
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today the POS automatically calculate the Vatable sales, VAT, VAT Exempt Sales and Zero Rate (ZR is always 0 today as it’s manually calculated)

2. TO-BE Overview

The DMALL POS system can automatically calculate the pre-tax price (Vatable Sales), tax amount (VAT), and post-tax price for each individual item and the entire order based on the tax code specified in the article master data.

All prices displayed on the POS interface are post-tax prices, and cashiers do not need to perform any additional tax calculation related operations.

The pre-tax price, tax amount, and post-tax price of the order will be reflected on the receipt and in the backend order management system.

3. TO-BE Flow

Not Applicable

4. TO-BE Description

For transactions in the Philippines, articles are divided into two categories: Vatable and VAT Exempt. VAT can be set at a tax rate of 12% (the tax rate can be configured in the tax calculation system based on government requirements).

For special types of transactions that involved extra tax calculation, such as senior citizen discounts, please refer to the relevant section on government sales in chapter 3.3.13 for the specific tax calculation logic.

5. Exceptional Scenario

Tax Calculation can function normally even if POS has no internet connection.

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
Today POS can perform tax calculation for Vatable sale, VAT, and VAT Exempt. But not zero-rate amount. | DMALL POS provides all existing tax calculation in both online/offline and also calculate zero-rate automatically. | On top of the existing tax calculation, make the zero-rate amount calculation automatically. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Suspend/recall order
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today POS can perform order suspend and order recall with a suspend receipt. Also, before EOS all suspended orders must be resolved.

2. TO-BE Overview

If an order does not need to be processed immediately, or a customer request to finish it later, it can be suspended and be recalled later for payment.

Suspended orders can be recalled from any POS in the same store if the POS has a network connection.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Suspend/recall order
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Suspend Order Function Entry | Suspend Order When there are articles in the shopping cart, select the "Suspend Order" button to suspend order Suspend order function can be configured to require supervisor intervention as per needed After suspending the current order, the current shopping cart will be cleared out and start the next transaction After putting an order on suspend, the "Recall Order" button will show the number of orders currently on suspend. Note One POS can suspend max 99 orders at the same time. Cashiers cannot log out with unfinished suspended orders.
Recall Function Button Entry Recall Order Menu Check Suspended Order Details | Recall Order On the sales page, when there are orders suspended, a small red circle will appear on the top right corner of the "Recall Order" button, indicating the number of orders suspended. Clicking on the "Recall Order" button. If there is only one suspended order, clicking this button will recall that order to the shopping cart directly. If there is more than one suspended order, then the recall order menu with a list of suspended orders appears. The recall order menu contains all the orders that have been suspended currently. Cashiers can view the basic info of different orders in the right-side drawer, includes Suspend data & time The number of articles for this order Subtotal Click on the [Details] button to expand and view the list of articles included in the selected suspended order. Click on [Recall] to restore the selected order to the shopping cart. Note The cashier is only able to recall an order when the current shopping cart is empty. The cashier cannot log out or cater end of shift (pre-commit) if there are unfinished suspended order (this restriction is configurable as per need) To clear out the suspended order, cashier can recall them all and then void the transactions and put all suspended items to the CS personnel

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Suspend/recall order
content:
5. Exceptional Scenario

With network connection, the POS allows order suspension and recall within the same store across different terminals. For instance, if there are two POS terminals, A and B, within the same store, an order can be suspended on terminal A and recalled on terminal B.

However, when the POS is unable to connect to the internet, it can still support order suspension and recall, but only within the same terminal. In this case, it is not possible to recall orders across different terminals within the same store.

If POS has been force exited, then next time login will require supervisor intervention if there are unfinished suspended orders. Once authorized, all suspended orders remain in the terminal.

6. Related Reports

Suspend Order Details

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Item Void & Cancel order
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today POS provides three functions “Item Void”, “Line Void” and “Sale Cancel” for voiding item or voiding transactions.

2. TO-BE Overview

In DMALL POS, if a customer no longer needs an item that has been added to the shopping cart, or cashier accidently adds an unexpected item in the cart, this item can be void with supervisor intervention.

Similarly, during the sales process, if a customer decides not to purchase this order anymore (without having made a payment), the current order can also be cancelled without impacting following finance or accounting records.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Item Void & Cancel order
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Popup To Confirm Void Item Reason Codes for Item Void Deleted Item lines Grey Out with Deleted Indicator | Void Item Line There are two ways of voiding an item line Select the item line and then click the "Delete Item" button on the function area Reduce the quantity of the item line to 0. Item void might need supervisor intervention depending on the current user role. If the current user is a cashier, then he will see an authorization page shows up and require the supervisor to input the account and password to authorize. If the current user has the permission to do item void already, like supervisor, then there is no need to authorize. A confirmation popup will appear asking if confirm to delete item. Clicking "Cancel" will close the popup and not void item line Upon clicking "Confirm," a drawer will appear to select the reason for void (reason options are configurable). Once a deletion reason is chosen, the item will grey out from the shopping cart with an indicator "deleted" Clicking on "Subtotal" or "Submit" will permanently remove the deleted item lines from the shopping cart. Sales receipt or invoice will not include the deleted item lines.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Item Void & Cancel order
content: Popup To Confirm Void Order Reason Codes for Order Void | Cancel Order When there are items in the shopping cart, and the customer has not made a payment yet, clicking the "Cancel Order" button will initiate the cancel order process. Cancel order might need supervisor intervention depending on the current user role. If the current user is a cashier, then he will see an authorization page shows up and require the supervisor to input the account and password to authorize. If the current user has the permission to do cancel order already, like supervisor, then there is no need to authorize. A confirmation popup will appear asking if confirm to cancel order. Clicking "Cancel" will close the popup and not void order Upon clicking "Confirm," a drawer will appear to select the reason for the cancellation (reason options are configurable). Voiding order not only clears out all items in the shopping cart but also removes all associated customer information, such as membership validation and coupons.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Value Pack
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today once value pack has been sold from POS, store member has to manually deduct the inventory for each component.

2. TO-BE Overview

Value Pack is a unique type of promotional product offered by SM Market. It typically consists of a combination of several items offered at a more advantageous price as a Value Pack.

Value Pack items are exclusively available for purchase by SMAC members, and there is a specified limit on the quantity of Value Packs that can be purchased in a single order.

3. TO-BE Flow

E2E Flow

POS Operation Flow

4. TO-BE Description

E2E Flow

SAP should create the dummy value pack item in the article master data and include the value pack tag in the article description.

The SAP / SM team should establish the Bill of Materials (BOM) relationship between the components and the dummy value pack item in the DMALL merchandise system. This will ensure proper deduction of stock. Refer to the master data blueprint for more information.

Cashiers can add the Value Pack item to the POS following the specified workflow and restrictions below and complete the payment accordingly.

Once the payment is made, DMALL will generate the corresponding order in the DMALL Order Management System (OMS) and deduct the inventory based on the BOM configuration.

Finally, DMALL OS will transmit the sales details back to SAP through regular data posting procedures.

POS Flow

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Value Pack
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Popup Notifying Only SMAC Member Can Purchase Value Pack Popup Notifying Cannot Purchase More Value Pack Item Over Max Quantity Limit | Prerequisites: SM needs to tag Value Pack items in the SAP article master data. DMALL POS provides backend configuration to set the maximum quantity of Value Pack items that can be purchased in a single order. When a cashier adds a Value Pack item to the cart, the POS system will check if the customer is currently validated in as a SMAC member. If not logged in, a pop-up window will appear, indicating that only members can purchase this type of item. Clicking on "Validate Member" will proceed to the SMAC membership validation process. Clicking "Cancel" will cancel the addition of the Value Pack item. If the customer is already validated as a SMAC member, the POS system will check if the number of Value Pack items in the shopping cart exceeds the maximum allowable quantity per order. If it exceeds the limit, a pop-up window will appear, notifying the user that the maximum quantity has been exceeded. The user can click "Confirm" to close the pop-up window. After the order is completed, the system will automatically deduct the stock of the individual components of the Value Pack items.

content:
5. Exceptional Scenario
