DMALL POS will automatically reduce it with a valid BOM relation. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Online Sales Picking
content:
In the current SM system, it is necessary to synchronize orders from the Magento system to the POS system for Online Sales and generate printed receipts through the POS system and count online sales amounts under offline store sales.

However, with the transition to the DMALL system, the Online Sales picking process will no longer involve the POS client. Instead, Magento system will sync the online orders directly to the DMALL POS order system and send the receipt to customer via email. The detailed Online Sales picking process can be found in the Picking module blueprint.

Please also note that this solution will require final approval from BIR. Therefore, prior to BIR's confirmation, this solution may be subject to modifications based on their recommendations or requirements.

Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
Today, online POS will have to go through POS eventually to adjust price, identify online sale source, and print receipt. | Magento will sync order info to DMALL automatically without going through POS client. The sale receipt shall be sent to the client via email. | Remove the POS client process during the online sales. All online order info will be synced to POS order system automatically without scanning items on the POS client. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Pre-order
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today there are two types of pre-orders for the SM:

Normal pre-order for the customer: the customer will pay for a deposit article for a certain amount (determined by the cashier). Then for the 2nd transaction, the cashier needs to present the deposit sale receipt so that the deposit amount will be adjusted accordingly. In this way, the deposit amount has to be used fully in the 2nd transaction, and no change for the exceed deposit amount in the 2nd transaction.

Pre-order for corporation: Sometimes the corporate prefers to deposit a certain amount with the Treasury office and then use a deposit for multiple transactions.

Note: Deposit should just be a part of the whole pre-order business. After the deposit, the store needs to prepare the article ordering, inventory lock later.

2. TO-BE Overview

Pre Order is a type of transaction currently offered by SM Market. Customers have the option to prepay a deposit for items that are not yet in stock. Upon arrival of the items, customers can use their deposit ticket to prioritize their purchase eligibility and offset the payment with the prepaid deposit.

Usually there are three approaches for the pre-order scenario:

(SM AS-IS) The POS system adds a negative service item to deduct the deposit amount.
Not recommended as negative service items are not commonly used, especially from a financial perspective. Additionally, it does not accurately reflect the pre-order business on the system level, as shopping carts should only include articles or services that are purchased by the customer.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Pre-order
content:
Finance should create a new type of order called "Deposit Order". Deposit orders should not be considered as sales since the income is not technically realized until the customer pays the remaining amount. When a customer uses their deposit to settle the second order, the deposit order will be automatically deducted to maintain balance between the second order and the deposit payment.
This approach should be the best practice, but it needs a lot of modification from the finance side and impacts the reconciling process. It's recommended to apply this solution once SM adopt the DMALL ERP system and uses the DMALL finance module.

Based on SM's current situation and best practices, it is recommended to simplify the second option for pre-order transactions. Pre-paid orders will be processed as special sales orders (not post to BIR) with an additional indication as pre-paid orders. When customers use their deposit to pay for the second order, the deposit amount will be deducted as part of the payment, and the payment will be associated with the pre-paid order.
The advantage of this approach is that it provides a clear and intuitive process for both cashiers and customers. Furthermore, this approach does not require any modifications from the finance department, making it more feasible.

For the two types of the Pre-order in SM, we recommend following solutions:

Normal pre-order for the customer:

The first transaction to collect deposit will be recorded as a service transaction. The deposit item has to be sold solely without mixing with other regular items. And the deposit amount is manually inputted by the cashier. Eventually, the customer will get a deposit receipt with a QR code on it.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Pre-order
content:
This transaction will not be posted to the BIR but kept in the SAP posting

For the second transaction, the cashier needs to add all the items in the shopping cart first and then use a “deposit” tender to collect the deposit paid before. Deposit tender requires scan the QR code on the deposit receipt so it can read the deposit amount automatically.

Pre-order for corporation:

Since this is a recent release feature without any impact on the POS client, it is recommend keeping the current flow: to let the Treasury office issue this kind of deposit and then redeem it with a designated tender “Corporate Sale”. The cashier can determine the redemption amount and record it manually.

3. TO-BE Flow

Since the pre-order for corporation will follow the as-is solution, the to-be flow and description will only focus on the pre-order for customers.

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Pre-order
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Pre-order Button Entry Input Amount for Deposit Deposit Ticket Example | Pre-order The cashier selects the Pre-order button in the functions area, which prompts them to enter the amount of the Pre-Order deposit. Pre-order function can be configured to require supervisor intervention as per needed If there are remaining item(s) in the shopping cart, a popup will appear stating "Pre-order cannot be used with items in the shopping cart. Please clear the shopping cart to proceed with the pre-order." After inputting and confirming the amount, the cashier proceeds to the payment page directly to complete the purchase of the deposit. Once the purchase is completed, the POS prints out a Deposit Ticket (with a barcode on it) indicating the store and the amount pre-paid. This pre-paid order will be counted as deposit and will not be posted to the BIR (but will be posted to the SAP).

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Pre-order
content: Deposit Tender on the Payment Page Cashier Scan Deposit Ticket Barcode to Get Amount After Successfully Use a Deposit | Using Deposit When the customer wants to redeem the deposit, they need to show the Deposit Ticket to the cashier and add the desired items to the shopping cart. The cashier clicks on "Submit" to proceed to the payment page. In the payment options, the cashier selects "Deposit" and scans the QR code on the deposit ticket or manually enters the barcode. The POS system will automatically recognize the deposit amount and complete this deposit payment (the deposit recognition function is still available when the POS is offline, but the redemption record of the deposit ticket will be synchronized to the cloud after the POS is connected to the internet). If the Deposit Amount exceeds the remaining payment amount, a pop-up appears to indicate that the deposit amount cannot be greater than the remaining payment amount, and the payment fails. After successfully paying the deposit, the customer can choose another tender to complete the order and print the receipt. The added deposit record can also be void via clicking the cross after the payment record if the customer does not want to use the deposit anymore. Once the order is completed, the POS system will automatically link the deposit payment record to the initial pre-order and conduct a return for the original pre-order to ensure financial balance. A designated return reason code will be applied to this type of automatic deposit order return. Example: The customer pre-paid 100 pesos, and the pre-paid order ID is 1001 for example. The customer returns to the store to make a second order for 2000 pesos. After scanning the deposit ticket, a deposit payment record for 100 pesos will be generated and automatically associated with order 1001. The customer pays the remaining 1900 pesos using another payment method (e.g., cash), and the order is completed, let's say the order ID for the second order is 1002. The

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Pre-order
content: POS system will automatically process a refund for order 1001, with the refund order number being 9001. In total, this pre-order process triggers three transactions: Sale of 100 pesos for order 1001 Sale of 2000 pesos for order 1002 Refund of 100 pesos for order 9001

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Pre-order
content:
5. Exceptional Scenario

“Deposit” tender must be paid in full, otherwise POS will prompt an error message to indicate deposit cannot be used partially.

Both pre-order and the deposit adjustment can proceed even if POS has no network connection.

Please note that the deposit receipt can be used in any store if there are no further restrictions.

When deposit adjustment, the deposit amount is manually inputted from cashier and it’s not safe. | POS will read the deposit amount automatically during the adjustment phase to prevent cashier cheating. | Change the deposit adjustment flow from manually input deposit amount to scan deposit receipt QR code and let system recognize the amount automatically. | Cashier | All companies in scope.
When deposit adjustment, cashier will use a negative article to deduct the deposit amount. | POS will treat deposit as a regular tender and perform an automatic return on the original deposit order. | Removed the negative article approach and make the deposit adjustment as a tender and perform an automatic return on the original deposit order. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Govt Deduction
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today all govt deductions are not coming from system calculation but from offline manual calculation, which brings a lot of burden to the cashiers and risks for inaccurate calculation.

2. TO-BE Overview

Govn Deduction refers to several special transaction types defined by the government for different groups of people/companies.

When the cashier confirms offline that the customer qualifies for the mandatory deduction, they need to enter the relevant information in the POS system. This allows the POS system to automatically calculate the correct discount or tax exemption amount based on specific rules.

Govn Sales includes the following types:

TWA: TOP WITHHOLDING AGENTS

GOCC: GOVERNMENT OWNED AND CONTROLLED CORPORATIONS

ZR: ZERO RATED

SCD: SENIOR CITIZEN DISCOUNT

PWD: PERSON WITH DISABILITY DISCOUNT

NAC: NATIONAL ATHLETES AND COACHES DISCOUNT

SP: SOLO PARENT DISCOUNT

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Govt Deduction
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Govn Sales Entry Govn Sales Sub-menu Certain Sale Types Require Additional Info Govt Sales Payment Be Automatically Added | The cashier confirms offline that the current customer qualifies for the Govn Sale requirements (e.g., senior citizen or government enterprise). After confirming the identity, the cashier clicks the "Govn Deduction" button in the function area of the sales page and selects the corresponding sales type from the submenu. Based on the selected sales type, the cashier needs to enter relevant information, including: For TWA & GOCC, the cashier needs to input the company name, address, TIN# and the business style. For SCD, the cashier needs to input the senior citizen's Name, OSCA ID#, remaining balance for the current week, etc. For PWD, the user needs to input the Name, PWD ID#, remaining balance for the current week, etc. For NAC and Solo Parent, the cashier needs to input the NAC Name and ID#. To reduce queue pressure at the store's POS, the information entered here is only necessary for calculating the deduction amount and not for the detailed information required for invoices. Therefore, deduction types such as TWA and GOCC do not require any information to be entered into the POS system at this stage. Detailed information required for invoices can be found in the invoice process described in section 3.3.15. After the confirmation is complete, the POS system will automatically execute the Subtotal function and recalculate the amount of the current order after regular promotions and government-mandated deductions. At this point, the customer can continue adding items, but the promotion amount and government-mandated deduction amount will not be recalculated until the next time the customer manually clicks Subtotal or submits the order. The user clicks Submit and enters the payment page where the payment amount is the amount after regular promotions and government-mandated deductions. The tax withholding and

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Govt Deduction
content: the operation cost will be automatically added as the form of tender. After payment is complete, the customer can choose to print a receipt or an invoice. The invoice process is described in section 3.3.15. If the user chooses to print a receipt, the receipt will display the required tax calculation information and relevant fields that the customer needs to manually fill in, such as address and TIN (Tax Identification Number). Other than the sale receipt, a separate govt deduction receipt is also needed to print out after the sales receipt.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Govt Deduction
content:
Special Notes

TWA: TOP WITHHOLDING AGENTS

TWA only enjoy 1% off for total sales exclude VAT, which will be recorded as an automatic tax withholding tender named “1% CWT”

GOCC: Government Owned and Controlled Corporations

GOCC enjoys two deductions:

1% off for total sales excluding VAT, record as an automatic tax withholding tender named “1% CWT”

5% off for vatable sales excluding VAT, record as an automatic tax withholding tender named “5% WVAT”

ZR: ZERO RATED

ZR enjoys 12% VAT off

The ZR transaction has a VAT tax of 0%, so no tender or discount needed.

SC & PWD: SENIOR CITIZEN DISCOUNT & PERSON WITH DISABILITY DISCOUNT

SC/PWD enjoys two discounts:

5% on Prime Commodities and Agricultural Articles, which will be recorded as a discount.

20% on Ready to Eat or Medicine items, which will be recorded as an automatic operation cost tender named “20% SC/PWD”. The max quantity for the eligible item is one per transaction.

20% on Ready to Eat item shall apply the Meal portion, which should come from the article master data.

VAT EXEMPTION will be recorded as 0% VAT on the RTE items

SCD/PWD 5% discount subjects to the “remaining balance” input by the customer. The total 5% SC discount amount should not exceed P65 per week (discountable amount should not exceed P1300 per week).

e.g., customer input remaining balance is P500

Customer purchase P5000 SC items

The customer can only get 500 * 5% = P25

NAC: NATIONAL ATHLETES AND COACHES DISCOUNT

NAC enjoys 20% on Ready to Eat items, which will be recorded as an automatic operation cost tender named “20% NAC”. The max quantity for the eligible item is one per transaction.

SP: SOLO PARENT DISCOUNT

SP enjoys 10% on selected Milk Products, Food Supplements, Medicines and Diapers for Children, which will be recorded as an automatic operation cost tender named “10% SP”

VAT EXEMPTION will be recorded as 0% VAT on the applicable items

Above design and conclusion may vary based on the BIR advice.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Govt Deduction
content:
5. Exceptional Scenario

All government deduction transaction and corresponding tax calculation are available even if POS has no network connection

When return the govt deduction transaction, the quota is not returned

The BIR document has a rule to give the SCD/PWD 20% best offer logic compared to the regular promotion. It is confirmed with SM team that RTE and MED item should not have regular promo in general. If it accidentally has the regular promo, the system should stack the 20% SCD/PWD discount.

If the customer didn’t present the identification on the purchase, but wants to enjoy the govt. deduction later, it is recommended for them to do a return transaction and then place an exact same order with the govt. deduction.

6. Related Reports

Sales Summary Report

Senior Citizen Sales Report

Persons with Disability Sales Report

National Athletes and Coaches Sales Report

Solo Parent Sales Report

Each Govt Deduction order needs two transactions (sale + a return) | Each Govt Deduction order only needs one sale transaction like any other order. | The govt deduction order process changes from two transactions to one single sales transaction. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Shopping Bag
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today the cashier will have to add the free shopping bag to the cart as per request, but these shopping bags will not appear on the sale receipt or BIR files, but the ops team should be able to view the bag usage from the report.

2. TO-BE Overview

DMALL POS typically treats shopping bags as regular articles, but in the current state of SM Market, shopping bags are not charged for and are not displayed as articles in the order and receipt. Therefore, we need to handle shopping bags differently.

Cashiers can choose to add different types of shopping bags, but the shopping bags will be treated as additional supplementary information for the order, rather than product information. This allows users to observe the usage of shopping bags in backend reports.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Shopping Bag
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Add Shopping Bag Entry Add Shopping Bag Menu (Bag Info Configurable) Total Bag Quantity Appear in Red Dot Popup to remind adding shopping bag | Preparations: The POS system will provide a backend configuration field to add different types of free shopping bags and their corresponding item codes. Operation Flow Cashiers click on the "Add Shopping Bag" button in the function area. The POS system opens a side drawer, displaying different types of shopping bags based on the backend configuration, along with a stepper. Cashiers can adjust the quantity of shopping bags using the stepper. After confirmation, the quantity of shopping bags will be displayed in a small red dot on the top-right corner of the "Add Shopping Bag" button. The cashier is able to update the shopping bag quantity anytime by clicking the "Add Shopping Bag" button After completing the order, the receipt or invoice will not display shopping bag information. However, sales information for shopping bags can be viewed in backend reports. Supplemental Logic When the cashier first clicks the "Submit" button to submit an order that does not contain any plastic bag items, a prompt window will appear asking if they would like to add plastic bags. If the cashier confirms, they will be directed to the adding plastic bag side drawer. Please note that this reminder is only triggered during the initial order submission and will only be prompted once per order at the most.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Shopping Bag
content:
5. Exceptional Scenario

If no plastic bag items have been configured, clicking on "add plastic bag" will display a prompt window indicating that there are currently no available plastic bag items.

If the configured plastic bag items cannot be found in the master data, clicking on "add plastic bag" will display a prompt window indicating that the item cannot be found.

The functionality to add plastic bags is still available even when the POS has no network connection.

POS Real-time Sales

Only one shopping bag can be used in this case. | DMALL POS allow configure multiple types of free shopping bags | Add more flexibility to have various types of free shopping bag for customers. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Sales Invoice
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today if customer needs to have manual invoice, they need to reach out the CS to manually fill an invoice and then void the original transaction in the POS. So, it involves three steps: finish sale transaction, manually fill invoice, and then void the original sales invoice.

2. TO-BE Overview

SM Market / BIR mandates that customers have the option to request an invoice instead of a sales receipt. Therefore, the POS system needs to support the input, generation, and printing of invoices.

This new invoicing process is designed to simplify store workflows and minimize errors resulting from manual operations.

Please also note that this solution will require final approval from BIR. Therefore, prior to BIR's confirmation, this solution may be subject to modifications based on their recommendations or requirements.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

Prerequisite: DMALL will try to follow the current SM invoice format and style unless BIR has concerns or recommendation in terms of the invoice format and style.

After completing the sale and making the payment, the POS system will print the sale receipt with a barcode on it.

If the customer needs the manual invoice, the customer or cashier will go to Customer Service to request the printing of the invoice.

The Customer Service staff will log in to the DMALL Partner system's invoice page, scan the receipt barcode or manually enter the receipt barcode, and request the customer to provide the necessary information for the specific type of sales invoice.

Once all the information is entered, the system will automatically generate a PDF-format invoice for downloading. The staff can then use a standard A4 printer to print the invoice.

The serial number of the sale receipt # and invoice # is still pending on the BIR confirmation. Ideally the receipt # should be the same as the invoice number.

5. Exceptional Scenario

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Sales Invoice
content:
As long as the sale receipt has been printed out, the customer should be able to print out the sale invoice from the partner system.

Since the partner system is an online web portal, it requires the CS counter to have network connection to be able to access the partner system.

If POS is offline, the invoice portal might not be able to find the transaction in time as the transaction is not uploaded yet. In this case, it is recommended to generate the invoice the next day and email the customer offline.

There is no time limit to request a sale invoice technically but should store operation set the SOP to limit the max period that customer can request an invoice.

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable.

7. Key Changes

Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
The cashier needs to manually fill in the manual invoice. | Cashier can input the necessary info to the system rather than manually write it. | Change the way cashiers collect customer information from handwriting to input to the system. | Cashier | All companies in scope.
Cashier needs to make an extra void transaction on the POS after creating manual invoice. | Cashier no need to do the extra void transaction. | Remove the unnecessary step to have the cashier void the sale transaction after creating the manual invoice. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Bagger ID
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today the cashier needs to manually determine whether a transaction needs bagger ID input, and then manually click the bagger key and scan the bagger ID.

2. TO-BE Overview

SM Market requires the entry of Bagger information for orders that meet a certain threshold amount, so that management can track the efficiency of baggers.

Therefore, in the DMALL POS system, after the cashier submits the order for the first time, if it is determined that the current order amount meets the threshold for inputting bagger information, the cashier will be automatically prompted to enter the bagger ID and cannot skip.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

Page Screenshot | Function Description
Prompt To Input Bagger ID | Prerequisite The POS backend will provide a configuration option to specify the threshold amount for orders that require bagger information (default: 1000 pesos). The POS backend will provide a configuration option to disable/enable the bagger # input feature. While disable the bagger # input feature, the following process will not be applied. POS Operation When the cashier completes adding the items and clicks "Submit" for the first time, the POS system will automatically determine whether the current order exceeds the configured amount. If the total amount of the order is less than the configured amount, the Bagger ID entry step will be skipped. If the total amount of the order exceeds the configured amount, a bagger ID entry page will automatically pop up. The cashier scans the Bagger ID to complete the entry (or manually enters the ID and clicks "Confirm" to complete the entry). The Bagger ID will be displayed on the sales receipt and can also be viewed in the backend for sales summaries of baggers.

5. Exceptional Scenario

The bagger number input feature should function well even if POS has no network connection.

The mentioned configuration change will take effect after the POS reboot.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Bagger ID
content:
If the POS gets force exited after capturing the bagger ID, once POS gets rebooted, the original transaction and the corresponding bagger ID should be restored automatically.

6. Related Reports

POS Real-time Sales

7. Key Changes

Key Changes

Cashier has to manually determine if the transaction needs a bagger. | POS will automatically determine if the transaction needs a bagger and force the cashier to input a bagger ID. | Change bagger ID capture logic from cashier manually determine to system automatically determine and automatically alter cashier to input the bagger ID. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Cashier Alert (confirm no need in 1A)
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today there is no cashier alert function in legacy POS.

2. TO-BE Overview

Cashier Alert is a feature that automatically checks for any predefined promotions when a cashier submits an order. It identifies any promotions that nearly meet the criteria but have not yet been triggered and alerts the cashier to add the relevant products to maximize the benefits for customers.

This version of Cashier Alert supports promotion types of the "Buy X Get Y" or "Buy X Amount Get Y". These promotions involve giving a gift when a certain spending threshold is met or when specific products are purchased. The alert will be triggered when the promotion conditions are met but the gift items have not been added to the cart.

Please also note this function is out of the RFP scope for 1A and might need further discussion.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Sales > Cashier Alert (confirm no need in 1A)
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Cashier Alert Popup Second Confirmation Prompt for Missing Free Gift | The Cashier Alert feature in this version does not require any additional configuration. However, if support for more types of promotions is needed, additional alert configurations will be required. When the cashier adds items and clicks on "Submit," the POS system will automatically execute the Cashier Alert logic. If there are no alerts that meet the conditions for the order, the process continues to the payment page without any additional steps. If there are alerts that meet the conditions, a pop-up window will display all the unfilled gift promotions. It will show the promotion slogan, description, and the specific gift items and quantities that are missing. The user can return to the cart to add the gift items and resubmit the order. Each time an order is submitted, the Cashier Alert will be rechecked. If the user ignores the Cashier Alert, they can choose to continue submitting the order, but a second pop-up confirmation will appear to ask if they want to proceed without giving the customer the gift.

5. Exceptional Scenario

The cashier alert can function normally even if the POS has no network connection.

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > Promotion Trigger
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today POS can trigger promotion by clicking the subtotal or scanning certain offline promo barcode.

2. TO-BE Overview

Moving to DMALL, during the process of adding items to the cart, the total amount of the order is the direct sum of the unit discounted prices of each item.

When the cashier manually clicks "Subtotal," the current order will be recalculated to reflect all applicable promotions and deductions (including government-mandated deductions). The system will then display a summary of the applied promotions at the bottom of the Sales Page.

In addition, cashiers can also trigger certain manual promotion by manually scanning a promotion barcode.

3. TO-BE Flow

Not Applicable

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > Promotion Trigger
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Promotion Info on Sales Page (Promotal Alert) Promotion Info on Sales Page (After Subtotal) Expand Top Discount Field (After Subtotal) | Unit Promotion When adding items, the POS system will check if each item has any unit promotions and calculate the total amount using the price after unit discount. Item lines that trigger unit item promotions will be marked with a green "%" symbol in their respective item lines. Promotion Alert When a specific item line is selected in the shopping cart, the Promotion Details area at the bottom of the sales page (on the right-hand side of the Submit button) will display a Promotion Alert, indicating all the promotions that this item line is potentially eligible for. By default, up to three promotions will be displayed in the green area, and if there are more, the cashier can click on the green area to expand and view more promotions. It is important to understand that the displayed promotion alert are not the ones that have been triggered for current order, but rather the ones that can be triggered by the selected item potentially. By selecting different item lines, the promotion alert will change accordingly. Subtotal After clicking subtotal, the entire order amount will be recalculated with all triggered promotion, coupon and govn sales deduction. In terms of the UI change, the "Discount" field at the top and the Promotion Details at the bottom will also be updated. Top Discount Field: Displays the total discount amount for the current order, including promotions, coupons, and government-mandated deductions. Clicking on the Discount field will provide a detailed breakdown of the discount amounts. Bottom Promotion Details After subtotaling, no individual item lines in the shopping cart will be selected. At this point, a summary of all promotional types applicable to the current order will be displayed in the promotion details section, including the cumulative amount of discounts for unit item

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > Promotion Trigger
content: promotions, conditional promotions, transaction promotions, and so forth. Manual Promotion Cashiers can apply manual promotion by scanning certain promotion barcodes offline, to handle some flexible promotion, like BDO Payment Promo, etc. Store members can print out promo barcode offline, when cashier needs to apply manual promo to the customer, they can simply scan this barcode in the sales page and POS will automatically execute subtotal to get the latest pricing.

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > Promotion Trigger
content:
5. Exceptional Scenario

Trouble Shoot Suggestion

Before the new version or new promotion type is released, SM IT team can fully test it with virtual store in the UAT environment.

If the expected promotion is not triggered even after subtotal, it’s suggested to check the related article with the “Article Search (3.3.3)" function and check if the expected promotion is there or not.

If the expected promotion appears in the article details, it means the promotion data is there, please double check if the promotion threshold is met.

If the expected promotion does not appear in the article details, it means the promotion is not created or synced properly in the DMALL promotion engine, please double check with the team who created this promotion or reach the DMALL IT Team.

Promotion can be triggered with the offline database if POS has no network connection. But by default, it will request the cloud to fetch the latest promotion info.

6. Related Reports

Promotion Performance

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > SMAC login
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today, cashiers can validate the SMAC member by selecting the SMAC button on the keyboard and then swipe card / scan virtual QR code / manually input member ID to login the membership.

2. TO-BE Overview

SMAC is the membership system of the SM Group, and the POS system needs to support user login for SMAC members. Similar to the AS-IS, SMAC members can login with a card swipe, scan a QR code or manually input membership ID (with supervisor intervention) to log in.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > SMAC login
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
Entry For Membership Login Select Validation Approach Manually Input SMAC ID Membership Login Success Membership Detail Page | The cashier clicks on the yellow "Member" button in the top right corner of the Sales Page. A membership login side drawer appears, where the cashier needs to select the login method as either card swipe or QR code scan, or manually input membership ID. If a card swipe is selected, the cashier can swipe the SMAC card directly. If QR is selected, the cashier can use the scanner to scan the SMAC QR code presented by the customer. If Manually Input is selected, the cashier first needs to have supervisor authorization. Once authorized, he/she can use the soft keyboard to manually input the 16 digits member ID. If the cashier scans another QR or swipes a non-SMAC card at this point, the POS system will display a popup indicating that the member login is invalid. Whether it is card swipe or QR scan, the POS system can obtain the following key information about the member even in offline mode: Member ID Member type (DAC_Loyalty & SVS Product Codes as of 06162023_from DAC 11222023.xlsx) Expiration date of the membership Member Name If POS reads track one, POS can also get the customer's name. Other tracks don’t have SMAC member’s name. After a successful login, the "Member" button in the top right corner will display the member's card number (with some digits replaced by asterisks to protect privacy) indicating the membership login success. After a successful login, the cashier can click on the "Member" button in the top right corner to view the member details (member ID, member type, expiration date), and to log out via clicking the "Log Out Member" button on the bottom. Privacy Control As per the current SM data privacy control, the SMAC member ID and name will be masked on the screen or receipt. SMAC Member ID Mask the 1-12 digits of the SMAC number using six * chars and leave the last 4 digits exposed. SMAC

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > SMAC login
content: Member Name Show the first 3 letters of the name then mask the rest of the letters via * with fixed length of 13 characters regardless of the name’s actual length. For the member's name and ID stored in the database and returned to SAP, according to the SM requirement, DMALL POS will encrypt this info before saving to the DB or passing to SAP. DMALL IT will share the decrypt method to SAP as well. For the data that needs to be returned to the DAC the field needs to be decrypted beforehand.

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > SMAC login
content:
5. Exceptional Scenario

By swiping member card, POS will read the first track by default (with name) and use the second track as a backup (no name) if first track is damaged or not readable.

Both swiping card and scan QR code don’t need API request so login membership can be done even if POS has no network connection.

When manually inputting the membership ID, POS will validate if the membership ID is 16 digits or not. If it’s not 16 digits, POS prompt error message “Please input the valid SMAC membership ID.”

If the user's membership has expired already, the POS system will display a prompt “Cannot login SMAC membership as this membership is expired.” and the login has failed.

If POS gets force exit, once the POS gets reboot, all items and the membership status will be restored.

When a cashier suspends a transaction that has membership validated, once recalled, the membership status will be recalled as well.

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
Current POS cannot view the member details and exit membership. | DMALL POS can display the membership info like membership card #, name, expired date, etc. | Add a new feature to display the membership info like membership card #, name, expired date, etc. Also, cashier can exit membership manually from there. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > SMAC Point Issue
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today SMAC points are not issued in real time but sent to the member within 3 business days after customer’s purchase.

2. TO-BE Overview

With DMALL POS, after a customer completes SMAC login and successfully pays for an order, the POS system will use the point issuance API provided by DAC to issue points to the SMAC member in (almost) real-time.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

Cashier needs to complete the SMAC member login during the sales process (membership login is also available when POS has no network connection).

The POS system calculates the eligible points based on DAC's rules.

After completing the payment for the order:

If the POS system has internet connection, it will immediately initiate a request to issue points.

If the API requests success, the points will be issued to the SMAC member directly.  Customers should expect to receive the points within 15 minutes after completing the order.

However, if the API request fails or times out, there will be no prompt on the POS side (as the order has already been completed on the POS).

If the POS system does not have internet connection after payment, it will temporarily keep the point request locally and call the API when the POS is reconnected to the internet. 
In this case, customers will receive the points after a certain period after completing the order (depending on the duration of POS disconnection).

There will also be a daily feedback file sent to DAC, which will include all the orders. It is recommended that DAC handle any failed or timed-out point requests as a fallback solution (confirmed with DAC on 7th Dec 2023).

5. Exceptional Scenario

If the POS system does not have internet connection after payment, it will temporarily keep the point request locally and call the API when the POS is reconnected to the internet.

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > SMAC Point Issue
content:
API requests may fail or get time out, but POS will only send point issuance API once to avoid duplicated issuance. In this case, POS will still send the DAC file on the daily basis and should DAC to handle any failed or timed-out point issuance request as a fallback solution,

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
Current POS issues points within 3 business days via sending DAC files | DMALL POS issues points in real time via sending API call, but also keeps the DAC file posting as a backup solution. | Changing from the 3 days points issuance to nearly real-time point issuance with the API call. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > SMAC Express Kit
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today if POS scans the first barcode on the SMAC Express Kit, it has to scan the second barcode as a dummy article that binds with the first barcode. Deleting the first barcode will remove the linked 2nd barcode as well.

2. TO-BE Overview

SMAC Express Kit is a special article offered by SM Market, where customers can purchase the kit to register as a SMAC member. Normally, the SMAC Express Kit will have an item code and a separate account id (second barcode), and the POS system needs to capture both codes to purchase the SMAC Express Kit.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

Page Screenshot | Function Description
Side Drawer That Requires 2nd Barcode | Prerequisite: DMALL OS provides a backend configuration option to input the item code of the SMAC Express Kit When the cashier adds an item to the shopping cart, the POS system will check if the item is a SMAC Express Kit. If it is a SMAC Express Kit, the POS system will automatically prompt the user to scan the SMAC Express Kit Account ID (2nd barcode) through a side drawer menu. If the cashier scans barcode or manually confirms the input, the SMAC Express Kit will be added (only one item row for SMAC Express Kit, the account ID will not be displayed in the shopping cart but will be returned to SAP & DAC through data posting). If the cashier clicks "Back" on the side drawer that requires inputting the account ID (2nd barcode), the SMAC Express Kit item will not be added to the shopping cart. If the user chooses to delete an added SMAC Express Kit, the associated SMAC Express Kit Account ID (2nd barcode) will be automatically removed without additional confirmation.

5. Exceptional Scenario

POS can only make sure the account ID has been captured, but POS cannot validate the account ID is correct or valid. It should be DAC to do the validation afterwords.

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > SMAC Express Kit
content:
POS will validate the length of the 2nd barcode and make sure it’s from 6 digits number to 20 digits number. If the length verification fails, an error message prompt says “Please scan the valid barcode on the SMAC Express Kit”

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
Current POS uses the dummy article for the 2nd barcode and link two articles together. | DMALL POS considers the 2nd barcode as a special attribute of the SMAC Kit article. | Changing the treatment of the 2nd barcode from a dummy article to a special attribute of the SMAC Kit article. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > SMAC Only Promo
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today the SMAC only promotion can only be triggered after the Cashier validates a SMAC membership on the POS, and the system will trigger the corresponding promotion as per the SMAC member type.

2. TO-BE Overview

Similar to the AS-IS, DMALL POS supports promotional types that are only applicable to certain SMAC members based on the membership type.

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

During the configuration process of the DMALL promotion system, the system supports configuring certain promotions to only be applicable to SMAC members.

In the shopping cart, before logging into SMAC membership, SMAC Only promotions will not be triggered.

Once a SMAC member logs in, the system will automatically execute a subtotal and apply all applicable SMAC member promotions.

5. Exceptional Scenario

SMAC only promotion can also be triggered even if POS has no network connection.

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable.

7. Key Changes

Not Applicable as there is no change between the AS-IS & TO-BE for this topic.

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > E-Voucher
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Today customers can present an E-Voucher to the POS, and POS will ask the E-Voucher system to determine if the E-Voucher is valid to use and provide the updated price after using the E-Voucher.

2. TO-BE Overview

E-Voucher is an electronic coupon issued by the SM DAC team for SMAC members. After using the DMALL system, the generation and redemption of E-Vouchers are managed by the DMALL coupon system (see Promotion Master Data Blueprint for details). SMAC members can use E-Vouchers on the DMALL POS system to trigger corresponding discounts.

Since the use of E-Vouchers involves real-time online redemption, this function is only available when POS has internet connection.

Check the E2E E-Voucher flow for more contexts

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > E-Voucher
content:
Page Screenshot | Function Description
E-Voucher Entry Button Submenu To Scan/Input E-Voucher (Coupon) Available E-Voucher List Cashier Select Best Choice | The customer presents the E-Voucher to the cashier. The cashier clicks on the "E-Voucher" button in the function area (or scan the E-Voucher QR directly on the sales page), which prompts the sidebar drawer to scan/input the E-Voucher code The cashier scans the QR code of the E-Voucher presented by the customer. The POS system will automatically call the coupon system to reserve the coupon (Note: E-Vouchers cannot be reserved without network connection. If the POS is offline, an error message will be displayed indicating that E-Vouchers cannot be used offline. Please try again later). Once the reservation is successful, the E-Voucher will be added to the POS system. The customer can add multiple E-Vouchers in the same order, but they will be categorized as either "Available" or "Unavailable" based on the usage conditions of the E-Vouchers. "Available" indicates that the E-Voucher can be used for the current order. "Unavailable" indicates that the E-Voucher is temporarily not applicable to the current order. For example, the order has not reached the minimum amount threshold for using the coupon. There may also be mutual exclusions among the "Available" E-Vouchers. For example, if E-Voucher A and E-Voucher B are both "Available," but they offer the same type of discount for the same article, only one can be chosen. If one is selected, the other will be grayed out (still visible in the "Available" list) and marked as the reason for being grayed out. The added E-Vouchers will be displayed as cards, including promotion information, E-Voucher code, and validity period. When an E-Voucher is in an "Unavailable" state, the reason for being unavailable will be indicated, such as not reaching the minimum order threshold. Note that being "Unavailable" does not mean it will always be unavailable. For example, if a coupon can

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > E-Voucher
content: not be used because the minimum threshold is not met, it may become "Available" after adding more items to the order. The POS system will automatically select the combination of E-Vouchers that provides the maximum discount based on the currently added E-Vouchers, but the user can also manually choose which E-Vouchers to use. Additionally, the user can click on "Best Choice" to let the system automatically select the combination of E-Vouchers that provides the maximum discount. E-Vouchers that are not selected will not be redeemed after the payment is completed and the reserved status will be released.

title: Function Design Solution > Promotion Related Process > E-Voucher
content:
5. Exceptional Scenario

E-Voucher can only be used with network connection as POS needs to query the E-Voucher status.

Customer can add several E-Vouchers in one transaction but not all of them will be redeemed. Only the E-Voucher that has been used in this transaction will be redeemed.

Technically it’s possible for one E-Voucher to be added on two different POS at the same time as adding E-Voucher only calls the query function. But once one transaction submits the order, this E-Voucher's status changes to “Reserved”, and then another transaction cannot submit order until manually remove the reserved E-Voucher.

6. Related Reports

Not Applicable

7. Key Changes

Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
Current POS can only determine if the E-Voucher can be used or not at this moment, if not, the E-Voucher will be rejected. | DMALL POS supports adding E-Voucher regardless of the availability at this moment. POS also helps choose the best offer if there are multiple E-Vouchers. | New feature to support adding E-Voucher regardless of the availability at this moment as the availability might change later with more items added. POS also helps choose the best offer if there are multiple E-Vouchers. | Cashier | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > Payment in general
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

POS support multiple tenders to pay for the payable amount and finish the transaction

2. To-BE Overview

After the cashier submits the transaction on the sales page, the amount payable will be calculated and settled. POS will enter the payment page to proceed with payment and complete the transaction.

On the payment page, the cashier selects the payment method and completes the payment process for the corresponding payment method. After the amount payable has been fully paid, POS will print receipts and back to cart automatically.

3. To-BE Flow

4. To-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > Payment in general
content:
Function entrance | Function entrance After finishing add-to-cart and all the sales operation on the sales page, click [Submit] to enter the payment page.
Payment page | Payment page Transaction info Amount payable Transaction detail button Display transaction detail info Receipts no. Cart info Display article list with name, qty and subtotal amount [Back to cart] [Cancel order] Payment info To be paid & paid amount When 'To be paid'=0, transaction complete Tender list Display all supported tenders, 6-7 tenders can be displayed in the area and rest of the tenders will be displayed in [All Tenders] The tender list, including tender sequence and process logic, is configured in the backend payment system Click [Tender] will trigger the corresponding payment process Support supervisor intervention Payment record After completed a payment process, the payment record with tender name and amount will display in the area Those tenders that can be voided will display [X] button with the payment record Payment operation Amount text box If the paying amount is different from transaction payable amount, cashier have to input the actual paying amount If the paying amount is the same as transaction payable amount, cashier do not need to input anything Cash quick payment button The most often used cash denomination (max 4) can be configured to display as buttons in this area Click the button to quick pay by corresponding denomination of cash Keypad [Cash] Pay by cash Transaction detail info Display transaction original total amount, discount total amount and detail amount for different promotion type All tender list Display full tender list

When to be paid=0, the payment process and the whole transaction completed. The system will automatically print receipts, back to the sales page and synchronize the transaction to the cloud server.
Tender configuration | Tender configuration Create payment tender EFT and online tender such as GCASH, QRPH and SMAC points need to be integrated first. The Display and sequence can be configured The logic of payment process can be configured Payment Name on POS Icon Used on POS Open Cash Drawer Include in EOS Counting Enable Rounding Allow Cancel Payment Enable Last Payment Maximum Payable Amount

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > Cash
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

POS support to pay by cash, which system does not need to request any device or Api.

2. To-BE Overview

POS support to pay by cash, which system does not need to request any device or Api.

3. To-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

Cash payment | Cash payment There is a cash payment shortcut button in the lower right corner of the keypad which has the same logic as the tender [cash] button. When there is an amount in the amount text box, enter the amount to complete the payment; if there is no amount in the amount text box, use the current remaining payable amount to complete the payment. However, there is also a short cut cash payment area for cash above the keypad where the most used denomination of cash can be configured. Click the button to quickly pay by corresponding face value as cash tender. For cash payment, it is allowed to pay more than 'to be paid' amt. The excess amount will be displayed on the pop up as changes to remind the cashier. Cash payment record can be voided by clicking [X] without any additional system logic.
Remark The currency for cash payments will be pre-configured in the system which is PHP for SM. No rounding needs for SM as it is configurable in the system [CASH] payment can be processed offline

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > EFT Tender
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

POS support to pay by EFT, which system needs to request EFT device to finish the payment.

For online EFT tender, Castles and Ingenico EFT terminal will be integrated to DMALL POS. Offline EFT tender will be described in the offline tender part.

2. To-BE Overview

POS support to pay by EFT, which system needs to request EFT device to finish the payment.

For online EFT tender, Castles and Ingenico EFT terminal will be integrated to DMALL POS. Offline EFT tender will be described in the offline tender part.

3. To-BE Flow

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > EFT Tender
content:
Select tender | Select tender Click [EFT] tender If the paying amount is different from transaction payable amount, cashier have to input the actual paying amount If the paying amount is the same as transaction payable amount, cashier do not need to input anything EFT cannot pay more than to be paid amount
Payment request | Payment request After selecting EFT, a loading prompt will pop-up with a timeout countdown which is configurable POS will send a payment request to EFT device Cashiers have to operate on the EFT device to finish the rest of the process
Payment success Payment failed | Payment success When POS gets the payment success response, a payment record will be displayed in the payment record area If to be paid amount=0, the whole transaction will be finished automatically Payment failed When POS gets the payment failed response, a prompt will pop-up to remind the cashier with an error message. The cashier has to select EFT again or use another tender.
Payment timeout | Payment timeout If POS cannot get any response within timeout period, a prompt will pop-up to remind the cashier [Confirm] POS will go back to the payment page and the payment will be considered failed. The cashier has to manually check EFT terminal whether the payment is successful. If it is successful, the cashier processes the payment through offline EFT tenders. If it is not successful, the cashier could select EFT tender or another to process payment again.

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > EFT Tender
content:
Payment void | To cancel the payment record, click [X], POS will send a 26-Void request to EFT with invoice no. Success: Only BDO debit/credit before EFT settlement can be voided, EFT will send back success response POS will delete payment record and reverse remaining amt Failed & Timeout: 1) BDO debit/credit cannot be voided after EFT settlement. 2) Other tenders besides BDO debit/credit cannot be voided. 3) POS did not receive any response from EFT. In these three cases: POS will prompt the error message with [Confirm] POS will delete payment record and reverse remaining amt anyway as the cashier will contact operations to process refund offline
Remark [EFT] tender can be processed when POS is offline After POS sent a message to EFT, within 1s if no ACK, POS sent the message again, if still no ACK within another 1s, the request will be seemed as failure. If ACK is received, POS would start loading to wait for business response. POS will maintain a mapping between EFT tenders and POS tenders. Cashier selects [EFT] to trigger the payment process. After the payment is done, the system will record the tender as subdivided tenders
Data capture Card Issuer Name Card Number The card number is masked on the POS receipt (first 6 digits and last 4 digits show, middle part is masked with X). POS local database will store the actual card number and sync to cloud server. In the data captured file (middle part is masked with 0) except for the SM Credit Card tender. Invoice Number Expiry date Approval Code Issuer ID
Offline EFT According to the scenario of unintegrated or disconnected EFT, EFT offline tender will be used to record the payment Offline BDO Credit Offline BDO Debit Offline MAYA Offline Metrobank Offline GPAP

content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Currently, GCASH payment has to go through EFT

2. To-BE Overview

POS support to pay by GCASH without EFT which the cashier can scan the QR code using the POS scanner and request GCASH Api to finish the payment.

3. TO-BE Flow

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > Online Tender-GCASH
content:
Select tender | Select tender Click [GCASH] tender If the paying amount is different from transaction payable amount, cashier has to input the actual paying amount If the paying amount is the same as transaction payable amount, cashier do not need to input anything GCASH cannot pay more than to be paid amount
Payment request | Payment request After selecting GCASH, a prompt will pop-up to remind the cashier to scan customer QRcode POS will support scanning both QRcode and linear barcode or manually input GCASH number (if the scanner cannot recognize the code, cashier has to manually input) After scanning the customer QRcode, a loading prompt will pop-up with a timeout countdown which is configurable POS will send a payment request through cloud payment system and gateway to GCASH The merchantTransId, scanned code string, order amount, device number, etc. will be sent to GCash to obtain payment results (successful, unknown, failed)
Payment success Payment failed | Payment success When POS gets the payment success response, a payment record will be displayed in the payment record area If to be paid amount=0, the whole transaction will be finished automatically Payment failed When POS gets the payment failed response, a prompt will pop-up to remind the cashier with an error message. The cashier has to select GCASH again or another tender.
Payment timeout | Payment timeout If POS cannot get any response within timeout period, a prompt will pop-up to remind the cashier with two options [Inquiry] POS will display 'loading' while send an inquiry request with merchantTransId, through cloud payment system and gateway to GCASH [Cancel] POS will go back to the payment page while sending a cancel request through cloud payment system and gateway to GCASH in case the customer has paid successfully

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > Online Tender-GCASH
content:
Payment void | Payment void GCASH payment can be void through [X], [Back to cart], [Cancel order] and payment timeout [Cancel] POS will send void requests through payment system and gateway to GCASH. Once POS sends a void request, payment will be cancelled on POS side as the payment system will continuously request GCASH to cancel the payment until it is successful. If payment system continuously requests GCASH to cancel the payment for 2 days but it still does not get a successful request, it will be viewed as exception in payment record and have to process it offline.
Remark [GCASH] payment only supported online [GCASH] payment can still be proceeded through [EFT]

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > Online Tender-GCASH
content:
5. Exceptional Scenario

Payment failed

When POS gets the payment failed response, a prompt will pop-up to remind the cashier with an error message. The cashier has to select GCASH again or another tender.

Payment timeout

If POS cannot get any response within timeout period, a prompt will pop-up to remind the cashier with two options

[Inquiry] POS will display 'loading' while send an inquiry request through cloud payment system and gateway to GCASH

[Cancel] POS will go back to the payment page while sending a cancel request through cloud payment system and gateway to GCASH in case the customer has paid successfully

Payment void

POS will send void requests through payment system and gateway to GCASH. Once POS sends a void request, payment will be cancelled on POS side as the payment system will continuously request GCASH to cancel the payment until it is successful.

If the payment system continuously requests GCASH to cancel the payment for 2 days but it still does not get a successful request, it will be viewed in payment record and have to process it offline.

content:
1. AS-IS Overview

2. To-BE Overview

POS support to pay by QRPH without EFT which the customer screen can display QR code and request QRPH Api to finish the payment.

3. TO-BE Flow

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > Online Tender-QRPH
content:
Select tender | Select tender Click [QRPH] tender If the paying amount is different from transaction payable amount, cashier have to input the actual paying amount If the paying amount is the same as transaction payable amount, cashier do not need to input anything QRPH cannot pay more than to be paid amount
QR code request | QR code request After selecting [QRPH], a loading prompt will pop-up with a timeout countdown which is 6 seconds POS will send a payment QRcode inquiry request through cloud payment system and gateway to QRPH to fetch the payment QR code and display DMALL will specify and send BillNumber, CreditMID (Payment Institution ID), and merchant ID (Merchant ID within the Payment Institution) to QRPH
QR code fetching success QR code fetching failed QR code fetching timeout | QR code fetching success After fetched the payment QR code, POS will display it on the customer screen for the customer to scan and pay Meanwhile, a loading prompt will pop-up on the main screen with a payment timeout countdown which is configurable. The QR code will be shown on the main screen as well in case there is something wrong with the customer screen. QR code fetching failed When POS gets the QR code fetching failed response, a prompt will pop-up to remind the cashier with an error message QR code fetching timeout If POS cannot get any response within timeout period, a prompt will pop-up to remind the cashier with two options [Inquiry] POS will display 'loading' while send another fetching QR code request through cloud payment system and gateway to QRPH and wait for response [Cancel] POS will go back to the payment page
Payment request | Payment request After fetching the payment Code, a loading prompt will pop-up with a timeout countdown which is configurable POS will continuously send inquiry requests through cloud payment system and gateway to QRPH The payment result is obtained through the query interface. Specify BillNumber and CreditMID to obtain payment results

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > Online Tender-QRPH
content:
Payment success Payment failed | Payment success When POS gets the payment success response, a payment record will be displayed in the payment record area If to be paid amount=0, the whole transaction will be finished automatically Payment failed When POS gets the payment fail response, a prompt will pop-up to remind the cashier with an error message
Payment timeout | Payment timeout If POS cannot get any response within timeout period, a prompt will pop-up to remind the cashier with two options [Inquiry] POS will display 'loading' while send an inquiry request through cloud payment system and gateway to QRPH [Cancel] POS will go back to the payment page as the payment is not down. As QRPH does not support to void or return the payment, the cashier has to contact operations, and will reversal from Acctg if customer paid successfully
Payment void | Payment void QRPH payment can be void through [X], [Back to cart], [Cancel order] and payment timeout [Cancel] The cashier has to contact operations, and will reversal from Acctg if customer paid successfully POS will not send any void request through payment system and gateway to QRPH, as QRPH does not support to void or return the payment

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > Online Tender-SMAC point

Inquiry points request | Inquiry points request After selecting [SMAC point], a loading prompt will pop-up with a timeout countdown which is 6 seconds POS will send a SMAC point inquiry request through cloud server to DAC to fetch the SMAC point with its redeemable amount and display

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > Online Tender-SMAC point
content:
Inquiry points success Inquiry points failed Inquiry points timeout | Inquiry points success After fetched the SMAC point with its redeemable amount, POS will display them on the payment page The minimum value of the redeemable amount and the to be paid amount will be filled in the input box by default Cashier can change the payment amount, but the payment amount cannot over minimum value of the redeemable amount and the to be paid amount After cashier submit the SMAC points payment amount, POS will send a payment request through cloud server to DAC [Pay by another SMAC membership] SM allows the customer to use their own SMAC to login and get points while using another SMAC or other person’s SMAC to pay. As in most scenarios, the customers are going to use the same SMAC to pay. POS will request the balance of the logged in SMAC by default but provides a button in the points inquiry page allowing the cashier to use another SMAC to finish the payment process. Payment void | Payment void SMAC points payment can be void through [X], [Back to cart], [Cancel order] and payment timeout [Cancel] POS will send void request through cloud server to DAC. Once POS sends a reversal points redemption request, payment will be cancelled on POS side as the payment system will continuously request DAC to cancel the payment until it is successful. If payment system continuously requests GCASH to cancel the payment for 2 days but it still does not get a successful request, it will be viewed as exception in payment record and have to process it offline.

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > Back to cart/Cancel order
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

The current SM POS system does not have a separate payment page.

2. TO-BE Overview

In the payment page, the cashier can go back to the cart to keep the article or cancel the transaction.

3. TO-BE Description

-

4. TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Pay > Back to cart/Cancel order
content:
Back to cart | Back to cart The cashier can return to the shopping cart by clicking the "Back to Cart" button in the lower left corner After clicking the back to shopping cart button, POS will determine whether there are currently added payment records If there is no added payment record, the pop-up window confirms whether you need to return to the shopping cart on the payment page. If you confirm, go back to the shopping cart directly. If any payment record exists, determine the payment record type For EFT and online tenders, the system will pop up to prompt that you cannot return to the shopping cart. The cashier has to manually cancel the payment record first. For cash and offline tenders, the system will pop up a prompt to remind the cashier to refund the amt. Click [Confirm] to return to the shopping cart. Back to cart will retain all the transaction info including articles, SMAC login, promotion, govn sales info etc.
Cancel order | Cancel order The cashier can cancel the order by clicking the "Cancel Order" button in the lower left corner After clicking the Cancel Order button, POS will determine whether there is currently an added payment record If there are no added payment records, the pop-up window confirms whether the order needs to be canceled on the payment page. If confirmed, the user is required to select the reason for canceling the order(configurable). Cancel the order after confirming the reason. If any payment record exists, determine the payment record type For EFT and online tenders, the system will pop up to prompt that you cannot return to the shopping cart. The cashier has to manually cancel the payment record first. For cash and offline tenders, the system will pop up a prompt to remind the cashier to refund the amt. Click [Confirm] to return to the shopping cart. Cancel order will delete all the transaction info and back to an empty cart. Support supervisor intervention. The cancelled transaction will be synced to the cloud server.

title: Function Design Solution > Return > Return with receipt
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Post void: Fully cancel transaction within a day, refund through the original tender.

Return: Input the SI no. and scan the return article with no system verification whether the article belongs to the original transaction, or it already been returned once. Refund through MR form.

2. To-BE Overview

Won't have post void anymore because in the cloud-based structure, the original transaction might already sync to the cloud server and do the inventory deduction and other following procedure. Therefore, if the transaction is finished, it should go through the return process for any void scenario.

For return with receipt, the cashier has to input the receipt no. and the system will verify the original transaction and related return record. Support to refund through original tender or custom tender (MR form and cash)

3. TO-BE Flow

4. TO-BE description

title: Function Design Solution > Return > Return with receipt
content:
Function entrance | Function entrance Left menu (configurable) Middle function bar (configurable) Shortcut function bar (configurable)
Query orders | Query orders After entering the return with a receipt page, the focus will automatically be in the upper right corner to query order input box. Enter the receipt number to query the order
Confirm return All return Partial return | Confirm return After successfully querying the order, the page will display the quantity and amount information of the refundable/refunded articles in the order If the order exceeds the return time limit for returning with the receipt, a pop-up message will appear indicating that the return cannot be processed. (the return time limit is configurable) All return After clicking <Select All>, all refundable articles in the order will be selected with the maximum quantity Partial return Click on the <->，<+> in the product list to select the article to be returned and adjust the return quantity Submit the return After confirming the quantity of returning articles and the estimate refundable amount will be displayed, click < Return > to submit the return Change order Click on the upper right corner of the query order text box, re-enter the order number/small ticket number to re-query in order to change the return order Cancel returns Click < Cancel Return > to cancel the ‘return with receipt’
Choose Reason for Return | Choose Reason for Return If the reason for the return is configured, you need to select a reason for the return after clicking < Return > to submit the return

title: Function Design Solution > Return > Return with receipt
content:
Choose refund mode | Choose refund mode After selecting the reason for the return, you need to select the refund method. If the original refund is not turned on, you will directly enter the custom refund page (for return without receipt, it will directly enter the custom refund page) Original refund Jump to the original refund page for a refund The refund will be based on the tenders that used in original order Custom refund Jump to the custom refund page to make a refund The refund will be based on the tender that are configured in backend as custom refund supported which is MR form and cash for SM
Refund page | See Refund section
Complete return with receipt | Complete return with receipt After the refund is completed, there will be a pop-up showing the tenders and amount of the refund. After confirmation, the system will automatically pop up the drawer and print the receipt The return transaction will be synced to cloud server

title: Function Design Solution > Return > Return without receipt
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Cannot return without receipt

2. To-BE Overview

If the customer cannot provide the previous sales receipt, the store will decide whether to complete the return without receipt by adding the return article to cart and refund through MR form and cash.

This function is also used when customers want to return with receipt, but the original transaction happened before DMALL system went live.

Support three ways to add-to-cart including scanning barcode, search, quick access articles. Edit quantity and confirm price Edit Product Returns Edit product return price Support editing article unit price when the negotiated return amount is inconsistent with the current execution price Submit the return Confirm the return goods and click < Return > to submit the return Cancel order Click < Cancel Order > to empty the shopping cart for returns without receipts

Refund page | See Refund section
Complete return without receipt | Complete return without receipt After the refund is completed, there will be a pop-up showing the tenders and amount of the refund. After confirmation, the system will automatically pop up the drawer and print the receipt The return transaction will be synced to cloud server

Refund through MR form (cash also supported)

2. To-BE Overview

For returning with/without receipt, the system supports the refund mode of original refund and custom refund. For original refund, the refund will be based on the tenders that used in original transaction. For custom refund, the refund will be based on the tender that is configured in backend which is MR form and cash for SM.

content:

If the original refund is not turned on, you will directly enter the custom refund page (for return without receipt, it will directly enter the custom refund page) Original refund Jump to the original refund page for a refund The refund will be based on the tenders that used in original order Custom refund Jump to the custom refund page to make a refund The refund will be based on the tender that are configured in backend as custom refund supported which is MR form and cash for SM
Original Refund | Original Refund Original payment After submitting the return request, the return system will send back the original tender with its amount. As the tenders have already been allocated to articles, the refund amount for different tenders will be calculated and sent back to POS for partial return. If the original tenders only include cash or offline tender, POS will not enter the refund page but directly return successfully and pop-up to remind cashier the refund tender and amount. If the original tenders include device (EFT) or online tender, POS will enter the refund page. Original Refund Page Article Information List Remaining refundable amount and refunded amount List of original payment methods For device (EFT) and online tender, cashiers have to click [refund] to trigger the corresponding refund flow, which is the same flow as void tender. If it cannot be refunded, POS will see it as refund success as the cashier has to contact operations to process refunds offline

If it is not entered, click < Payment Method > to complete the refund with the remaining refund Keypad: Edit refund amount text box < Cash >: Refund by cash payment Select Refund Tender Enter the refund amount in the Amount text box, otherwise a full refund of one tender does not need to be entered. Click < tender > to complete the refund with the remaining refund Choose the tender for the refund After completing the refund, it will be displayed in the refund record. Cancel refund record Click < x > to cancel the refund record Refund completed When the remaining refundable amount is 0, the refund is completed, and the overall return process is completed Cancel Refund Click < Cancel refund > to cancel refund

When using MR deduct tender in the next sales transaction, the MR form barcode can be scanned or inputted, and POS can capture the amount from the barcode to finish the payment.
