title: Function Design Solution > Cash Mgt > Pick up
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

CS personnel do pick up 4 times per day to make sure the cashdrawer would be full and to ensure the security of cash. The cash will be counted and taken out from the cashdrawer while the system needs to record the amount of pick up.

2. To-BE Overview

The pick up function allows cashiers to input the qty for each denomination and take out the money from the cashdrawer. The pickup record will synchronize to cloud server and impact the short and over for end of shift(pre-commit).

3. To-BE Flow

4. To-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Cash Mgt > Pick up
content:
Function entrance Input qty by denomination | Function entrance Left menu (configurable) Middle function bar (configurable) Shortcut function bar (configurable) Input qty by denomination Support supervisor intervention Click the [pick up] button to pop up the amount input page and the drawer will open automatically. Cash will be configured that supported to be picked up. The ‘pick up’ denominations are also configurable. Input the qty for different denominations and the total amount will be calculated by system. Submit to proceed to the next step.
Choose a reason Pick up completed | Choose a reason This step ON/OFF is configurable. If [Choose a reason] is ON, reason options shall be displayed as the attached mockup, reason options are configurable also. After selecting a reason, pick up will be completed and receipt will be printed. Receipt A pickup receipt will be printed out automatically. The template for the receipt sees the receipt section. Remarks After completing the pickup process, an order with type 'pick up' will be generated and synchronized to cloud server. When POS is offline, the 'pick up' can still proceed, the order will stay in the local data queue and synchronize to cloud server once the network is connected. The pickup amount shall be counted in 'End of Shift'(pre-commit) and participate in the calculation of short & over. Receivable declaration amount calculation should deduct pick up amount
Pick up management | Pick up management Once the 'pick up' synchronized to the cloud server. The record can be viewed in pick up management in backend system. Support multiple search criteria and detail viewing.

title: Function Design Solution > Cash Mgt > End of shift (Cashier Pre-commit & X)
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

End of shift(pre-commit) is used for cashiers to close their daily work. Cashiers have to count and take out the cash inside the cash drawer, meanwhile input the amount by denomination into the system.

For SM, pre-commit is going to use cashier accountability which the sales statistics calculation will include the cashier's sales data from all POS devices in the store.

2. To-BE Overview

End of shift(pre-commit) will be cashier accountability. The cashier needs to enter the qty for all denominations of cash to finish the process. An end of shift receipt will be printed.

POS will request the in-store POS server to fetch the cashier’s transaction data within the store during the shift. The end of shift receipt and record will combine the current SM pre-commit and cashier X-report which contains the sales, return, tender, etc. statistics data and short&over (difference between receivable and actual declaration cash amt).

3. To-BE Flow

4. To-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Cash Mgt > End of shift (Cashier Pre-commit & X)
content:
Function entrance | Function entrance Left menu (configurable) Middle function bar (configurable) Shortcut function bar (configurable)
Cash counting | Cash counting Count the qty of all cash denominations inside the cash drawer and input on the page. The denomination for both bill and coin will be configured in cloud server. The system will calculate the total amount based on the denomination and qty.
Submit end of shift | Submit end of shift Support supervisor intervention POS will fetch the data from the in-store POS server and do the calculation. (if in-store POS server is not available, POS will request cloud server to do the calculation) Calculation period starts from the cashier's last end of shift to current time in store level The data include the transaction data of all POS devices in the store by cashiers during the shift period. Cash short&over (=declaration amt - expected amt) will be calculated for the shift Declaration amt: cashier count and input in system Expected amt: calculate by system (=float + sales - return -pick up)
End of shift completed | End of shift completed After the end of shift is completed, the end of shift receipt will be printed. The data contains the sales, return, tender, etc. statistics data and short&over. The end of shift record will be synced to cloud server The POS will automatically logout and restart based on configuration
Cash counting record with short&over | Cash counting record with short&over The cash counting record with short&over for every shift can be viewed in backend system

title: Function Design Solution > Cash Mgt > End of shift (Cashier Pre-commit & X)
content:
Since the pre-commit and X read solution needs BIR confirmation, this solution might change as per BIR regulation.

5. Exceptional Scenario

POS will prioritize fetching data from the in-store POS server to do the calculation. If the in-store POS server is not available, POS will request cloud server to do the calculation.

If both the in-store POS server and cloud server are not available, POS will print out an offline end of shift receipt which only include the cash counting data, put an asynchronous task into the queue and keep trying to sync the ‘end of shift’ to cloud server. Once the task reaches the cloud server, cloud server will do the calculation and generate receipt as EJ.

6. Related Reports

Shift cash counting record with short&over

Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
Cashiers do pre-commit as the end of shift to count the cash CS personnel do terminal and cashier X-report to calculate the transaction data and generate receipt | Combine pre-commit and cashier X-report into end of shift function. The end of shift receipt includes the data from pre-commit and cashier X-report | In the store level, only need cashiers to do end of shift after their daily work | Cashier, CS personnel | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Cash Mgt > End of Day (Term Z)
content:
1.AS-IS Overview

After all the cashiers finished their pre-commit for today. EOD will be operated on the POS server to generate multiple files and synchronize to HQ for different systems. Meanwhile, every POS terminal in store will print out a terminal Z-report which contains the calculation of today's transactions.

2.To-BE Overview

In DMALL cloud server, the EOD trigger time can be configured. The system will automatically trigger EOD and will first do a reconciliation between cloud server and in-store POS server to check if the sales data is complete. Secondly, if reconciliation passes, the system will generate Z-reports for every POS terminal and record as EJ files. Lastly, the system will generate and synchronize corresponding data files to corresponding external systems.

3.TO-BE Flow

4.TO-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Cash Mgt > End of Day (Term Z)
content:
EOD configuration | EOD configuration (store level) EOD term Z trigger time For POS to automatically trigger and generate term Z
EOD term Z | EOD term Z Auto When POS reaches the term Z trigger time, POS will first verify if POS is logged in If not logged in, POS will generate term Z base on local data and sync to cloud server Example: Tigger time at 12AM, generating term Z from 12AM - T+1 12AM If logged in, POS will skip the term Z trigger time and not generate term Z report. When the next time POS login, POS will generate the term Z. Example: if cashier still on sales during 12AM, and logout at 3AM, no term Z generated but when the cashier login this POS the next time (e.g. 7AM), then term Z generated at that point with the range from 12AM - T+1 7AM Example (24h): if cashier still on sales during 12AM, and logout at 3AM, no term Z generated. the next cashier takes over at 3:01AM, then the term Z generated from 12AM - T+1 3:01AM Manual Normally, manual term Z is not need, but CS personal can still do manual term Z on the POS anytime Example: if the POS has generated term Z on Jan1st 12AM, and CS Personal manually trigger term Z on POS at Jan 1st 10AM. The term Z calc period will be: Dec 31st 12AM - Jan 1st 12AM Jan 1st 12AM - Jan 1st 10AM Jan 1st 10AM - Jan 2nd 12AM

title: Function Design Solution > Cash Mgt > End of Day (Term Z)
content:
Data reconciliation | Data reconciliation In-store POS server will reconcile the sales data between cloud server and in-store POS server for every store every 3 hours (configurable). Only verify the total transaction qty and amt. All day reconciliation pass Start EOD data posting procedure All day reconciliation not pass Timeout: re-try 3 times continuously and automatically; timeout 3 times in a role will seem as failed at this time point Failed: re-try every 3 hours within 12 hours; If all failed, the EOD data posting will be seemed as an ultimate failure which needs IT to manually ensure data consistency and manually trigger EOD data posting without data reconciliation (manually copy paste transaction files through program). For the manually trigger EOD data posting, IT have to click [re-try] on that failed EOD data posting record and the system will trigger data posting to generate file and send to SAP, DAC, etc.
EOD data posting | EOD data posting When store reconciliation passes, the cloud server will calculate the data for the whole natural day, generate and synchronize data to corresponding systems such as SAP, DAC, etc. (View details in integration blueprint) EOD data posting records with their status can be viewed in backend system. All records provide a manual data posting button for exceptional scenarios.
Remark EOD term Z generation and data posting will be divided into 2 separate procedures Term Z is generated automatically on POS with the configurable trigger time Data posting is triggered on cloud server every natural day based on data reconciliation

title: Function Design Solution > Cash Mgt > End of Day (Term Z)
content:
Since the end of day solution needs BIR confirmation, this solution might change as per BIR regulation.

5. Exceptional Scenario

Reconciliation failed

If reconciliation fails for a certain store in a certain day, the EOD data posting record will fail and can be viewed in backend system. Normally, the reason will be the network exception, which some of the transactions haven't been able to synchronize to the cloud server. IT should manually export the transaction data from in-store POS server and import it to cloud server. After that, IT can trigger EOD in the cloud server manually without reconciliation.

POS does not connect to in-store POS server

There is scenario that POS does not connect with in-store Server during the daily sales (move to the mall outside the market). For the SOP this POS has to be move back to the market and connected to the in-store POS server before closing the daily sales or EOD trigger time.

If the employee forgets to move the POS back to the store, There is a chance that the reconciliation passed when this POS does not successfully send any transaction to cloud server within this day (otherwise the reconciliation will not pass because there are more transaction in cloud compared to in-store POS server), therefore the EOD data posting is complete without the transaction in this POS.

In this case: 1) CS personnel and IT manually copy the transaction files from POS and paste to cloud server; 2) SAP reverse the whole store posting for this day; 3) IT manually triggers the EOD data posting again from backend.

POS physically broken

Firstly, make sure the hard disk still works, at least the transaction files can be copied from POS.

Secondly, manually paste the files into cloud server and manually trigger the EOD data posting.

6. Related Reports

Terminal Z-report (EJ)

7. Key Changes

title: Function Design Solution > Cash Mgt > End of Day (Term Z)
content:
Key Changes
Current State: As Is | Future State: To Be | Impact / New / Remove / Change Process | Roles or Teams Impacted | Company / Organization
CS personnel do term Z manual after store business day on POS server and every POS terminal will print out a term Z receipt | Cloud system automatically trigger EOD and generate term Z receipt as EJ | Change EOD from manual trigger to automatically trigger | CS personnel, operation | All companies in scope.

(only image for POS, support video for PVT) For ad image, support 16:9 and 4:3 file size which are mainstream POS screen ratio File format Image: JPG, PNG, etc. Video: MP4, WAV, AVI, etc.

Remark For busy time, as it is a full size image for the whole screen but the right half will display transaction info, it is suggest to put the ad content on left half of the image and leave the right half blank with only background color

title: Function Design Solution > Other Cases > Offline Service
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

SAP and other related HQ systems synchronize master data such as articles, promotion, etc. through files to store telnet server and then to POS server. Then the POS server synchronizes data to the POS terminal to support POS business flow.

Daily transaction data from POS terminal will be synchronized to POS server and generate files to send back to SAP and other related HQ systems through telnet server.

2. To-BE Overview

As DMALL system is cloud based, POS terminal can directly request cloud to use latest data to process transactions while the transaction data can be synchronized to cloud server in real-time as long as the network is normal. However, due to the complexity of the store network situation, an offline service strategy is needed to ensure normal POS sales when the network is disconnected.

Offline master data synchronization

Any master data change will generate a new offline data version and synchronize to POS offline service data. Both delta and full-data sync are supported.

For BCP strategy, offline data can be exported from cloud server and imported to the in-store POS server. POS offline service can fetch offline data from in-store POS server.

Transaction data posting

POS transaction data will be synchronized to cloud server in real-time as long as the network is connected. If the network is disconnected, transaction data will remain in POS upload task queue and synchronize immediately until the network is restored. The transaction data will also be synchronized to the in-store POS server for back up.

For BCP strategy, if the store network is disconnected for a long time, transaction data can be exported from in-store POS server and imported to the cloud server.

3. To-BE Flow

4. To-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Other Cases > Offline Service
content:
Offline master data synchronization | Offline master data synchronization Offline data type Account data: used for cashier login and functional authorization Article data: used for article search, add-to-cart, subtotal (price calculation) Promotion data: used for article search, add-to-cart, subtotal (price calculation) Offline data generate Every time there is a data change for account, article and promotion, corresponding system can push the data (delta/full data) to offline data platform. Support real-time/periodically and auto/manual push. Offline data synchronization The offline data platform manages the offline data version. The POS offline service will query the offline data platform every 15 minutes and will automatically download the latest version.
Online & offline mode switching | Online & offline mode switching Auto switching For basic business operations such as login, authorization, article search, add-to-cart and subtotal (price calculation), POS will prioritize requests to the cloud. If the network disconnected, POS would switch to offline automatically and request the POS offline service to process the business flow. In addition, if the store network condition is always not good, the system supports the configuration of prioritizing the request for POS offline service to complete the business process. Manual switching If the store network suddenly disconnected for quite a long time, to protect the overall efficiency of sales, the cashier can manually switch the POS to offline mode, which all requests will be prioritized to go POS offline services. Support supervisor intervention Offline data version viewing On the POS setting page, POS offline data version for account, article, promotion with its date can be viewed and checked.

title: Function Design Solution > Other Cases > Offline Service
content: Transaction data posting | Transaction data posting To the cloud server Every time POS finalizes a transaction, POS will put the transaction data into a local asynchronous task queue and synchronize it to cloud server. If the network disconnected, the task would stay in the queue and keep trying synchronization until the network is restored. Once it is successfully synchronized, the task will be removed from the queue. To the in-store POS server Every time POS finalizes a transaction, POS will also synchronize it to the in-store POS server for backup.

title: Function Design Solution > Other Cases > Offline Service
content:
5. Exceptional Scenario

BCP strategy

For offline master data synchronization, if the store network disconnected for a long time and the changes of master data need to be used, the store offline master data can be copied from cloud server and pasted manually to the in-store POS server. Therefore, the offline service in POS terminal can fetch the latest offline data from the in-store POS server.

For transaction data posting, if the store network disconnected for a long time and the store transaction data cannot be synchronized to the cloud server, the store transaction data can be exported from the in-store POS server and imported to the cloud server. Therefore, the cloud server can process EOD and synchronize the data to SM and other external systems.

6. Related Reports

-

7. Key Changes

Urgent sync has to triggered by IT to sync to store | POS can prioritize request cloud server so that any master data changes can be effective immediately. Offline data can also be synced to POS offline service in real-time. | For urgent data changes, IT no need to manually trigger for the POS to take effect. | IT | All companies in scope.

title: Function Design Solution > Other Cases > Weighing Scale
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Article master data changes from HQ will be synchronized to store through files. The FRG.CreateWS program will generate PCACSV and ALLCSV files and put them in a fixed folder in workstation. The weighing scale application will capture the 2 csv files and update the price in all weighing scales.

All barcode printed from the weight scale is in the EAN-13 format with 13 digits. For barcode start with 27 has the price amount within, while for barcode start with 28 has the weight info within.

2. To-BE Overview

After the initialization, any weighing article data change from article and promotion system will be synchronized to scale mgt system to generate PCACSV and ALLCSV files and put them on FTP. After the 2 csv files transfer to the certain folder on workstation, the vender weighing scale application will capture the 2 csv files and update the price in all weighing scales.

In DMALL, the weight item barcode is 18 digits which contain both the price info and the weight info, see the barcode logic below:

Prefix: 2 digits, either 28 or 27

Item code – 5 digits

Weight – 5 digits

Price (Total price) - 5 digits

Check digit – 1 digit

3. To-BE Flow

4. To-BE Description

title: Function Design Solution > Other Cases > Weighing Scale
content:
File generation-DMALL | File generation Manual trigger Scale mgt can manually click [sync to scale] button to synchronize full weighing article data to scale system, which normally used for initialization. Auto trigger Any weighing article data change from article and promotion system will be synchronized to scale mgt system. File generation For both manual and auto trigger, scale system will update the store level weighing article info. The unit price could be the price after unit promotion. Then will generate PCACSV and ALLCSV files according to the template from the scale vender. PCACSV file will include the delta data as ALLCSV file will include the full data. DMALL system will put the 2 csv files for different stores on FTP.
File transfer-SM | File transfer SM would be able to transfer the csv files to a certain folder on the store workstation.
Weighing scale update-Scale vender | Weighing scale update The weighing scale application would be able to capture the csv files from the certain folder and update all the connected weighing scale.

title: Function Design Solution > Other Cases > Weighing Scale
content: 18 Digits Barcode | Weight Scale Weight scale should be able to print out the 18 digits barcode as per the defined rules, which contains both price info and the weight info. POS POS should be able to parse the 18-digit barcode for the weight item and get the accurate price & weight info for the weight item. With an 18-digit barcode, all weight items should show the actual weight info on the shopping cart and sale receipt, see the receipt details on the 5.2 receipt section. Pricing Logic For barcode starts with 28, POS will only read use the price on the barcode For barcode starts with 27, POS will read the price on the barcode (label price) and also calculate another price with weight and latest unit price after unit promo (calc price), and then choose the lower one. If the label price is higher than the calc price, the calc price is used. No extra steps. If the label total price is lower than the calc price (current unit promo price*weight), the label price is used, and POS will prompt a warning message for cashier “The label price is lower than the regular price, please report to the CS when this order is finished.”. No supervisor intervention needed. There will also be a report to show all the cases that label price is lower than the calc price. See details in the dashboard blueprint. Sales Posting 18-dgits barcode for weight item is only between the weight scale and the POS. SAP sales posting is not impacted at all because the weight item will be posted to SAP in the format of “28/27 + 5digits item code + 6 zero”, which is the exact same format inn the SAP today. See details in the integration blueprint,

title: Function Design Solution > Other Cases > Weighing Scale
content:
5. Exceptional Scenario

For BCP strategy, if the store network disconnected for a long time, the 2 csv files can be copied from cloud server and pasted into store workstation manually to update the price in weighing scales.

For any article/promo data update, DMALL will make sure that all data will be synced to the weight scale in real-time, all the operation on the weight scale hardware and weight scale application should remain the same.

6. Related Reports

7. Key Changes

title: Promotion
content:
「SM」_Phase 1」_「Promotion」_V1.0.0

Editor: | Michael Li | Edit date: | 22/12/2023
Approver: | Approve date:

Copyright Reserved

Document Information

Document Source

This document is intended to present the Business process design from SM. Business rules and DMALL system design based on the SM requirements. This document will be available in SM’s Market SharePoint directory.

Revision History

Version | Date | Responsible | Change Summary
1.0.0 | 19/10/2023 | Michael Li | Draft
1.0.1 | 12/14/2023 | Michael Li | Following the review of the Blueprint on December 6th, various modifications have been made to the naming, management logic, and interaction of vouchers, including Next Purchase Coupon, Bank Promotion Barcode, Advertising etc. Additionally, in response to some custom requirements from SM, updates and supplements have been made to the allocation of Freebies and PWP （Purchase With Purchase） articles, the process for Double Receipt Freebies, and the promotion approval flow.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 1.1 Background Introduction
content:
Promotion management consists of promotion planning and execution. 1) In promotion planning, the business team would set sales and gross margin targets and plan promotions. 2) After planning, the system admin team would maintain promotion and marketing toolset activities across different companies (e.g. Supermarket, Hypermarket, Savemore Market,) or stores in the system and the promotions would take effect in the store so that customers can get a discount and a satisfying shopping experience.

Phase1 offers multiple promotion types together with marketing toolset system to support the business team to achieve various price strategies and promotion mechanics.

In the promotion planning stage, business needs to formulate price strategy and negotiate with vendors on the rebate agreement to build the promotion planning against each item category. But in DMALL promotion system and POS settlement process, DMALL directly records the promotion discount as shared by the merchant, that is SM. And the data is passed to SM through the order information and then processed by SM uniformly.

In the promotion execution stage, the system admin team would set up promotion activities and submit to supervisor review. Once the promotion activity is approved, its status would become ‘Approved’ automatically. When approved, the promotion will be executed normally during the validity period.

For any ongoing promotion activity, the system admin can terminate the activity any time and the termination will take effect instantly.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 1.2 System Overview
content:
DMALL Systems | Notes
Promotion sys | The DMALL Promotion system supports the creation and management of various types of promotion activities on the POS side. Promotion vouchers (that are vouchers, including E-vouchers and Next purchase coupon, which is on tailend receipt, are also generated by DMALL Promotion system, and these codes can correspond to specific promotion activities. In the POS settlement process, determine the hit of the promotion activity, and perform pricing and financial allocation. Provide pricing services for Weighing scale and Shelf label, etc. DMALL promotion system data will be synchronized to the In-store server. If there is no network, most of the above services, including promotion hits and pricing, can work normally (But if the store is offline for a long time, it will cause the promotion to fail to update normally), but vouchers and points cannot be used normally. Operators can set Bank promotion barcodes for promotion activities.
Marketing toolset | Marketing toolset support setting up activities to print next purchase coupons/ points/ double receipt freebie or advertising messages on tailend receipt after the order is completed or paid, when the order meets specific conditions. DMALL marketing tools system data will be synchronized to the In-store server. If there is no network, the above services can work normally.
Payment sys | Support payment.
POS service | Connect various systems in the transaction process to form an order in the end. Identify member information. Identify discount information such as Promotion vouchers, including E-Voucher and next purchase coupons.
Order sys | Record order articles and promotion information.
Shelf Label sys | DMALL promotion will affect display of the unit price and other promotion information on the Shelf label. To avoid the problem of untimely price, the data will be directly sent from the DMALL Cloud Service to the TPS in real time.

title: 1. Blueprint Document Scope > 1.2 System Overview
content:
Weighing scale | When weighing and then printing the weighing price tag, promotion system will calculate the price of the weighing article, which may hit unit promotion.
Member & Points sys | In the SM project, the DMALL Member system will not participate directly. Instead, it completes the transaction process by the POS identifying member information. DMALL Points system can support the setting of basic points rules, calculate the data of points and points rewards in the settlement process, and synchronize the data with SM through the interface.

title: 3. Function Description > 3.2 Promotion Type > 3.2.3 Bank Promotion Barcode
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

SM supports tender discount.

2.To-Be Overview

DMALL supports tender discount, via bank promotion barcode.

3.To-Be Flow

4.TO-BE Description

For SM's "Promo per tender" promotional format, such as "Get FREE Item with a minimum grocery item worth P3,000 using BDO Credit Card E-VOUCHER PROMO", DMALL will support the Tender discount/ bank promotion to correspond to it. Operators need to configure a regular promotion with a bank promotion, and configure tender type. For example, create the promotion as "Get FREE Item with a minimum order worth P3,000" (it's a basket promotion), and correspond it to a tender type and barcode that can be downloaded.

Then, the promotion creator will send the barcode and promotion information to the store cashiers.

In the POS settlement process, the cashier can manually remind the customer that they can enjoy a discount if they pay with a specified tender type when the customer’s order may hit the tender discount promotion(e.g., order reach P3,000). If the customer agrees, the cashier can scan the barcode on the POS, and the system will try to hit the promotion and provide the customer with a discount. When the customer pays, the cashier supervises the customer to use the agreed payment method(e.g., BDO card). If the customer refuses to use the specified payment method(e.g., BDO card) at this time, the cashier will roll back the settlement process, cancel the promotion corresponding to the B, and settle again.

The management of bank promotion barcodes can be viewed in the Promotion configuration below (3.5.2.1).

The SM's discount types of E-Vouchers is similar to DMALL Regular Promotions in scope. Therefore, DMALL's promotion system directly supports the management of E-Voucher discounts. It enables the generation of E-Voucher codes, and the E-Voucher data is synchronized with SM for customer distribution. During the settlement process at the POS, E-Vouchers are recognized as promotions, participating in hitting and pricing calculations.

Refer to sections 3.4 for the management process, 3.5.1 for the operational interface, 3.6.4 for pricing logic, and 3.7.2 for the settlement logic at the POS.

title: 3. Function Description > 3.2 Promotion Type > 3.2.5 Next Purchase Coupon
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

SM supports printing certain content on the tailend receipt but does not support promotional

methods that can be automatically triggered by POS scanning on the receipt.

2.To-Be Overview

DMALL, supports delivering promotions to eligible customers in the form of Next Purchase Coupon, encouraging them to make repeat purchases. This coupon also allows direct scanning and automatic activation at the POS.

3.To-Be Flow

4.TO-BE Description

DMALL supports the marketing strategy of "Next purchase coupon", where paper coupons carry the discount of Regular Promotions. These coupons are printed on the tailend receipt after POS settlement. Upon receiving a next purchase coupon, customers can use it in next transactions to enjoy the corresponding promotional activities.

The promotion type for next purchase coupon aligns with Regular Promotion. The operational page for creating next purchase coupon is detailed in section 3.5.1, allowing direct management within the promotion activity. The conditions for coupons distribution are configured in the Marketing toolset page, as explained in section 3.5.2. Refer to section 3.6.4 for pricing logic and section 3.7.2 for settlement logic at the POS.

Meanwhile, it's important to note that DMALL's Next purchase coupon supports direct scanning of its Barcode or QR code by the POS for identification. A common coupon style is depicted in the image below. Moreover, DMALL's system supports a degree of style management for the display of next purchase coupon and additional points reward on the receipt. DMALL's product staff can assist SM in configuring these styles. If SM has specific style requirements, further discussions and confirmations can be conducted.

5.Exceptional Scenario

Not applicable

6.Related Reports

Promotion Performance

7.Key Changes

NO Changes

title: 3. Function Description > 3.2 Promotion Type > 3.2.6 Double Receipt Freebie
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

In addition to the freebies normally acquired and redeemed through promotional activities (such as 'Buy X Get Y') at the Point of Sale (POS), SM also handle a kind of Special Managed Freebies, These freebies, due to theft prevention, vendor considerations, etc., are managed in a dedicated area, called redemption booth, within the store. Customers need to collect them in redemption booth with a tailend receipt after their transaction.

2.To-Be Overview

DMALL aims to accomplish the goal of this kind of freebies and their inventory management through the product form of "double receipt freebie" in Marketing toolset system, you can view the detail in 3.5.2.2 below.

3.To-Be Flow

4.TO-BE Description

DMALL supports the marketing strategy of "double receipt freebie",  where paper vouchers carry information about freebies. These vouchers are printed on the tailend receipt after POS settlement. Upon receiving a "double receipt freebie", customers can use it to collect freebies in the redemption booth.

The operational page for creating  "double receipt freebie" is detailed in section 3.5.2.2.

Meanwhile, it's important to note that "double receipt freebie" code need to be input manually by SM’s operator or manager in redemption booth. A common voucher style is depicted in the image below. Moreover, DMALL's system supports a degree of style management for the display of voucher. If SM has specific style requirements, further discussions and confirmations can be conducted.

When an order is completed on the POS, the marketing toolset system listens to POS order data to determine if the conditions for the "double receipt freebie" award type are met. These include reaching the marketing activity threshold (like buying specific articles or reaching a certain amount) and whether there’s enough remaining freebie vouchers. If the activity is hit, a tailend receipt is printed with a unique double receipt freebie code.

title: 3. Function Description > 3.2 Promotion Type > 3.2.6 Double Receipt Freebie
content:
Customers bring the tailend receipt to the redemption booth to exchange for freebies. The store manager opens the DMALL Freebie Distribution and Inventory Management page, enters the double receipt freebie code information to record the usage of code.

5.Exceptional Scenario

title: 3. Function Description > 3.3 Promotion Management Process > 3.3.1 Promotion Creation and Approval Process
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

SM supports promotion creation, but the approval process is offline.

2.To-Be Overview

DMALL supports promotion creation, and the approval process is online.

3.To-Be Flow

4.TO-BE Description

SM chooses Manual Approval mode and supports multiple approval flows. When it is manual approval, only operators with the approver role or account can approve the promotion activities on the approval page.

Creation：

The operator can create promotion activities by selecting the promotion type, setting the promotion rules and various other options.

You can learn more about this in the Promotion Management section below.

Approval flow：

Approval flow configuration: DMALL's task system supports creating a corresponding Flowchart for SM's promotion approval. The Flowchart allows for multiple approval flows based on conditions. Each flow can have multiple approval nodes, with designated approvers and specific logic. This configuration can be very flexible.

When operator completes the configuration of promotion, he can choose the approval flow. If the promotion is created for testing, which is only used in testing stores, the approval flow can be skipped.

The approval flow needs to be completed before the promotion starts, otherwise the system will default to reject this promotion activity.

The approver can approve or reject the promotion activity.

If approved, the promotion activity can be executed normally.

In the pending review status, if the creator can set the promotion activity to a voided status, and then there is no need to approve it again.

You can learn more about this in the Promotion Management section below.

5.Exceptional Scenario

Not applicable

6.Related Reports

Not applicable

DMALL supports E-voucher.

3.To-Be Flow

Overall Flow  

 

 

E-voucher Creation & Issuance New Process

4.TO-BE Description

In DMALL’s promotion system, the promotion activities are link to the Voucher batch Ids, like B1. Each batch Id has one promotion Id. In practical development, the handling of promotion Id and batch Id may be merged. Please refer to the subsequent API documentation from DMALL for accurate information.

The validity and issuance info of all Voucher codes is managed uniformly in DMALL system.

E-vouchers which are created in the DMALL promotion system:

DMALL will integrate the new E-voucher codes, promotion information and other data into the SM DAC/ E-voucher system. The method prefers flat files, which will be determined in the integration section. The data fields include the following :

Batch Id / promotion Id(one batch ID contains multiple E-voucher codes)

E-voucher Codes (all codes, not code range)

Promotion Info (one batch id has one promotion info)

Legacy E-vouchers which were created in the SM DAC/ E-voucher system:

SM needs to integrate the legacy E-voucher and corresponding codes, promotion information and other data to Dmall. The method prefers flat files,  which will be determined in the integration section. The data fields include the following :

Batch ID (one batch ID contains multiple E-voucher codes)

E-voucher Codes (all codes, not code range)

Promotion Info (one batch id has one promotion info)

content:
1.Promotion List

Prototypes of Pages & Interactions | Details
Promotion List Add Notification | The promotion activity list displays all created promotion activities. On this page, you can also create new promotion activities and select specific promotion activities to view and manage. Promotion activities are distinguished by the "Promotion Id" field. Different operators can have controlled access to this page and different promotions based on their permissions. The status of promotion activities includes: Pending Review: The status where the promotion activity has been created but not yet approved. Approved: The status where the promotion activity has been approved but not yet started. Approval Rejected: The status where the promotion activity has not been approved. Ongoing: The status where the promotion activity is ongoing. Terminated: The status where the promotion activity has been forcibly terminated. Ended: The status where the activity has ended normally. Voided: The status where the promotion activity has been terminated before approval. Different promotion activity statuses allow for different operations, including: Modify: The promotion activity can be modified before it starts, which will redirect to the Modification page. Void: The operation to terminate the promotion activity before approval. Terminate: The operation to terminate the promotion activity after approval. Copy: The content of the promotion activity can be copied in any status to quickly create new promotion activities. Voucher management: If the promotion activity corresponds to E-voucher or Next purchase coupon, a button will be available after the promotion activity is created, which will redirect to the voucher list page. Approval flow: Enter the approval flow detail page . Get the details from the below section of 3.5.1.4. The first step is the Promotion Activity Configuration, and the second step is the Promotion Overview. The second step is used for configuring related to approvals. Promotion basic info allows you to set the promotion activity name and activity time. The activity time can be set as "Long-term validity." In the rule settings of Unit promotion, you can set the discount types of Deduction, %Off, and Special Price. You can also manually select articles or import articles in bulk and set the corresponding reward value. These promotion descriptions will appear on the POS terminal, reminding the promotion label (such as “X% Off” in Unit Promotion), message (such as “Buy M units get free gifts” in Buy X get Y), limit description (such as “The offer is limited to X times per order.”), and disclaimer (such as “While stocks last.”). You can choose “System default description”, which means the text of the promotion label, promotion message, purchase limit description and disclaimer are generated by the system according to the promotion configuration, but they do not include the brand, activity advertisement content and the specific article name. If you need to display this content, you can customize the description. 3.Buy X Get Y

Prototypes of Pages & Interactions | Details
Buy X get Y Store-level Configuration | In the activity rule, you can select the main article X and freebie article Y, where Y can be multiple articles. Both X and Y can be multiple items and are configurable. In the "Buy X get Y" promotion, article X can be a weighable article, while item Y, the freebie, cannot be a weighable article. Store-level configuration: Buy X get Y supports store-level configuration. If enabled, the operator can set store-level quantities for both product X and gift Y, including the option to set a total quantity limit for gift Y at the store level. Batch import is also supported for store-level configuration. At this point, there is no need to configure "Available stores" in the Activity limit. And the articles and freebies of this promotion type can be imported in batches, and store-level ones also supports batch import. Since the pricing order of the Buy X get Y type of promotion is after the Unit promotion, and it is configurable, you can configure in the Stack rule whether this promotion activity can be stacked with the Unit promotion. If not selected, it means that the main article X cannot hit this promotion activity if it has already hit a Unit promotion. Notes: For SM, the field "Stack with other promotion activity" are set to not enable, by default, and can only be turned on after manual selection. Note: In the Stack rule, whether different promotion types can be stacked with the preceding promotion types in the pricing process can be understood through the stacking rules explained in the Pricing section below. This will not be explained again in the subsequent promotion types. Therefore, the subsequent explanations of each promotion type will mainly focus on the activity rule section.

Both forms have multiple discount types, and you can refer to the Promotion Type section for details. For Amount promotion, you can configure the threshold amount and discount. Promotional articles support various article dimensions and batch importing. That is, when promotional articles reach the threshold amount, they can enjoy corresponding discounts. For Quantity promotion, you can configure the threshold quantity and discount. Promotional articles support various article dimensions and batch importing. However, pay attention to the "Promotional Articles Range Type" field. When its value is "Same article", it means that when the quantity of a single article in the promotional articles reaches the threshold quantity, they can enjoy corresponding discounts. When its value is "Any article", it means that when the sum of the quantities of any articles in the promotional articles reaches the threshold quantity, they can enjoy corresponding discounts. When the discount type of condition promotion is Deduction, %Off, or Special price, the promotion also supports multi-tier. In addition, when it is not multi-tier, you can configure double discounts. When the discount type of condition promotion is PWP, operator can configure whether to support "Double receipt PWP" If enabled, it means that when this promotion is triggered at the POS, a tailend receipt will be printed, indicating the PWP articles. content:
6.Basket Promotion

Prototypes of Pages & Interactions | Details
The basic logic of Basket promotion is consistent with Amount promotion. The difference lies in the fact that promotional articles default to all articles.

7.Step 2 Configuration - Promotion Overview

Prototypes of Pages & Interactions | Details
Promotion Overview Skip approval flow | On the page for creating promotional activities, after completing the basic configuration of the promotion, a 'Promotion Overview' step will be added. Before saving the promotion, this step supports the SM promotion type configuration, approval flow configuration. 'SM Promotion Type' is the promotion type in SM's current business, used to determine the appropriate Approval Flow, necessary SM-specific fields, and might be used for dashboard statistics. 'Approval Flow' field allows operators to manually select a specific approval flow. If the selected stores in the basic configuration of the promotion activity in Step 1 include only test stores, the Approval Flow selection here can be set to skip approval, then the promotion status can be approved directly, this function is designed to assist SM in promotional testing work.

8.Promotion Modification

heading_path: 3.5.1.1 Promotion List & Basic Type Configuration & Modification

title:
3. Function Description > 3.5 Promotion Management & Operation
3.5.1 Promotion Management > 3.5.1.1 Promotion List & Basic Type Configuration & Modification
content:
Prototypes of Pages & Interactions | Details
When the promotion activity is in the Pending Review and Approved statuses, the promotion activity can be modified. However, in the Pending Review status, the modification page is similar to the creation page, and almost all fields of the promotion activity can be modified. But when in the Approved status, the modification page of the promotion will control the fields that can be modified, only supporting modifications to the period, stores, and promotion articles. The operator can choose the fields to be modified and then make the modifications. In the Pending Review status, the modification logic of the period and stores is the same as during creation. But the modification of promotion articles only supports addition or deletion of articles. In addition, if during creation, the promotional articles were selected based on the article dimensions of brand, category, and tag, the promotional articles cannot be modified.

heading_path: 3.5.1.1 Promotion List & Basic Type Configuration & Modification

title:
3. Function Description > 3.5 Promotion Management & Operation
3.5.1 Promotion Management > 3.5.1.1 Promotion List & Basic Type Configuration & Modification > 3.5.1.2 Promotion Voucher Configuration
content:
There are multiple types of Promotion vouchers: E-voucher, Voucher on receipt and Bank promotion voucher. The issuance and usage processes for them are described above. The configuration details are as follows:

1.E-voucher

Prototypes of Pages & Interactions | Details
Promotion voucher -- E-voucher | E-voucher: There are two ways to generate E-voucher codes: "System generate automatically" and "Import from existing batch". If "System generate automatically" is selected, just like the voucher on receipt. If "Import from existing batch" is selected, the system will obtain data from the E-voucher database integrated with the merchant, provide filtering for existing batches, and then match the corresponding codes. Notes: the maximum usage times of each codes can only be 1 in this phase. Supports the configuration of Display name and Rule description, which are used to display the information of the coupon on the front end.

2.Voucher on Receipt

Prototypes of Pages & Interactions | Details
Promotion voucher -- Next purchase coupon | The codes of Next purchase coupon only has the "System generate automatically" generation method. the system will generate the corresponding number of non-repeating codes according to the total number of codes and the rules agreed upon with the merchant. Promotion codes can be set with "Maximum Usage times of Each Code," which is the number of times each code can be used. Notes: the maximum usage times of each codes can only be 1 in this phase.

3.Voucher List

content:
Prototypes of Pages & Interactions | Details
Voucher list --Next purchase coupon Voucher list --E-voucher Redeemed Detail | You can view the list of voucher codes under a specific promotion activity, and support viewing the issuance and usage of codes. For different types of Promotion vouchers, the next purchase coupon, E-voucher will differ in the displayed data. Since the next purchase coupon is all issued on the DMALL side, there will be data display of issuance, while E-voucher is issued on the SM side, so there is no related data here. If the Promotion voucher code is used in the order, it has the corresponding Redeemed Detail button. Clicking it can display the time and order details of each redemption of the code.

Tags such as Value Pack, etc. All these article dimensions can be multi-selected. For example, multi-selecting tags means that any article with the selected tags is a promotional article, as shown in the left figure. Add the "Vendor & Category" dimension to the existing article configuration for promotional activities that support the "Category" dimension, allowing for the selection and configuration of multiple vendors and categories. Just like the left figure. If multiple vendors and categories are selected, it means that articles from all the selected categories of these vendors participate in promotional activities.

heading_path: 3.5.1.1 Promotion List & Basic Type Configuration & Modification

title:
3. Function Description > 3.5 Promotion Management & Operation
3.5.1 Promotion Management > 3.5.1.1 Promotion List & Basic Type Configuration & Modification > 3.5.1.3 More Configuration Options
content:
5.Offline Approval Support

To support SM operator in exporting promotion activities for offline approval, DMALL's Promotion Management will incorporate specific fields for SM approval and make some product adjustments. Once the configuration of a promotion is complete, operators can export documents for offline approval. These documents will be designed according to the template style preferred by SMs, allowing for printing and subsequent offline approval.

This feature details requires more discussions with SM regarding their offline approval template styles and fields. Therefore, the implementation details are not specified in this Blueprint document at the moment. These details will be compiled into the relevant documents after aligning with the appropriate SM personnel.

6. [Ongoing] For the Promotion Weighable Article as Freebie

Currently DMALL does not support weighable articles as freebies, but SM has the requirement to distinguish articles that are bundled with freebies from the articles that are not bundled with freebies in the order data and report, and which is used for Fresh pre-bundled promotion items.

The current proposed solution involves printing a generic barcode and sticking it next to the barcode of the article that is bundled with freebie. At the POS, both barcodes will be scanned consecutively to identify that the article is bundled with freebie.

However, this solution is still under discussion and has not been fully finalized. Therefore, this requirement will be conclusively determined in P2 and is expected to be completed by September 2024.

heading_path: 3.5.1.1 Promotion List & Basic Type Configuration & Modification

title:
3. Function Description > 3.5 Promotion Management & Operation
3.5.1 Promotion Management > 3.5.1.1 Promotion List & Basic Type Configuration & Modification > 3.5.1.4 Approval Flow
content:
Prototypes of Pages & Interactions | Details
Approval flow configuration Approval Task List Approval Flow Details Page | Approval flow configuration: DMALL's task system supports creating a corresponding Flowchart for SM's promotion approvals. The Flowchart allows for multiple approval flows based on conditions. Each flow can have multiple approval nodes, with designated approvers and specific logic. This configuration can be very flexible Promotion Approval Process: Approval flow: After creation, promotions enter the approval process, using the configured Approval flow. Notification to approver: When it comes to each approval node, DMALL's task system sends DMALL notifications to the approvers' accounts. Notification to creator and related staff: When all approvers have approved the promotion activity, the promotion transitions to the approved status. The system notifies the creator and relevant staff, including Merchandising, Marketing, and Supply Chain teams. SM needs to explicitly provide the account information for these staff members for further communication. Approval task list：Approvers can view approval task list on the task system page, click details to access the specific promotion approval page, view details, and approve or reject. This list can support "Approve in batch" or "Reject in batch". After approval at each node, it automatically moves to the next node. Approved status：If all nodes approve, the promotion is approved. If any node rejects, the status becomes 'Approval Rejected'. If 'Approval Rejected', creators can use the 'Copy' function of promotion list to recreate and resubmit for approval. Both approval and rejection notifications are sent to creators by DMALL's task system.

5.Exceptional Scenario

In the Rules setting, you can configure the companies, stores, customers range just like the promotion activity, and configure the article range and Minimum purchase amount. You can set All articles, Designated articles, or Exclude articles. If you choose Designated articles, you can choose the specific method of selecting articles, such as directly selecting, selecting by brand or category, vendor&category, tags, etc. The specific selection method is similar to that in the promotion activity mentioned above. Multiple selected articles mean that the total amount or quantity of these articles in the order reaches the minimum purchase amount or quantity to enjoy the reward. If the condition to be set is that the order contains certain articles to enjoy the reward, then set the minimum purchase amount to 0. In the Reward setting, you can choose the form of the reward to be Points, next purchase coupon, double receipt freebie and advertising. If the reward is Points, you can choose Points types, including "Extra points" and "N times points for orders". Additionally, if the article is selected by the Designated articles method, you can also choose "N times points for selected articles". "N times points for orders" means that N times the points corresponding to the total amount of the order are issued in the order. "N times points for selected articles" means that N times the points only apply to the selected articles. Both of these methods require setting Points multiple N. For "Extra points", you also need to set its value. In addition, if the reward is Poin

title:
3. Function Description > 3.5 Promotion Management & Operation
3.5.1 Promotion Management > 3.5.4 [Label System] Article Tag Management
content:
Note: This section will be supported in a DMALL new system, label system, and the solution temporarily is written in the blueprint of the promotion.

1. AS-IS Overview

SM supports multiple temporary tags.

2.To-Be Overview

DMALL supports SM’s multiple temporary tags.

3.To-Be Flow

Not applicable

4.TO-BE Description

The operator can upload a spreadsheet with all relevant articles and corresponding effective stores to create temporary tags for the articles of specific stores.  The following tags are included by default:

Buy Me/ Mailer

Other sponsor tag—Corporate promo, including:

Seasonal

Launchpad

Product Highlight

In-store Activation (Streetfood Festival, Treat Yourself, Kids Kitchen, Complete Home)

These temporary tags, and permanent tags (including Value Pack/SC etc.), can be used as a way to select promotional articles for promotion activities. See above for details.

Configuration

DMALL will provide a new page for managing these tags, supporting the display of a tag list and enabling the custom creation of new tags. Additionally, it will allow for the querying and maintenance of tags to store level.The above-mentioned "Buy me/Mailer" and a few other corporate promo tags will be provided by default. In addition, DMALL also allow operator to create tags manually.

Store-specific articles and their validity periods can be added either manually or in bulk through file import. To add store-specific articles, operator needs to import columns for articles, stores, and start and end times of the validity period under the specified tag's detail page. The data uploaded in the second batch will not automatically overwrite the data from the first batch. Store-specific articles that are not yet effective（Inactive） can be deleted, while those that are active can be changed to inactive. Editing of the start and end times of the validity period is possible until the article becomes expired (for active articles, the start time cannot be edited).

4.TO-BE Description

The pricing rules & logic are based on the current DMALL rules and logic. The basic pricing process when an order hits multiple activities with different types, is like:

Notes: As mentioned above, for E-vouchers, Next purchase coupon, or Bank promotion, the DMALL Promotion system will convert them into corresponding promotion activities. Therefore, there is no specific explanation in the pricing process for them. But the priority of this kind of promotion has some special rules:

The priority of promotion activities which are converted from E-vouchers, next purchase coupon, or bank promotion will be the highest, which would be higher than the general promotion activities. It means that if articles have multiple activities of the same type, the activities which were converted from E-vouchers, next purchase coupon, or bank promotion will be hit.

title: 3. Function Description > 3.6 Pricing > 3.6.2 Stacking Rule
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

SM’s pricing logic.

2.To-Be Overview

DMALL’s pricing logic

3.To-Be Flow

Not applicable

4.TO-BE Description

For each promotion type, each article will only hit at most one promotion activity in the pricing process. The rules for hitting which activity include the Best offer rule (BOR), Normal priority rule (NPR), Special priority rule (SPR) shown in the figure above. And when an article hits a promotion activity of one type, whether it can still hit the promotion activities of subsequent types, the rules are shown in the table below:

Unit promo | Buy x get Y | Match promo | Condition promo | Basket promo | Payment Promo
Unit promo | BOR | Config | √
Buy x get Y | NPR | Config | √
Match promo | NPR | × | √
Condition promo | SPR | √
Basket promo | SPR | √
Payment Promo | NPR

“√”（tick） :means that when the article hits other types of promotion activities, it can still hit this type of promotion.

“×” （cross）:means that if the article has already hit the match promotion, it cannot hit the condition promotion again.

Config:Configurable, means that the promotion activity can be configured to allow or not allow the article to hit this activity when it hits the previous types of promotion activities.

BOR: Best offer rule. If the priorities are different, higher priority will be applied; If the priorities are the same, the best offer rule will be applied.

The priority of the general promotion activity can be customized and configured. The enumeration values include very high, high, normal, low, and very low.

NPR: Normal priority rule. If the priorities are different, higher priority will be applied; If the priorities are the same, the last created promotion will be chosen.

title: 3. Function Description > 3.6 Pricing > 3.6.2 Stacking Rule
content:
SPR: Special priority rule. If the priorities are different, higher priority will be applied; If the priorities are the same, Condition/Basket promo with freebie > Condition/Basket promo with redemption > other condition/basket promotion reaching specific amount / quantity. If still the same,the last created promotion will be chosen.

Background information

Articles | A: Coke: Original unit price = $5 B: Soda: Original unit price = $10 C: Potato Chips: Original unit price= $15 D: Cola: Original unit price = $1 E: Coffee: Original unit price = $10
Promotions | Promo1: Unit promo: Buy C at special price $10 Promo2: Unit promo: Buy C at 20% off Promo1 & Promo2 have the same priority. Promo3: Buy X get Y: Buy C get D for Free Configuration: Stackable with Unit promo Promo4: Match promo: Buy Bundle(2A+E) at $16 Promo5: Condition promo: Buy 2A at 10%off Promo6: Basket promo: Tier 1: Buy over $10, get $2 off Tier 2: Buy over $30, get $9 off Promo7: Payment promo: Use Visa to buy over $20, get 20% off
What a customer buy | 2A+B+C+D+E And pay with Visa

Pricing

title: 3. Function Description > 3.6 Pricing > 3.6.3 Pricing Example
content:
Promotion Type | Pricing | After Promotion
Unit Promo | C have Promo1 & Promo2. If Promo1, C : $10; If Promo2, C : $(15×20%)=$12. And Promo1 & Promo2 have the same priority, based on the best offer rule(BOR). C can enjoy Promo1, but not Promo2. | C: $10
Buy X get Y | C&D have Promo3 Promo3 is stackable with Unit promo C&D can enjoy Promo3 D will be a freebie | D: $0
Match Promo | 2A&E can enjoy Promo4 Discount amount = $(5×2+10-16) =$4 The discount amount will be distributed proportionally between 2A and E, so each A can enjoy $1 off, E can enjoy $2 off. | A: $4 E: $8
Condition Promo | 2A have Promo5 But 2A have already enjoyed the match promo, and condition promo is not stackable with match promo, so 2A cannot enjoy Promo5
Basket Promo | 2A+B+C+D+E: total price: $(2*4+10+10+0+8)=$36 >$30 The order can enjoy Promo6 and Tier2. Discount amount = $9 The discount amount will be distributed proportionally between 2A+B+C+D+E, so each A can enjoy $1 off, B enjoy $2.5 off, C enjoy $2.5 off, D enjoy 0 off, E enjoy $2 off. | A: $3 B: $7.5 C: $7.5 D: $0 E: $6 Total: $27
Payment Promo | The total price:$27 > $20, and the payment channel is Visa. Order can enjoy Promo7. Each article enjoys 20% off. | A: $2.4 B: $5 C: $5 D: $0 E: $4.8 Total: $21.6

title: 3. Function Description > 3.6 Pricing > 3.6.4 Pricing Logic with Promotion Voucher
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

SM’s pricing logic.

2.To-Be Overview

DMALL’s pricing logic

3.To-Be Flow

Not applicable

4.TO-BE Description

In DMALL system, Voucher, E-vouchers, next purchase coupon, or bank promotion

will correspond to specific promotion, each order can support multiple vouchers, the order pricing logic with vouchers is basically the same as order with general promotions, the difference is: In DMALL’s system, promotion activities corresponding voucher has a higher priority than others without voucher of the same promotion type. For example, if the promotion corresponding to the voucher is Buy A get B, and there is another promotion which is Buy A get C, and if the order contains articles A, B, and C, it will hit Buy A get B only, but not Buy A get C.

In the DMALL pricing rules, an article can generally only hit one promotion under the same promotion type. So in this solution, if article A has a general unit promotion: Buy A get $5 off and a voucher: Buy A get $8 off, then in the order, article A may only hit the voucher,and get $8 off, not get $(8+5) off.

More Examples:

SMs pricing logic | DMALLs pricing logic | Subject to DMALLs pricing logic. | IT/Marketing | All companies in scope.

title: 3. Function Description > 3.7 POS Order Settlement & Pricing Execution Process > 3.7.1 POS Execution Process with Marketing Toolset awarding
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

SM supports the possibility of hitting promotions at the time of POS settlement and issuing points to customers.

2.To-Be Overview

DMALL supports the possibility of hitting promotions at the time of POS settlement and issuing points to customers. And DMALL will call the API of SM to issue or reclaim. At the same time, DMALL can issue other kinds of marketing rewards like Next purchase coupon, Double receipt freebie, Advertising.

3.To-Be Flow

4.TO-BE Description

Promotion pricing: Promotion system will judge the hit of promotion activities based on articles and activity info, and then calculate the price of order.

Points awarded:

After payment, DMALL will calculate the points numbers that should be issued based on basic points rules, and then call SM's Points Awarding interface to issue membership the points.

And if the order hits the points reward rule, DMALL Marketing Toolset system will call SM's Points Awarding interface to issue membership extra points and N times points, and print corresponding info on the tailend receipt.

Points awarding reversal:

If order are returned or refund on POS, DMALL will call SM’s Points Awarding Reversal API, pass in the membership, order and refund information. Then SM reclaims the points.

Notes: As above, the points awarding and reversal requires real-time calling of SM’s interface. When the store network is error, this process can not be performed. Therefore, points can not be issued in this case.

Next purchase coupon, Double receipt freebie, Advertising awarded on tailend reiceipt: After payment, DMALL Marketing Toolset system will monitor order information. If the order qualifies for marketing activities with relevant reward types, the Marketing Toolset system will print the corresponding reward information on the tailend receipt. These reward types, in addition to the previously mentioned points, also include Next purchase coupon, Double receipt freebie, Advertising.

5.Exceptional Scenario

title: 3. Function Description > 3.7 POS Order Settlement & Pricing Execution Process > 3.7.2 Execution Process with E-Voucher
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

SM supports the use of E-Vouchers at the time of POS settlement to give discounts to customers.

2.To-Be Overview

DMALL supports the use of E-Vouchers and tailend receipt at the time of POS settlement to give discounts to customers. And DMALL will call the API of SM to redeem or restore E-vouchers.

3.To-Be Flow

E-voucher Handling details Process in DMALL POS  

 

 

 E-voucher handling Process in SM Legacy POS

4.TO-BE Description

DMALL POS:

E-voucher code: The E-voucher code can be entered or scanned at the POS, and DMALL's promotion system can judge its validity and use the code to identify the corresponding promotion activity, enabling the hitting and pricing of the promotion.

Calculating order: As described in Section 3.6.4 above,, if voucher and regular promotion triggers the same type of promo, the voucher, including E-voucher and voucher on tailend receipt, always has higher priority as it's the discount that customers actively requested, making it more likely to be hit. And multiple Voucher codes can be used in one order, which will be converted into corresponding promotion activities for pricing. The pricing detail can be seen above section of pricing logic with Promotion voucher.

SM should provide the following APIs:

E-voucher redeem interface: When the payment is completed at the POS, DMALL's promotion system will invalidate the E-voucher code used in the transaction and call this interface in real-time, passing the code to SM. SM will then mark the corresponding E-voucher as redeemed on the customer's app.

Note: In DMALL's promotion system, if an E-voucher batch corresponding to a promotion activity ends or terminates early, DMALL will also call the E-voucher redeem interface to invalidate these codes. Therefore, this interface needs to support batch calls and distinguish between different calling scenarios.

heading_path: 3.7 POS Order Settlement & Pricing Execution Process

title: 3. Function Description > 3.7 POS Order Settlement & Pricing Execution Process > 3.7.2 Execution Process with E-Voucher
content:
E-voucher reversal interface: In case of order cancellation at the POS, DMALL's promotion system will re-enable the E-voucher code used in the transaction and call this interface, passing the transaction information to SM. SM will restore the corresponding E-voucher on the customer's app.

SM legacy POS:

On the SM legacy POS, E-vouchers can still be used. However, since the validity of E-voucher codes is centrally managed by DMALL's promotion system, DMALL will provide the following APIs to SM:

E-voucher Code Validity Check API: By providing the E-voucher code, this API will determine whether the code is in a valid state.

E-voucher Code Redeemed on SM Side API: When an E-voucher is redeemed on the SM legacy POS, this API is called to inform DMALL about the redeemed status of the code.

E-voucher Code Reversal on SM Side API: In case of order cancellation on the SM legacy POS and the need to restore the codes used in the order, this API is called to inform DMALL about the restoration status of the code.

As above, E-vouchers can not be used when POS has no network connection , since the APIs between SM and DMALL are not available when there is no internet connection.

title: 3. Function Description > 3.7 POS Order Settlement & Pricing Execution Process > 3.7.3 Special Handling in Store for Promotion Execution
content:
1. AS-IS Overview

Not applicable

2.To-Be Overview

Not applicable

3.To-Be Flow

Not applicable

4.TO-BE Description

Here, we summarize consistent special situations that SM may encounter when using DMALL promotions in stores and provide corresponding solutions and suggestions:

Advance IT Testing of Promotion Activities: If SM wishes to perform early validation in test stores before the official start of a promotion, you can:

Copy the promotion activity in the promotion system.

Modify the start and end time for testing to precede the official promotion.

Set the store to a test store.(The premise is that the store code of the test store is created in the master data of DMALL.)

Choose to skip the approval process.

Then, conduct order testing for articles in the test store to verify the effectiveness of the promotion and article discounts.

After IT testing, operator can “Notification” the relevant personnel for further processing (3.5.1.1).

Abnormal Execution or Pricing of Promotion Activities in Stores: This includes issues such as failure to hit promotions, difference between discounted prices and expectations, confusion with stacking rules, and data error when POS is offline. DMALL recommends that SM promptly inform promotion activity managers of through internal communication channels. Managers should assess potential causes based on experience. If the issue cannot be determined or fixed, terminate the promotion activity immediately and contact DMALL's SM business liaison for judgement and resolution.

heading_path: 3.7 POS Order Settlement & Pricing Execution Process
3.7.3 Special Handling in Store for Promotion Execution

title: 3. Function Description > 3.7 POS Order Settlement & Pricing Execution Process > 3.7.3 Special Handling in Store for Promotion Execution
content:
POS Offline: As mentioned above, if the POS network is offline, points awarding and reversal, as well as the use of E-vouchers, promotion activities involving the selection of articles in vendor fields, store-level article purchase limitation, will be interrupted. However, other functions such as the hitting and pricing of promotions, and the printing of vouchers, Freebies, and Paper coupons on the tailend receipt, will not be immediately affected. Nevertheless, if the network remains offline for an extended period, issues may arise as the stores won't receive updated data from the Promotion and Marketing Toolset systems. Therefore, if the POS network is offline, prompt action by the store staff is still necessary. Once the network connection is restored, the Promotion system will also return to normal without additional operation.

For issues like abnormal execution, Pricing of Promotion Activities, or potential problems with store POS systems being offline, DMALL will provide more detailed and explicit guidance documents before delivering the project to SM.

Sales Detail and Negative gross profit indicator

The sales detail report can provide the sales order corresponding to the amount of articles, promotions, discounts, and cost.

The “Sales Detail” function contained two items：Query condition and Display list。Query Condition：It can be using "Company Name"、"Site Name"、"Income Date"、“Order No”、"Business Type"  and so on as condition to query data。 2. Display list：It will display detailed information for sales order , such as business type,amount,transaction date,promotion,discount,site and so on. And the negative gross profit indicator is on of the fields to show the article with gross profit, and you can see the Order No. of the article, and then you can go to the details page of the POS order to view the details of the article hit promotions, so as to determine the reason for the negative gross profit, and then to deal with it.

3.7.3 Special Handling in Store for Promotion Execution

content:
Note: (1)The calculation rule for negative gross profit is that the amount of articles （including tax） minus the promotion amount, minus the discount amount, and the cost amount (including tax) is less than 0. (2)The MAV values corresponding to sales orders are derived from the SAP system and integrated daily, with relatively delayed data. Therefore, the sales gross profit information in the OS is for reference only.
