content:
「SM」_「Phase 1A」_「System Integration Blueprint」_V1.0.0

Editor： | James You | Edit date： | 12/29/2023
Approver： | Approve date：

Copyright Reserved

Document Information

Document Source

This document is intended to present the Integration design, business rules and DMALL system design based on the RFP captured by SM. This document will be available at SM’s SM Market SharePoint directory.

Blueprint Chapter | Module | Catherine Canlas | Katherine Lim | Chanda Osinsao | Romyr Crisolo
3 | High-Level Integration Solution
4.1.2.1 | Master data Detail Integration Design
4.1.2.2 | Store Operations Process Flow
4.1.2.3 | EOD Detail Integration Design

Revision History

Version | Date | Responsible | Change Summary
1.0.0 | 10/18/2023 | DMALL | For Review

1. Blueprint Document Scope

1.1 Background Introduction

This document is for SM digitalization transformation project. This project is divided into 4 phases, and this document is for Phase 1A, and in this phase, DMALL will help SM to switch POS and Inventory related systems. As defined in the project scope, the proposed solution includes feature support for POS, Inventory, Master data structure, Picking and Dashboard.

To support these features, system integrations are needed to provide data exchange between DMALL OS and multiple SM Legacy systems, this document will provide solutions for the system integrations, which will be mainly focusing on these aspects:

Master data, which is the basis for the project.

Inventory(Store Operations Interaction flow), which is for inventory related support features.

End of Day(EOD), for compatibilities with Legacy or External system interactions.

title: System Integration
content:
Note: For more business processes and details, please refer to different blueprint documents for the specific modules. Also, these blueprint documents may be mentioned in this document as [X], for example [Master Data], [Inventory] etc. For integration field mapping during the development, user SHOULD follow the mapping excel file since the file will be up-do-date in the process of development.

2. Definitions

ARC: Data report of SM sales data, without other affiliates sales.

BCP: Business Continuity Plan.

BIR: Bureau of Internal Revenue of Philippines.

BOM: Bill of Materials.

DAC: Digital Advantage Corporation, previously known as MCI(Marketing Convergence Inc, Member point system.

FRS: Food Retail System.

NHO: National Head Office, means headquarters, during communication it’s interchangeable with HQ, or HO. MMS(Retail affiliates MMS): Merchandise Management System, the MajorCon's Masterfile System, User portal for Consigners.

OCCS: Online Contract Creation System.

PCA: Price Change Adjustment.

PDT: Portable Data Terminal.

PIMS: Physical Inventory Management System.

PVT: Price Verification Terminal.

STDR: Stock Transfer Discrepancy Report.

TPS: Tag Printing System.

WS: Weighing Scale.

IBMS: Integration Business Management System (DMALL), also means DMALL Middle Layer.

POS – Point of Sale System.

3. High-Level Integration Solution

3.1 AS-IS System Integration Architecture Diagram

title: System Integration
content:
The diagram above is the original architecture provided by SM for reference, and the following is an AS-IS  diagram based on the current status quo.

As show in the AS-IS Diagram currently system architecture of SM contains the following components:

SAP and related system around the SAP system at HQ, in which several components are used: Master Data Supply Chain System, WMS and FICO etc.

3rd party systems, normally they are SM Group scope systems or external systems such as BDO, Retail Affiliates MMS, Marketing, DAC, Credit Info System, e-Treasury, BIR etc.

Telnet Server at Store, which acts as a key component that exchange data directly or in-directly between Store POS Servers and SAP/NON-SAP systems at HQ, and the Telnet Server also provide utility services for store daily operations, EOD processes, PVT and so on.

POS related software and hardware at Store, includes POS Terminals and different POS Servers(Regular POS Server, Online POS Server, and Roving POS Server, in which the Regular POS Server is run in primary/secondary mode), Weighing Scale, EFT devices etc.

3.2 TO-BE System Integration Architecture Diagram

(Note: Workforce HR Suite is Out of Scope, just for references from the AS-IS.)

As shown in the TO-BE architecture above, the components marked as gray are replacements of old components, and red components are newly introduced, the following is a list of the key components, for more detail please reference different Business Module Blueprint separately:

DMALL OS Service(SM Private Cloud)

Omni-channel Master Data. Includes mainly the Article Base Info, Merchandise Hierarchy, Brand, Article Package, Article Package Barcode, BOM, Site, Site-Article, Site-Article-Price, Vendor, Purchase Information and Source Information.

Promotion Management. Includes related promotion settings, rules and different promotion price algorithms that should be applied in the sales process.

title: System Integration
content:
Inventory Management. Includes Goods Receiving, Goods Issuance, STDR, Store-To-Store Transfer, OSA, Stock Take, Expiry, Gap Check, Stock Transfer, Production, Dismantle, Conversion, Stock Adjust.

Big Data & Dashboard. Includes the Big Data and BI reports features provided by DMALL OS.

Omni-channel Inventory. Includes the real-time inventory data result.

Omni-channel Sales Order. Includes the real-time sales order data that processed in DMALL OS.

POS Service(Realtime Data & Report EOD Online). Includes different POS services for DMALL Cloud based POS system.

TPS, please note that in the diagram it shows as a replacement component of the AS-IS in the same place, but it will be a DMALL Cloud Service in the TO-BE phase, user can access it through DMALL partner web user interfaces.

DMALL partner

PC Web Browser UI, to support Store and HQ daily operations in web browser on a Personal Computer.

PDT, to support Store daily operations on Mobile Devices(Android and iOS).

In-Store POS Servers, more features listed:

Data Backup

BCP USB Copy after Download from Cloud

EOD USB Copy then Upload to Cloud

IBMS(Also known as: DMALL Middle Layer) Includes JCo client/gateway components and FTP. It is the main components that deployed between DMALL OS and the exist SAP and Non SAP Legacy systems.

title: System Integration
content:
Picking. Includes Picking, Delivery and related Online to Offline fulfillment features to support online sales.(Integration of Picking and new e-Commerce will be in the new e-Commerce stage, Picking will not integrate with legacy e-Commerce, so Picking is described separately here).

3.3 Integration Gap Analysis

Legacy POS and FRS will be taken offline after DMALL OS goes online. The original features of Legacy POS and FRS are replaced by the systems of DMALL OS. The original integration of SM with systems such as SAP, Retail Affiliates MMS, DAC, ARC, BIR, e-Treasury, Credit Info System, Mall sales and so on will be switched to the integration of DMALL OS with these systems.

For the “No.” column in the table below, refer to 3.1 AS-IS System Architecture Diagram.

title: System Integration
content:
3.4 Technical Considerations for To-Be Integration Design

3.4.1 Data Integration Approach：

SFTP (Secured File Transfer Protocol)

For the compatibilities with SM existing systems and data flows, DMALL will follow SM existing FTP conventions as long as they are considered safe and secure.

SM provides the SFTP Server for data integration.

For the new FTP based data exchange, we suggest both SM and DMALL follow the following rules:

No. | Rules contents
1 | Both parties agree to the file uploading directory, such as /upload/business.
2 | The party who writes the data, first creates a temp directory in the above directory.
3 | Write all the data files in the temp directory or its subdirectories.
4 | After all the files are written, the operator needs to rename the temp directory to the final directory.
5 | Both parties agree on the data reading time (UTC+8). Data modification is allowed before the reading time. Data updates after the reading time need to be manually processed after the data reading party confirms the updates.
6 | Before the data is read, add the current processing timestamp as a prefix for all files in the final directory, and then move the files to the bak directory to prevent the data from being modified during the reading process, the files which timestamp is before 31 days in the bak directory will be archived to ./bak/${YYYYMM} directory early morning on the first day of every month, and the retention period is half a year. After the data is processed, if there is still content to be processed in the final directory, the content is considered as a change after the file reading and needs to be processed manually.
7 | If multiple files need to be uploaded, it is recommended to compress the files first before uploading. The .zip compression format is recommended.
8 | Unified UTF-8 encoding is required for files.
9 | Merchants who want to access the SFTP server need to apply for an account and whitelist from DMALL.

SAP Connector

title: System Integration
content:
DMALL Middle Layer should offer this capability. And to maintain maximized security and audition, the JCo Client env(multiple nodes/instances for failover) should be a single access point that access any SM SAP services.

SM has 3 different SAP env, dev/QA/Prod.

100 JCo(to replace the old FRS) user ids for 300 stores; (1 sap key for 1 FRS user id)

For now an SAP-Frontend connects to a pre-assigned App Server on a pre-allocated store/region basis, and the DMALL system should follow the existing SM server routing rule to maintain stability.

3.4.2 Performance &  Stability Consideration

Running performance & stability monitoring:

Each DMALL OS system will add event tracking and monitoring in key links such as interface interaction, database access and file transfer, collect relevant performance indicators in real time.

And will notify the IT maintainer of DMALL OS system through a unified alarm mechanism if the link exceeds the threshold, so as to intervene in troubleshooting problems and eliminate hidden dangers at the first time.

title: System Integration
content:
DMALL IT maintainers will operate on the issues as soon as possible when the situation occurs. And we will notify SM the first time to solve the problem together if necessary.

The interaction of interface data and transmission of file contents needs to be completed within the time limit agreed by both parties, subject to the detailed integration plan.

3.4.3 Security Consideration

SFTP:

As SM provides the SFTP server, we recommend that SM IT team ensure the security and high availability of SFTP server, including but not limited to creating individual username/password or SSL certificate for integration to authenticate.

Encrypted communication based on SSH protocol can ensure the communication content will not be eavesdropped on by the third party.

Where applicable, DMALL will follow SM IT standards utilizing Service Accounts.

Https: Use the SSL/TLS protocol for encryption and authentication. And Https Use port 443 by default instead of HTTP's port 80.

JSON based API adopts OAuth user authentication and authorization rules.

3.4.4 Exception Handling Consideration

Under the premise that all the infrastructure such as hardware, network and others work normally, the Exception Retry Mechanism will be applied for the following scenarios automatically:

API Interface failure

SFTP files transfer failure

JCo interface failure

Monitoring matrix for the kinds of API Interface failure:

title: System Integration
content:
No. | Code | Description | Explanation & Solution
1 | 0000 | Success | Response Success
2 | GW4000 | Bad Request | Request Error
3 | GW4010 | Parameter Error | Header Parameters are missing or incorrect, please check the parameters according to General Guide Line and Integration Rules.
4 | GW4011 | Unauthorized | Authentication failure, please check the correct API path and requesting Token.
5 | GW4013 | Token is invalid | Invalid Token, please check Token is correct or not.
6 | GW4014 | Token is invalid | Token expired, client should refresh the token.
7 | GW4015 | Appkey is invalid | Check if appKey is correct.
8 | GW4016 | Signature is invalid | Check if the process of signature is correct or not.
9 | GW4017 | token refresh is fail | Check the parameters to refresh the token is correct.
10 | GW4030 | Unauthorized | Client app is not authorized to use this API.
11 | GW4070 | Unauthorized | The accessing IP address of the client app is not in the white list.
12 | GW4040 | Not Found | No API path found, please check the accessing path.
13 | GW4290 | Too Many Requests | Access too frequently, contact DMALL Support to increase the quota or limit the access rate.
14 | GW5000 | Internal Server Error | Error in Gateway, contact DMALL tech support.
15 | GW5020 | Internal Server Error | No Service provider, contact DMALL tech support.
16 | GW5021 | Internal Server Error | No Service provider, contact DMALL tech support.
17 | GW5030 | Service Unavailable | Service provider busy, normally retry can solve this error.
18 | GW5040 | Gateway Timeout | Service provider busy, normally retry can solve this error.
19 | GW5050 | Internal Server Error | Gateway error contact DMALL tech support.

title: System Integration
content:
The integrated module is connected to the DMALL OS monitoring platform, triggering real-time alarm in case of abnormal transmission.

Both sides of the integration should support retry of the transmission process to ensure that abnormal data can be retransmitted.

3.4.5 Monitoring

During the data exchange processes, when Master Data are sent from SAP to DMALL OS and Inventory related API call from DMALL OS to SAP, the summaries should be logged. And for EOD files(any periodically sent files), when data are sent from DMALL to SAP the summaries should also be logged. During these data transmissions, errors might occur, any abnormalities, failures and exceptions should be caught, logged and sent in an email. The recipient should include the related email groups of business and IT team members.

For JCo API the following KPI will be recorded

StoreId | Interface Name | Direction | Line Count | Success/Failure
<StoreId> | <FM Name> | SAP -> DMALL or DMALL -> SAP | <Line Count> | Success/Failure

All the API based monitoring should cover integration details in 4.1.1 SAP API List.

For file based transfer The following KPI will be recorded

StoreId | File Link Name | Direction | Size | Line Count | Time | Src/Destination Path | Success/Failure
<StoreId> | <Data Link Name or short description> | DMALL -> SAP or SAP -> DMALL | Size | <Line Count> | Time | Full Path | Success/Failure

All the file based monitoring should cover integration details in 4.1.2.3 EOD Detail Integration Design and 4.1.2.1.14 Majorcon Master Data.

Here is a suggested process to follow when Alarm raised.

4. To-Be Detail Integration Design

4.1 SM To-Be Design

4.1.1 API List

content:

Integration after phase 1A switch involves systems such as SAP, Retail Affiliates MMS, DAC, ARC, BIR, e-Treasury, Credit Info System, and Mall sales etc. In the following sub chapters, these integrations are categorized into master data, Inventory and EOD.

If the category is created or changed in SM SAP system, it needs to be synchronized to DMALL system by API.

If the category is removed in SM SAP system, it needs to be synchronized to DMALL system by API.

4.1.2.1.14 Majorcon Master Data

Solution 2:

An item tag logic will be created in article master data to logically separate majorcon and regular articles.

A sales code transfer logic will be created between POS order and Omnichannel order.

Only 1 site price record for site articles if the price is the same for all stores.

Majorcon article codes: It will be MJ+majorcon article code, user can separate the majorcon articles by the prefix MJ

Duplicate barcode between majorcon and outright articles: The data will be filtered and regarded as abnormal data; it won’t be saved in article master data. Exception messages will be included in the integration exception report.

title: System Integration
content:
Delete majorcon articles: To avoid too much outdated majorcon data staying in the system, SM will provide majorcon DELETE files periodically to remove the outdated data.

User can still find the article in the system with the status: Deleted. Delete majorcon articles will not be available to do business.

Sales：All the sales will be counted in the major article code.

Reference Business Definition:  [Master Data] 3.3 Majorcon.

This chapter is designated for the Majorcon master data integration.
Note that the Majorcon data should be Read-Only in DMALL system.

Background: For Majorcon the data source is Retail Affiliates MMS and files are sent to DMALL through FTP, also note that the Japan Home is also included here in this chapter.

Integration Approach: FTP

File naming Format (If SFTP)：N/A

Frequency & Rules: Daily (5PM, 10PM for next day update), configurable by user. And As Needed(when emergency update needed)

Integration Detail Content: See bellow

There are 5 Majorcons maintained in Affiliate system and will be integrated to DMALL system :

Majorcon name | Majorcon code | EAN category
Supplies Station | 25 | Z2
Toy World | 33 | Z2
Nursery Care | 35 | Z7
Miniso | 339 | ZH
Japan Home | 479 | Z2

Once the majorcon code=025, items will be tagged as SC/PWD items, the information will be saved in Article masterdata＞basic information＞Extended information There’s drop down list for user to check. The tag can’t be edited, only can be reviewed for phase 1A.

All majorcon items are VATable, tax rate will be 12% for all majorcon items.

Majorcon vendors don’t manage inventory

To avoid too much outdated majorcon data staying in the system, SM will provide majorcon DELETE files periodically to remove the outdated data.

title: System Integration
content:
4.1.2.2 Store Operations Process Flow

Please note that in process of Bundle and Dismantle, the Premium, The Auto TP process not in Blueprint, but feature will be available in UAT, and for Premium, stay in Store or back to DC, should have master indicator.

for DC, STO manually.

for Store, TP to normal item.

4.1.2.2.1 Goods Receiving from Vendor (DSA)

Reference Business Flow: [Inventory] 3.2 Goods Receiving from Vendor (DSA)

The following are the integration points for this process:

DMALL to retrieve Purchase Order document information from SAP

DMALL to send Goods Receipt information to SAP

a. DMALL to retrieve Purchase Order document information from SAP

Process Flow

Background: Purchase Order (PO’s) documents are created in SAP and the items will be received and recorded in the DMALL system. DMALL system will retrieve all the released PO’s created for the store.

Integration Approach: JCo API

Frequency & Rules:  Daily Scheduled Job; Schedule is configurable by user.

Integration Detail Content: Refer to  (SM)_(Phase 1A)_(System Integration Blueprint)_(Store Operations Mapping)_ver1.0, worksheet named GR-Vendor-PO).

SAP Function Module:

ZRFC_POSTORE_PODOWNLOAD

ZRFC_POSTORE_HEADERITEMTEXT

b. DMALL to send Goods Receipt information to SAP

Process Flow

Background: After the store receives the goods, synchronize the result data to SAP.  Upon receipt of the data, SAP will create a material document to post the received goods.  SAP will send a return message to DMALL system – status and GR number (if “Success”

Integration Approach: JCo API

Frequency & Rules: Real Time, Upon posting of goods receipt in DMALL

Integration Detail Content:   Refer to (SM)_(Phase 1A)_(System Integration Blueprint)_(Store Operations Mapping)_ver1.0, worksheet named GR-Vendor-PO GR).

SAP Function Module:

ZRFC_POSTORE_GOODSRECEIPT

4.1.2.2.2 Goods Receiving from DC/Store(DC-Store)

Reference Business Flow: [Inventory] 3.3 Good receiving from DC/Store

content:
The following are the integration points for this process:

DMALL to retrieve DN and related info from SAP

DMALL to send Goods Receipt information to SAP

ZRFC_BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE

content:

The following are the integration points for this process:

Under Served/Wrong Served

Over Served

Damaged

Flow:

Background: If it is found that the article quantity obtained is less than the quantity of goods in the documentation when receiving the goods, this processing flow needs to be initiated.

Flow:

4.1.2.2.4 Return to Vendor(RTV) And Return to DC(RTW)

title: System Integration
content:
Reference Business Flow: [Inventory] 3.5 Transfer to vendor/warehouse.

The following are the integration points for this process:

DMALL to send Transferring Posting to SAP

DMALL to create return order and send SAP

DMALL to send Goods Issuance information to SAP

a. DMALL to send Transferring Posting to SAP

Process Flow:

Background: Before the approval process starts, goods should be moved from Selling Storage Location to BO Storage Location first; And when the approval process is rejected, a reversal change of Storage Location should be invoked to SAP to move from BO Storage Location to Selling Storage Location.

Integration Approach: JCo API

Frequency & Rules: Real Time, Upon posting of Production result in DMALL

Integration Detail Content: Refer to (SM)_(Phase 1A)_(System Integration Blueprint)_(Store Operations Mapping)_ver1.0, worksheet named `RTV-TP to Sloc 0003`, `RTV-Disapproved RPO`.

SAP Function Module:

ZRFC_RPOSTORE_TRANSFERPOSTING

b. DMALL to create return order and send SAP

Background: Once damaged items found, store users should scan article barcodes via PDT and generate RPO in DMALL system. Approval officer should approve the RPO in DMALL system based on current approval matrix.

title: System Integration
content:
Approval officers do not need to do any approval in SAP anymore. SAP should directly generate RPO based on data provided by DMALL and send back the RPO number to DMALL. RPO in SAP is the real return purchase order. RPO in DMALL is like a draft or reference return purchase order. There is a corresponding relationship between RPO in DMALL and RPO in SAP.

Integration Approach: JCo API

Frequency & Rules:  Real Time; Upon posting of goods receipt in DMALL

Integration Detail Content:  Refer to  (SM)_(Phase 1A)_(System Integration Blueprint)_(Store Operations Mapping)_ver1.0, worksheet named `RTV-Create Ret PO`.

SAP Function Module:

ZRFC_RPOSTORE_RPOCREATE

c. DMALL to send Goods Issuance information to SAP

Background: After the store issues the goods, synchronize the result data to SAP.

Integration Approach: JCo API

Frequency & Rules:  Real Time; Upon posting of goods receipt in DMALL

Integration Detail Content:  Refer to  (SM)_(Phase 1A)_(System Integration Blueprint)_(Store Operations Mapping)_ver1.0, worksheet named ` RTV-Create Ret PO-GR`).

SAP Function Module:

ZRFC_RPOSTORE_GOODSRECEIPT

Return to Vendor(RTV) : When return to vendor, DMALL system need to send return data to Vendor Portal, for detail please check chapter below: 4.1.2.2.13 GR/GI for Consignment without Inventory.

4.1.2.2.5 Store-to-Store(IBT)

Reference Business Flow: [Inventory] 3.6 Store transfer to store

For Store to store there are 2 options:

Store transfer to store directly

Store transfer to store via DC(The prerequisite is the transfer articles must be listed in DC)

Option 1: Store transfer to store directly

The following are the integration points for this process:

[Issuing Site] DMALL to retrieve IBT STO Order document information from SAP

[Issuing Site] DMALL to check and get IBT SAFETYSTOCK from SAP

[Issuing Site] DMALL to post DELIVERY NOTE document to SAP

[Issuing Site] DMALL to validate posted DELIVERY NOTE against SAP

title: System Integration
content:
[Issuing Site] DMALL to post PICKEDQTY by DN item and then confirm the GOODS ISSUE to SAP

[Receiving Site] DMALL to retrieve IBT DN document information from SAP

[Receiving Site] DMALL to post IBT GR document information to SAP

a. [Issuing Site] DMALL to retrieve IBT STO Order document information from SAP

Background: IBT STO is created in SAP at NHO, DMALL need to pull the open STOs from SAP.

Integration Approach: JCo API

Frequency & Rules:  Manual request

Store can perform ad hoc request to download selected STOs

Integration Detail Content: Refer to (SM)_(Phase 1A)_(System Integration Blueprint)_(Store Operations Mapping)_ver1.0 worksheet named `IBST-Issuing Store`.

SAP Function Module:

ZRFC_BSTSTORE_STODOWNLOAD

b. [Issuing Site] DMALL to check and get IBT SAFETYSTOCK from SAP

Background: DMALL to check SAFETYSTOCK item by item, to get the allowed transfer-out item amount.

Integration Approach: JCo API

Frequency & Rules: Automatically triggered after getting STO.

Integration Detail Content: Refer to (SM)_(Phase 1A)_(System Integration Blueprint)_(Store Operations Mapping)_ver1.0 worksheet named `IBST-Issuing Store`.

title: System Integration
content:
SAP Function Module:

ZRFC_BSTSTORE_SAFETYSTOCK

c. [Issuing Site] DMALL to post DELIVERY NOTE document to SAP

Background: After get the allowed transfer-out amt of items, DMALL will generate the DN according to the amt then post to SAP, any Items that with creation errors should be repost as new reference number.

Integration Approach: JCo API

Frequency & Rules:  Automatically triggered after SAFETYSTOCK checking.

Integration Detail Content: Refer to (SM)_(Phase 1A)_(System Integration Blueprint)_(Store Operations Mapping)_ver1.0, worksheet named `IBST-Issuing Store`..

SAP Function Module:

ZRFC_BSTSTORE_DELIVERYNOTE

d. [Issuing Site] DMALL to validate posted DELIVERY NOTE against SAP

Background: This call is to validate the DELIVERY NOTE created by SAP, to double check the created DN is the same as SAP.

Integration Approach: JCo API

Frequency & Rules: Automatically triggered after DN creation.

Integration Detail Content: Refer to  (SM)_(Phase 1A)_(System Integration Blueprint)_(Store Operations Mapping)_ver1.0,worksheet named `IBST-Issuing Store`.

SAP Function Module:

ZRFC_BSTSTORE_DNDOWNLOAD_ISS

e. [Issuing Site] DMALL to post PICKEDQTY by DN item and then confirm the GOODS ISSUE to SAP

Background: DMALL to post the PICKEDQTY of every item for a given DN, then call the FM of GOODSISSUE to end/confirm the GI process of the Issuing site.

Integration Approach: JCo API

4.1.2.3 EOD Detail Integration Design

Integration Process Flow:

Red lines are the new data links provided by DMALL OS that will be sent to target system directly. Grey dotted lines are EOD backup links when exceptions occur and verification from SM, Blue lines are old datalinks.

Integration Detail Design:

title: System Integration
content:
Integration Content | SM Legacy System | Description | Integration Approach | Frequency & Rules
Sales & Tender | SAP | Sales & Tender data includes sales data in article dimensions and aggregated sales payment data. These will be integrated from DMALL OS to SAP of SM daily | SFTP | Daily
DAC | SMAC | DAC data is the membership system of SM Group. The DAC (aka SMAC) data will be integrated from DMALL OS to SMAC of SM daily | SFTP | Daily
Credit Info | Credit Info System | Credit info data is the tender dimension aggregated data of sales. It will be integrated from DMALL OS to Credit Info System of SM monthly | SFTP | Monthly (Every first day of the month)
T1/Japan Home Consales | Affiliate MMS | T1/Japan Home Consales include two types of sales data in T1 and Japan Home affiliated business. These will be integrated from DMALL OS to Affiliate MMS daily | SFTP | Daily Ref Only: In AS-IS Daily (5:00 pm) - 9pm 2nd run.
E-Treasury | E-Treasury data is the sales per cashier. It will be integrated from DMALL OS to E-Treasury of SM daily | SFTP | Daily
EIS | BIR | EIS data that needs to be sent to BIR(Bureau of Internal Revenue) of Philippine goverment. For more information please check https://www.bir.gov.ph | SFTP to SM Then SM to call BIR | Hourly
ESALES | BIR | ESales will be replaced by EIS JSON files (one file for each valid sales and sales adjustments void and return transaction) for sending to government (BIR) near real time, DMALL will generate for compatible and when EIS is not officially used by SM and BIR. | SFTP to SM Then SM to send to BIR | Monthly
Mall Sales (Ayala, Araneta, Megaworld, Festival) | Mall Sales | Mall sales include sales data offered to different malls such as Ayala, Araneta, Megaworld, Festival and so on. | SM EOD Program (HQ) | Daily/Hourly

title: System Integration
content:
ARC | ARC data is the report of SM sales data, without other affiliates sales, will be replaced by the Dashboard of DMALL OS, so the old EOD Program can be run at HQ until all store cut-over is done | SM EOD Program (HQ) | Daily
Marketing/Operations-Requested Ad Hoc Reports | Ad Hoc Reports | Ad hoc Reports will be replaced by the Promotion of DMALL OS, so the old EOD Program can be run at HQ until all store cut-over is done | SM EOD Program (HQ) | Daily

4.1.2.3.6 EIS

Integration Process Flow:

Background: EIS (Eletronic Invoice System) is the data that needs to be sent to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) of Philippine government in JSON format. One file for each valid sales and sales adjustments (such as void and return transaction) for sending to government (BIR) in near real time. The estimated order amount is 6-8K Orders per store per day.  For more information please check https://www.bir.gov.ph.

Integration Approach:  SFTP

File naming Format(If SFTP):

Sales Invoice | Sales Transaction header Grain: Date, Time, Store, Till, Invoice Number, Invoice Type,Customer, Employee | Customer Analytics Loss Prevention Operations Analytics
Sales Invoice Level Discounts | Discounts & Promotions information at Invoice Header Level Grain: Date, Time, Store, Till, Invoice Number, Invoice Type,Customer, Employee, Discount Reason , Reason Type | Loss Preventions Operation Analytics
Sales Tender Details | Tender details for the Transaction Grain: Date, Time, Store, Till, Invoice Number, Invoice Type,Customer, Employee, Tender Type | Operations Analytics Loss Prevention
Sales Lines | Sales transaction Lines Grain: Date, Time, store,Till,Invoice,Invoice Type, Line Number,SKU | Merchandise Analytics Sales Analytics Customer Analytics
Sales Line Level Discounts | Discounts & Promotions information at Sales Line Level Grain: Date, Time, store,Till,Invoice,Invoice Type, Line Number,SKU,Discount Type / Promotion id, Discount Reasons | Operation Analytics

Integration Approach:  SFTP（to ARC via NHO FTP server）

File naming Format(If SFTP)：

title: Data Dashboard
content:
Data Dashboard
1. Log In
Operation steps:

Type in account and password, then type the verification code

Data Dashboard
1. Menu
Operation steps:

Click the Data Dashboard Menu Button and view all reports / functions authorized

Data Dashboard
2. View – Basic filters & Query
Operation steps:

Choose the report according to user’s analysis purpose

Input the filters based on user’s data access (time, store & category etc.)

Click [Query] button to generate the data in report

Click this button will go the column customization  (next page)

Data Dashboard
2. View – Column Customization
Operation steps:

To choose show/hide columns by clicking the checkbox

To choose fixed dimension column or not

To re-order the columns by dragging button

Pre-design KPI grouping according to Dmall retail experience

2. View – Report data analysis
Operation steps:

Can drill down to deeper analysis ways to view the report by click the blue color data

Can rank the report data (increase/decrease) by click the button

Data Dictionary will provide the measure’s detail explanation for current report

Operation steps:

Can export the current report page via xls. Or xlsx. Format

Export File will include the filter information (Time, Store, Article, LFL etc.)

Operation steps:

Export File will include the filter information (Time, Store, Article, LFL etc.)

title: Data Dashboard

User need to [create] a view under the selected report

User select the corresponding dimension & measures for table and line chartFor exampleSelect [Site Name] / [Site Code] / [Sales TP] / [Sales Qty TP]

content:

User can view the created view in the beginning page, and can do he operation below:View – see the report view detailEdit – change the view detailAuthorize – authorize the view to corresponding roleDelete – Delete the viewAfter Creation, we should do the [Authorize] Step

     c. MBU – Month + Store + Sub- division

7. Merchandising Label Management – Label View
Operation steps:

Merchandising Label source:a. Preset (Pre-designed label which cannot be edit manually)b. Manual (created by user)

User can have a whole view of all labels (even created by others) Also can filter out the label by the name or status

Operation steps:

Can use label filter to type and find the label article list immediately

Article in some preset labels (top_N, SC/PWD etc.) are integrated from SAP which means can not be edited. Users only have the view authority.

Operation steps:

Article in other preset labels (Hardline MBU etc.) are manually maintained by users which can be changed from Dmall label system directly

Operation steps:

Click the [Create] button to start manual label set up
Fill up the information a. Label Nameb. Label Code (can auto generate)c. Entity Level (by article or by site-article)d. Valid period button (limit the article validation in this label or not)e. Description

Operation steps:

Find the create label in view page, it will display the created information.

Next step is to bind the article for this label, click [Article Bind] button.Also user can edit or disable this label according to the business requirement.

title: Receiving detail information Instruction:
content:
Receiving detail information Instruction:

Receive Qty must be equal to or less than Plan Qty.
If Receive Qty is equal to Plan Qty, discrepancy type could be No differ, Over served, Damaged or Expiry.
If Receive Qty is less than Plan Qty, discrepancy type could be Underserved or Wrong served.
If discrepancy type is Over served, Damaged or Expiry, discrepancy qty should be input by users.
If discrepancy type is Underserved or Wrong served, discrepancy qty will be auto calculated and shown.
If discrepancy type is No differ, discrepancy qty is default as 0.
Profit center must be input if discrepancy type is underserved, wrong served or Damaged.

Operation steps:
Basic Info:
Plan status:
Plan name: fill in the name relate to acutal situation
Plan Type: 1）Regular inventory  2）Periodic inventory（Temporarily not supported）-- Default is Regular Plan

Other Info:
Storage Location:Select the storage location that needs to be counted
Stocktake Order Generation Rules：1)Site (annual count)   2)Site & MBU/non_MBU(cycle count)
Stocktake Method:can choose Store Stocktake or 3rd-party Stocktake

title: . Sales Process
content:
Steps/Explanation
Prior to initiating a suspend order, it is imperative for the cashier to include a minimum of one item in the shopping cart. 
Clicking the "Suspend Order" button located in the function area.
Once suspended an order, there will be a suspend ticket printed out, which should be given to the customer for later order retrieval.
3. Sales Process
3. Suspend/Recall Order - Suspend

Steps/Explanation
When you suspend an order, the "Recall Order" button will display a red dot with the numeral 1, indicating the presence of a single suspended order awaiting recall. (This number may progressively rise with additional suspended orders).
3. Sales Process
3. Suspend/Recall Order - Suspend

Steps/Explanation
To recall an order, select the "Recall Order" button from the function area.
If there is solely one suspended order, clicking the recall button will promptly retrieve this order without any additional steps. 
If there are more than one suspended orders, a list of suspended orders. Cashier can scan the QR code on the suspend ticket or identify the desired order for recall and click the "Recall Order" button next to the target order to proceed with the retrieval. 
(Please note that the membership status will not be remained upon recalling orders, to prevent misuse of membership privileges/assets.)
3. Sales Process
3. Suspend/Recall Order - Recall

Steps/Explanation
Selecting the item line intended for removal (which will be distinguished by a highlighted blue background).
Clicking the "Delete Item" button from the function area.
A double confirmation prompt will appear, and upon clicking "Confirm," the selected item will be deleted.
(Conducting delete item may require supervisor authorization.)
3. Sales Process
4. Delete Item

Steps/Explanation
The cashier will then be prompted to select a reason for the deletion.

title: . Payment
content:
4. Payment
1. Payment Interface
Steps/Explanation
Transaction basic info: Display amount payable and receipt no.; Click the [↓] to expand the details including the discount info
Article info: Display the article list with the names, qty and subtotal
Payment amount: Display the ‘To Be paid’ and ‘Paid’ amount(payment complete when To Be paid=0)
Tender list: Display the supported tenders; Click [Tender] button to process the payment using selected tender; Most used 6 tenders will be configured to display at the top of the list; Click [All] to view all tenders
Amount input box: Can input the payment amount when customer want to use a tender to pay partially(if pay ‘to be paid’ amount, no need to input)

4. Payment
1. Payment Interface
Steps/Explanation
Quick-pay denomination: Display the configured 4 most used cash denominations, click the button to pay by CASH of corresponding amount
Keyboard: Used to input the payment amount into the ‘Amount input box’; Click [Cash] to pay by cash(the same with clicking the cash tender button)
Payment record list: Display the records of the completed payment; Click [X] to cancel payment and the ‘to be paid’ amount will be revise
[Back to Cart]: Back to sales page remaining all transaction info including the articles, membership, govnt deduct…; Payment record will be cancelled automatically
[Cancel Order]: Cancel the whole transaction and back to sales page Payment record will be cancelled automatically

4. Payment
2. Pay by Cash
Steps/Explanation
Can input the payment amount when customer want to pay partially(if pay the whole transaction amount, no need to input)
Click [Cash] in the tender list or keyboard and the payment  completes
Can click the quick denomination button to pay by CASH of corresponding amount
Remark
Can pay exceed ‘to be paid’ amount by cash as a prompt will pop-up after transaction complete to remind cashier the change amount

title: . EOD & Others
content:
139
6. EOD & Others
6. Setting
Steps/Explanation
Network Mode: If set to offline mode, all POS transaction will be based on offline service and local DB. (used when the store network is unstable or disconnected temporary)
Program pin: If set to pin, the POS program will always on the top of the windows desktop
Hardware acceleration: If set to enable, POS will use GPU to make pages load faster
IP address: Display the IP address of the POS device
Unsync task: Check whether there is any local trans data that haven’t be synced to cloud.
Offline Service Mgt: Display the version of offline service and the version & update time of each offline service module (used to check whether POS fetched the latest version of offline data)

title: .In store pos-server

If store network is OK, the store everyday reconciliation will be successful. If it is failed, normally it is because some of the POS is disconnected to the cloud, you can import trans data from ISP to cloud and then do manual reconciliation success.

title: Master Data
content:
Operation steps:

1.Click[Export] to export all brand list information.
2. Input brand name and click[Query] to check the brand information, Click[Reset] to remove the  the filtered content.
Article master data-Brand Mgt

Article master data-Article Master Data Mgt
Operation steps:

1.Click[Export] to export all article list information.
2. Click[Add article image] to upload picture per article which will be applied to print price tag.
3.Click[Batch Edit] to edit multiple article information.
4. Chose data in the condition filter and click[Query] to check data, Click[Reset] to remove the filtered content.
5. Click[Edit] to edit single article data.
6.Click[View] to review single article data details.

Article Master Data Mgt-Add article Images
Operation steps:

1.Choose image type , it’s a single choice.
2.Click[Choose] to open the local file and choose picture to upload.
3.Click[Cancel]to stop the upload and will close the popup window.
4.Click[Ok] to confirm the upload behavior.

Article Master Data Mgt-Batch Edit
Operation steps:

1.Choose field which can be modified in article master data module（ Only field which are maintained in the system can be modified).
2.Click[Choose] to download the chosen article file and upload modified data.
3.Click[Cancel]to stop the upload and will close the popup window.

title: Article master data-Store Article Mgt
content:
Article master data-Store Article Mgt
Operation steps:

1.Click[Export] to export all store article list information.
2. Click[Import] to upload store article list to manually trigger article master data and store article master data integration.
3. Chose data in the condition filter and click[Query] to check data, Click[Reset] to remove the filtered content.
4. Input keyword to find store.

Article master data- BOM Article Mgt
Operation steps:

1.Click[Create] to create SM eat BOM relationship.
2.Chose data in the condition filter and click[Query] to check data, Click[Reset] to remove the filtered content.
3.Click[Abolish]to stop using the BOM relationship.
4.Click single voucher highlighted with blue to check BOM details.

Article master data- TP BOM Article config
Operation steps:

Find the base unit configuration that needs to be converted (the dummy unit after conversion).
Click "Create" and fill in or import the pre-conversion unit and the conversion relationship between units.
Click "Edit" to modify existing units and conversion relationships.
You can export the configuration.
After modifying or creating a new configuration, be sure to click "Save" and wait for 5 minutes for it to take effect.

BOM Article Mgt-Create SM Eat article
Operation steps:

1.Input BOM name.
2.Chose article to be the parent article.
3.Input mark up ratio %, default to be 0.
4.Click + to add article.
5. Choose corresponding article group.
6. Input quantity.
7.Click[Save] to save the setting.

title: Article Group Mgt
content:
Article Group Mgt
Operation steps:
1.Click[Add group] to create new article group.
2.Input group name to check information. Click[Query] to check data, Click[Reset] to remove the filtered content.
3.Click [Edit] to edit article group information.
4.Click [Delete] to delete article group.
5. Click[Add article] to add article in the article group.
6. Choose articles and click[Delete] to delete articles in the article group.
7. Click[Query] to check data, Click[Reset] to remove the filtered content.

Article master data-Scale Code Mgt
Operation steps:

1.Click[Sync to the Scale] to apply the scale code information.
2. Chose data in the condition filter and click[Query] to check data, Click[Reset] to remove the filtered content.

Article master data-Category Mgt
Operation steps:

1.Click[Export] to export all category list information.
2. Input keyword to find category.

Article master data-Retail Price Query Mgt
Operation steps:

1.Click[Import] to upload store article list that change the sell price( PLU by request).
2.Click[Export] export site articles list with current sell price.
3.Chose data in the condition filter and click[Query] to check data, Click[Reset] to remove the filtered content.

APP-Article Mgt
Operation steps:

1.Click[Article Mgt] ，user can scan the article barcode and check article information.

APP-Article Mgt-Article Query
Operation steps:

1. user can scan the article barcode and check article information.

1.Click [Import] to download site creation template and upload filled file.
2.Click[Export] to export all site master data.
3. Click[Batch Edit] to edit site information.
4.Chose data in the condition filter and click[Query] to check data, Click[Reset] to remove the filtered content.
5.Click [View] to check single site information.
6. Click[Edit] to edit site information.
7. Click[Create] to add new site.

Site Mgt-Create site master data
Operation steps:

1. Fill in mandatory and optional required field in the site module.
2.Click[Save] to save the filled in data.

 6.Click[Edit] to edit site hierarchy information.
7.Click[Delete] to delete the site hierarchy.

title: Account Mgt
content:
Account Mgt
Operation steps:

1. Click [Import] to download new account creation and role authorization template, then upload the filled file（only for new account).
2.Click[Export] to export all account list.
3. Chose data in the condition filter and click[Query] to check data, Click[Reset] to remove the filtered content.
4. Click[Reset password] to resend the new password to the account.
5.Click[Show] to check single account information.
6.Click[Edit] to modify the single account data. Including: name、email address、original site、phone number.
 7.Click[Authorize] to authorize the account to different modules and can operate bulk authorization.
8.Click[Create] to add new account.

Account Mgt-Create account
Operation steps:

1.Choose available terminal, multiple choices.
2.  Fill in account name ， email phone number and original site.Fill in account expiration date if account expiration is Yes.
3. Click[Cancel] to stop the action and close the popup window.
4.Click[Save] to save the filled in data.

Account Mgt-Bulk Authorization(step1)
Operation steps:



When you want to copy the role and data permissions of the current account, etc，to other accounts。Select the current account and then click“Authorize”.

Account Mgt-Bulk Authorization(step2)
Operation steps:
Click“Bulk Authorization”

title: Account Mgt-Bulk Authorization(step3)
content:
Account Mgt-Bulk Authorization(step3)
Operation steps:

Choose roles  
From List: Choose accounts from the list, can select multiple accounts.
3.From files: Upload files with multiple accounts 
4. Click Cancel to stop the bulk authorization
5. Click [Save] , then the chosen accounts  will be authorized the same roles of the selected account.

title: Role Mgt
content:
Role Mgt
Operation steps:

1. Chose data in the condition filter and click[Query] to check data, Click[Reset] to remove the filtered content. 
2.  Click[Edit] to modify single role data.
3. Click[Create] to add new role.

Role Mgt-Create role
Operation steps:

Fill in role name, applied terminal and original site(HQ/Store/DC). 
Attention: “terminal”and”original site "only can edited when created Role.
2.  Remove or add the authorize function and authorize account
3. Click[Cancel] to stop the creation.
4. Click[Save] to save the filled in data.
